items.jobDescription:
About the Company
At LeadSquared, we are building a global SaaS leader in sales execution and marketing automation. Our platform powers high-velocity sales teams across industries like EdTech, BFSI, Healthcare, and more, helping businesses drive revenue growth with speed and efficiency.
With a strong presence across India, APAC, and the US, we are on an ambitious journey to scale globally and this role sits at the heart of that growth.

What You’ll Own

1. CEO Office & Strategic Execution
Convert CEO priorities into clear execution roadmaps with owners and timelines
Drive weekly leadership reviews, MBRs, QBRs
Track company-level OKRs, KPIs, and strategic initiatives
Ensure closure and accountability across leadership team
Act as a thought partner to CEO on key business decisions
2. Revenue & GTM Excellence
Work closely with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success leaders
Track and improve pipeline coverage, conversion rates, and sales velocity
Identify revenue leakages and inefficiencies
Support GTM experiments (pricing, segmentation, partnerships)
Step into critical deals or escalations when needed
3. Cross-functional Execution & Special Projects
Lead high-impact, cross-functional initiatives (market entry, product launches, org restructuring)
Break silos and drive alignment across Product, GTM, and Ops
Own execution for 0→1 and 1→10 initiatives
4. Business Analytics & Decision Support
Build CEO dashboards across SaaS metrics (ARR, CAC, LTV, NRR, churn)
Translate data into insights and action
Apply first-principles thinking
5. Operating Cadence & Org Efficiency
Run weekly exec reviews, MBRs, and quarterly planning
Improve execution discipline and decision velocity
Identify and remove bottlenecks
6. CEO Leverage & Firefighting
Act as an extension of the CEO
Own high-priority, ambiguous problems
Step into and fix critical issues

Why This Role is Exciting
Work directly with the CEO and leadership team on high-impact, company-wide initiatives
Get a front-row seat to scaling a global B2B SaaS business
Own strategic programs across revenue, product, and operations
Operate in a high-ownership, high-visibility environment
Accelerated learning and career growth with exposure to leadership decision-making
Show more Show less

items.jobDescription:
About Us 
At Coverfox Group, we’re not just changing how insurance is bought - we’re redefining how protection is built, delivered, and experienced across every corner of India. 
Our mission is bold yet deeply rooted: to make insurance simple, affordable, and truly accessible - not just in cities, but in the most remote parts of Bharat. With innovation at our core, we launched India’s first digital insurance brokerage, pioneered the PoSP (Point of Sales Person) model, and enabled some of India’s largest consumer brands to embed insurance effortlessly into their ecosystems. But this is just the beginning. 

Our Mission: Protection for All, Everywhere 

More than 100 crore Indians still remain uninsured or underinsured. We’re here to change that. Our ambition is to safeguard 100 million lives by 2030 - and we’re doing it by building for the entire micro-insurance value chain. This positions us at the heart of the customer experience, empowering us to create tailored, affordable solutions that reflect the real risks people live with. 

For too long, insurance in India has been sold as a ‘product.’ At Coverfox, we see insurance as a solution - a tool for protection, stability, and progress. Especially in underserved markets beyond Tier I cities, our tech-enabled, solution-driven approach is reshaping how people access and adopt insurance that truly fits their lives. 

Our Journey: Built on Innovation, Driven by Purpose 

From breakthrough digital platforms to deep partnerships with MFIs, NBFCs, Agri-techs, and healthcare providers, our story is one of continuous innovation and real-world impact. Our business model’s ability to integrate seamlessly across the ecosystem - from manufacturers and reinsurers to distribution and end-customers - is a powerful differentiator. 

We work deeply with MFIs, NBFCs, agri-techs, and health partners to embed insurance into their ecosystems, and are also taking the initial steps to distribute small ticket size retail insurance products to their customers. 

Beyond insurance brokerage, Coverfox also operates Coverstack, a technology platform that enables non-insurance businesses to seamlessly cross-sell or up-sell insurance products. In the past year alone, we’ve touched over 2.5 million lives. And we’re scaling rapidly. 

Behind this journey is a founding team with a rare mix of domain expertise across financial services, technology, and capital markets - brought together not just by skill, but by long-standing trust and shared purpose. Together, we're building something meaningful - and we’re just getting started. 

Why Coverfox 
Join Coverfox if you want to: 
• Solve meaningful problems at national scale. 
• Build from the ground up with speed, ownership, and creativity. 
• Work collaboratively with brilliant minds who believe in real change. 
• Leave a mark that matters for generations. The future of insurance isn’t being built somewhere else. 

It’s happening here. It’s happening now. 
And you can be part of it. 

About the Role 
• Work very closely with the founder on all strategic initiates 
• Communicate the business strategy within the organization and ensure that the departments are working towards achieving the common objective by collaborating with them 
• Become SPOC for investor relationship management and work on investor presentations and story lining with the founder for the Board Meetings 
• Work on business models and financial models for different phases of the business 
• Define and maintain the Objectives and Key Results (OKRs) of the Company along with the founder and work with the various departments to ensure the OKRs are clearly defined and communicated at a department level 
• Track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) of the various business functions and working with the respective teams to set of dashboards 
• Collaborate with various teams to identify the various business problems and work with the respective teams to strategize and gameplan for it 
• Work with the founder on initiates such as organization structuring, manpower planning, budget planning, business planning, growth planning 
• Oversee daily operations in collaboration with business function leads 
• Develop and build on relationships with all employees for increased efficiency and responsiveness of existing operations, and help define new operational strategies by working with the founder and other executives on special projects and need base initiatives 

What we are looking for? 
As we scale, we are looking to hire a seasoned, horizontal thinker to join the Founder’s Office and work directly with the Founder on strategic, high-impact initiatives that cut across business, operations, growth, and corporate teams. 
This is a role for someone who brings depth of experience yet remains hands-on and hungry to build. You will work on some of the most mission-critical problems in the company and shape Coverfox’s next phase of growth. 
• Must be from top tier college, great if from Consulting background 
• Prior experience of 4+ years of experience from Consulting, Investment Banking, Startup backgrounds 
• Thrive in fast-paced environments and enjoy solving hard, undefined problems 
• Are ready to join immediately and create momentum from Day 1 
• Strong analytical skills - should be able to come up with detailed and actionable insights 
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills 
• A Critical thinker - should be able to come up with ideas/ help in ideation 
• A Problem solver - should be able to come up with potential solution/ view of internal and external queries 
• Experience working with or supporting leadership in high-growth environments 
• Comfortable in ambiguity, and equally comfortable rolling up sleeves 
• Someone who can zoom out to shape strategy and zoom in to fix a process 

What You’ll Love at Coverfox? 
➢ Comprehensive Health & Wellness Support 
Enjoy best-in-class group medical insurance for you and your family, along with personal accident coverage to ensure financial protection during unforeseen events. We offer both cashless and reimbursement options through a wide network of hospitals. 
➢ High-Impact Work Meets High Ownership Culture 
Work in a setup that rewards initiative, encourages experimentation, and recognizes outcomes. We believe in giving our leaders the autonomy to build and the support to scale. 
➢ Flexibility, Freedom & Growth 
Flexible work policies, an inclusive environment, and a culture that promotes collaboration ensure you can do your best work on your terms. Whether you’re solving complex problems or launching new initiatives, Coverfox gives you the room to grow. 
➢ Perks That Power You 
From tax-saving meal benefits to wellness programs and milestone celebrations, we’ve created an ecosystem that takes care of the little things so you can focus on the big impact.
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: Manager, Chief of Staff – GAOC Site Lead
items.jobDescription:
Company:

Qualcomm India Private Limited

Job Area:

Finance & Accounting Group, Finance & Accounting Group > Global Accounting Ops Center

General Summary:

Manager, Chief of Staff – GAOC Site Lead 

Job Location: Hyderabad

Job Overview:

Qualcomm Global Accounting Operations team is looking for a high-impact Chief of Staff to join the Office of the GAOC Site Lead. This role is the Site Leads strategic and operational partner owning the site leadership operating system, driving alignment across priorities, and ensuring decisions translate into crisp execution. The Chief of Staff serves as an extension of the Site Lead: shaping narratives for senior leaders, facilitating key decisions, proactively identifying risks and trade-offs, and leading cross-functional initiatives that increase organizational effectiveness across GAOC.

This Role Is Based In India And Partners With GAOC Leaders Across Geographies And Functions. The Chief Of Staff Runs The Sites Executive Cadence (planning, Governance, Communications, And Follow-through), Amplifies The Site Leads Impact Through High-quality Decision Support, And Leads Select GAOC-wide Initiatives. The Role Requires Strong Judgment, Discretion, And The Ability To Operate Effectively In Ambiguity. Key Expectations Include:

Strategy, Prioritization & Decision Support

Partner with the Site Lead to define and maintain an integrated view of site priorities; translate objectives into a clear roadmap with success metrics and an executable operating plan.
Drive decision-making: frame problems, surface trade-offs, synthesize inputs, and bring forward options and recommendations; ensure decisions are documented and communicated.
Produce executive-ready narratives for leadership forums: briefs, talking points, memos, and pre-reads that clarify context, decisions needed, and implications.
Own the leadership insight pipeline: maintain a small set of meaningful metrics/dashboards, identify patterns, and proactively flag execution bottlenecks, organizational risks, and opportunities for simplification and scale.
Serve as a trusted advisor and escalation path for complex cross-team issues; facilitate alignment, resolve conflicts, and drive timely closure on high-impact open items.
Build and maintain a stakeholder map across GAOC and partner organizations; ensure the right leaders are engaged at the right time and that messaging and expectations stay aligned.
Identify, scope, and lead high-priority initiatives on behalf of the Site Lead (operating model, process simplification, automation, engagement) from concept through measurable impact.
Represent the Site Lead in selected meetings/forums as delegated; capture outcomes, negotiate next steps, and ensure follow-through on commitments.


Executive Operating Cadence & Execution Discipline

Establish and run the site leadership cadence (staff meetings, leadership reviews, business rhythms), including agenda planning, pre-reads, facilitation support, notes, action/decision logs, and closure tracking.
Drive end-to-end execution across site priorities and cross-functional programs: define workplans, milestones, dependencies, and ownership; remove friction and ensure predictable delivery.
Run program governance for top initiatives like AI in Finance : maintain risk/issue registers, dependency tracking, decision records, and escalation mechanisms; unblock execution through stakeholder alignment.
Ensure progress on the projects and periodic review with stakeholders
Eliminate or escalate any bottlenecks potentially impacting timelines
Follow the Agile methodology
Plan and Participate in trainings to encourage Self Service projects
Contribute to the preparation of training plans and topics
Regular and frequent updates to global stakeholders on progress
Manage communication and updates to leadership around all the initiatives in GAOC
Lead select identified group of initiatives as Project manager for the AI initiatives for GAOC

Executive Communications & Stakeholder Management

Own the communications rhythm for the Site Lead and GAOC site: draft announcements, updates, FAQs, and narratives that improve clarity, alignment, and adoption.
Produce leadership-ready updates (weekly/monthly, MBR/QBR materials): progress vs. priorities, key insights, risks/issues, decisions needed, and stakeholder asks.
Manage stakeholder expectations across geographies and functions; ensure timely escalations, crisp alignment on ownership, and fast resolution of misalignment.


Organizational Planning, Talent Enablement & Site Effectiveness

Support strategic planning cycles (annual/quarterly planning) by driving timelines, consolidating inputs, validating assumptions, and synthesizing trade-offs for leadership decisions.
Partner with Finance/HR and functional leaders on headcount and budget planning (where applicable): track allocations, surface gaps, and support prioritization and resource trade-offs.
Establish and continuously improve onboarding and role-readiness mechanisms for the site (as applicable), partnering with HR/People Ops and functional leaders.
Ideate, implement and monitor the training and hiring programs for the site lead and propose and make changes to help upskill the team to the next level.


Additional Responsibilities

Create and maintain a central repository of site/GAOC processes, playbooks, onboarding materials, and recurring updates to reduce single points of failure and improve scale.
Drive execution discipline across teams by establishing simple standards for planning, reporting, and action tracking; coach partners on using these mechanisms effectively.
Lead cross-functional forums/working groups for site and GAOC-wide initiatives; ensure clarity on ownership, timelines, interlocks, and decisions.
Manage content hygiene for internal channels/portals as applicable (news, announcements, FAQs, calendars) so information is current, accurate, and easy to find.
For major rollouts, coordinate readiness and adoption (stakeholder alignment, communications, enablement logistics, feedback loops) to ensure smooth implementation and measurable outcomes.


Minimum Qualifications

12 - 16 years of experience in Chief of Staff/Business Operations, Strategy & Operations, Program Management, Consulting, or equivalent roles in a global, matrixed environment.
Proven track record driving complex, cross-functional priorities end-to-end including operating cadence, governance, risk management, and measurable delivery.
Demonstrated ability to operate as a trusted advisor to senior leaders with strong judgment, discretion, and the ability to influence without authority.
Excellent executive communication and synthesis skills (written and verbal), including creating decision memos, briefs, and leadership-ready narratives.
Strong prioritization, organizational, and stakeholder management skills; ability to manage multiple workstreams, ambiguity, and tight timelines with high attention to detail.
Preferred Qualifications
Prior experience in an Office of a Leader / Chief of Staff role supporting a senior leader and/or leadership team.
Experience building executive operating cadences and governance (staff meetings, MBR/QBR, KPI dashboards, action/decision logs) and facilitating senior-level decision-making.
Strong change leadership capability driving alignment through communications, enablement, and stakeholder management across geographies.
Experience supporting planning and resource prioritization (headcount/budget inputs, portfolio trade-offs) and driving alignment on outcomes.
Experience working effectively with teams across the Americas/Europe and India (or similar multi-region operating models).
Experience designing and executing enablement mechanisms (onboarding, playbooks, role readiness) and managing communications to broad audiences.


Required:

Educational Requirements

Bachelors / Master’s in arts / Science / Engineering from a reputed College / University


Optional:

MBA
Project / Program Management Certification (PMP, PRINCE2, Agile, or equivalent)
Change Management Certification (Prosci or equivalent) – beneficial


Minimum Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree.
7+ years of Finance, Accounting, or related work experience.
Completed advanced degrees in a relevant field may be substituted for up to two years (Master’s = one year, Doctorate = two years) of work experience.


Applicants: Qualcomm is an equal opportunity employer. If you are an individual with a disability and need an accommodation during the application/hiring process, rest assured that Qualcomm is committed to providing an accessible process. You may e-mail disability-accomodations@qualcomm.com or call Qualcomm's toll-free number found here. Upon request, Qualcomm will provide reasonable accommodations to support individuals with disabilities to be able participate in the hiring process. Qualcomm is also committed to making our workplace accessible for individuals with disabilities. (Keep in mind that this email address is used to provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities. We will not respond here to requests for updates on applications or resume inquiries).

Qualcomm expects its employees to abide by all applicable policies and procedures, including but not limited to security and other requirements regarding protection of Company confidential information and other confidential and/or proprietary information, to the extent those requirements are permissible under applicable law.

To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Qualcomm. Staffing and recruiting agencies and individuals being represented by an agency are not authorized to use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes, and any such submissions will be considered unsolicited. Qualcomm does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Qualcomm employees or any other company location. Qualcomm is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

If you would like more information about this role, please contact Qualcomm Careers. 
Show more Show less

What We’re Looking For:

items.jobTitle: Associate / Sr. Associate - Chief of Staff (Product)
items.jobDescription:
What We Do And Who We Are

Join the dynamic team at Wholsum Foods and be part of an extraordinary movement! Our mission is simple: to change the world's eats. With brands like Slurrp Farm and Mille, we offer healthy and delicious alternatives to the junk food epidemic. It's time to rediscover the nutritious

traditions of our ancestors and embrace the power of millets like Ragi, Jowar, Bajra, and Foxtail Millet.

Led by founders - Shauravi Malik and Meghana Narayan, we are food enthusiasts, entrepreneurs, and mothers committed to making a difference. We're expanding globally and seeking talented individuals who thrive in a collaborative, innovative, and high-growth environment. Join us and embark on a rewarding journey where you can grow professionally while having a blast!

At Wholsum Foods, we celebrate diversity and unity, all driven by a shared mission to revolutionize the way we eat. Our team is our greatest asset, and our core values—Relationship First, Dependable, Passionate, and Growth Mindset—bring out the best in each team member. We take pride in being an equal opportunity workplace, embracing individuals regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, or gender identity. Together, we will redefine the future of food!

We are also Great Place to Work Certified.

WHAT WILL BE YOUR MISSION AT WHOLSUM FOODS?

You will work directly with the two co-founders in helping the company to achieve its strategic and operational goals, by delivering key internal and external projects that are critical to the launch of this new brand

You will be responsible for driving the rigour of market-based insights into actionable co-ordination between the various internal teams and stakeholders

You will have a deep understanding of the company business model, the underlying profitability and levers of different business verticals

OUTCOMES: WHAT ARE YOU GOING TO DO?

Internal and external key communications

Create and keep a dashboard of project plan that the co-founders need on a daily/weekly/monthly basis
Coordination between various internal teams and external stakeholders/3rd party agencies
Helping in end-to-end implementation of various operational and strategic levers

Strategic alliances and strategy implementation as per plan

This is code for - catch all the balls which the co-founders drop and you can possibly catch!
You have to be someone who thrives on having something new to do every few months, and prioritizes ruthlessly

Culture Carrier: strengthening and deepening the core company values

Be involved in selection and recruitment of A-player candidates, with an emphasis on cultural fit as we grow the team.

Business planning and Insights

Creating business models entailing our growth plans and the building blocks to achieve this growth
Researching industry trends, competitive landscape and providing insights to drive decision making

ROLE RELATED COMPETENCIES

Ambitious

You like the challenge of playing at a senior level, interacting with the C-Suite, Board, Investors, and senior market stakeholders

Humility

Humility to counter your ambition, this is a MUST

Presentation Skills

You have strong presentation and communication skills. You enjoy crafting world class presentations for senior audiences and anticipating questions in advance

You are always prepared with you own analysis and internal logic to defend your thoughts

Organisation/Planning 

Your ability to plan, be organised and be on top of schedules is exceptional. You are proactive, focus on key priorities

Critical thinking

You are a logical thinker and a naturally deep thinker - we will interview you for this

Proactivity

You understand your mission and outcomes, and act proactively. You do not need to be chased or told what to do; instead you bring your ideas to the company

Fairness

You have a strong bent of fairness, you don't play politics within an office and you fit in to a team where the culture is to enable everyone to be the very best version of themselves

CULTURE COMPETENCIES

Customer Obsessed

Take care of the customer, and everything else will take care of itself. We live to delight and everyone spends time understanding the customer, regardless of their role

100% Commitment

You take ownership of what you do and your word matters. You always hold yourself accountable to that which you commit to. Expect the same of your team and boss, in return

Lifelong Learner

You learn from others and grow fast; you continuously seek improvement, are always curious and have a bias for learning

Bias for Execution

You are detail oriented, solution focussed and outcome driven. You enjoy working in a tight knit and fast-moving environment

Agile

We value agility and not just hustle

Honest and Integrity

You do not cut corners ethically. You earn trust and maintain confidence. Do what is right and not just politically expedient. Speak plainly and truthfully

Teamwork

You are always prepared to address the problem, and work collaboratively to land on the right answer, not just your answer. There is no "I" in team. Your ego is under control

Other Questions

What will you think about (a lot of the time?)

Am I pushing the co-founders and the team to be better at what they do every day?

Am I exhibiting and living the values I want to see every day in the people around me, and the people I help recruit?

Am I excited to be at Wholsum Foods, because I see myself become the best version of me?

Why is this exciting to you?

You are interested to operationalize strategy in real world setting, and want to build a world class modern digital business with over 10 million customers

You are keenly interested in the digital space in India, and excited to learn how to scale D2C in the FMCG food segment

You are interested in learning how to build great workplaces within a company that is both growing and highly diverse

You are about people, you care about being invested in, as an individual and you believe in mentorship

We are laying the foundation of a culture of conscious capitalism and this is your chance to work somewhere that enables you to road-test a shared passion

After the CEO office role for 2-3 years you will either a) run a large department that you enjoyed working in at Wholsum Foods b) do this at another corporate or c) venture into the world of operating companies at a PE/VC fund

Where have you been?

You will have 3-5 years of prior experience in a top notch organization, or with a great boss whom you have learnt a lot from

You are someone whose teams will really miss you when you leave, but they will understand your reasons for doing so

Being from a consulting/banking/strategy and planning background helps as you can hit the ground running in terms of presentations, but this isn't a must

MBAs from top global schools are welcome to apply, but it is important to demonstrate on the ground experience of having worked in India

You are well versed with using excel for building basic business models and want to understand financials. You don't need to be an accountant, just comfortable with numbers

WHAT WILL YOU GET?

Fast paced growth in start-up FMCG space. 
Mentorship from senior people / board members / in house experts
Ability to grow and learn with the brand.
You will be a part of a hyper charged growth story
You would be part of the team in creating the best place to work in India

Location- Gurgaon

STARTING DATE : Immediately 
Show more Show less

items.jobDescription:
Pibit.ai is an SF-based insurtech company reconstructing the art and science of commercial underwriting for carriers and MGAs.

At the heart is the CURE™ platform, the industry’s only centralized underwriting risk
environment powered by agentic underwriting services that deliver decision ready outcomes.
It converts submissions into decisions by automating intake, triage, and data enrichment
from documents and external sources while surfacing risk insights that help customers win
the right accounts faster, scale throughput, and reduce loss ratios.

Backed by Y Combinator, our proprietary platform serves dozens of clients across the U.S.,
enabling 85% faster underwriting, a 32% increase in GWP per underwriter, and up to 700
basis points improvement in loss ratios.
For details, visit pibit.ai.

Role Overview
The Chief of Staff to the CEO is a high leverage, high visibility role designed to serve as the extended arm of the CEO across all critical company functions - AI,Technology, Product, Sales, Operations, Marketing

This is not a coordination only role. You will be a hands on problem solver, strategic thinker, cross functional orchestrator, and enabler of founder alignment. You will operate with the authority and context of the CEO, diving deep into business challenges, driving execution discipline, and ensuring organizational clarity and momentum

This is the first Chief of Staff role at Pibit.AI, making it a unique opportunity to shape the Founder's Office function from the ground up and become a critical architect of our operational excellence as we grow

Key Responsibilities
Act as the CEO’s problem solving arm on complex, multi team challenges.
Convert CEO priorities into task clarity, execution plans, and measurable outcomes.
Prepare the CEO for high leverage decisions with analysis, insights, briefs, and recommendations.
Represent the CEO in selected internal/external conversations when required.
Run leadership syncs, founder reviews, and company wide OKR check ins.
Build systems for tracking execution, accountability, risks, and blockers.
Design processes, cadences, and mechanisms that keep teams aligned, fast, and focused.
Own high stakes problems end to end (hands on).
Work with functional heads to clarify priorities and accelerate outcomes.
Drive alignment between Sales, Product, Tech, AI, Ops
Identify operational bottlenecks and design solutions that scale.
Ensure decisions, priorities, and blockers flow smoothly across geographies.
Drive asynchronous execution culture with clarity and speed.

iExperience & Background
5–8 years of professional experience in high-growth B2B startups, management consulting (Tier 1 firms), or strategic operations roles or Ex - founders
Proven track record of operating in fast-paced, ambiguous, 0-to-1 or scaling environments.
Direct experience working closely with founders or C-suite executives in a strategic capacity (Chief of Staff, Strategic Operations, Founder's Office, or similar).
Business acumen: Strong understanding of B2B SaaS business models.
Cross-functional fluency: Comfortable navigating and contributing across Sales, Product, Engineering, Operations, Marketing, and Finance.
Problem-solving mindset: Ability to take on complex, multi-dimensional problems, break them down, and drive to resolution without handholding.
Execution excellence: Track record of owning initiatives end-to-end, driving accountability, and delivering results on time
Insurance or InsurTech domain experience: Familiarity with commercial insurance, underwriting workflows, carriers, MGAs, or broker ecosystems is a strong advantage.
Show more Show less

This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Salary range: Rs 6000000 - Rs 7500000 (ie INR 60-75 LPA)

Min Experience: 8 years

Location: Mumbai

JobType: full-time

We are seeking a dynamic and strategic Chief of Staff to work closely with senior leadership in driving high-impact initiatives across corporate finance, digital transformation, and business growth. This role acts as a strategic partner and execution anchor, ensuring that organizational priorities are translated into actionable plans and delivered with speed and precision. You will operate at the center of decision-making, enabling leadership to focus on long-term vision while you drive alignment, execution, and performance tracking across key functions. Based in Mumbai, this is a high-visibility role suited for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments and is comfortable navigating ambiguity while delivering measurable business outcomes.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

Partner with leadership to define, prioritize, and execute strategic initiatives aligned with growth and business objectives
Drive corporate finance activities including financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis to support informed decision-making
Lead and manage cross-functional projects related to digital transformation, operational efficiency, and organizational scaling
Translate strategic goals into structured execution plans with clear timelines, milestones, and measurable KPIs
Build and maintain dashboards, MIS reports, and executive summaries to track progress and highlight risks or opportunities
Act as a central coordination point across departments, ensuring alignment, communication, and timely execution of key initiatives
Identify inefficiencies in processes and systems, and drive automation or transformation initiatives to improve productivity and outcomes
Support leadership in investor, board, and stakeholder communications through high-quality presentations and insights
Conduct financial and business analysis to evaluate new opportunities, investments, and growth strategies
Establish governance frameworks, processes, and best practices to support sustainable and scalable business growth

What Makes You a Great Fit

8+ years of experience in strategy, consulting, corporate finance, or business operations roles
Strong foundation in corporate finance, including financial modeling, analysis, and performance management
Proven experience in driving digital transformation or process improvement initiatives within organizations
Demonstrated ability to manage complex, cross-functional projects and deliver results in fast-paced environments
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence senior leaders and cross-functional teams
High ownership mindset with strong execution capabilities and attention to detail
Experience working in high-growth, consulting, or investor-driven environments is highly preferred
Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution, ensuring both long-term vision and immediate impact
Show more Show less

About Eloelo Group

Eloelo Group is one of India's fastest-growing consumer technology companies, building category-defining products at the intersection of content, technology, and culture. The Group's flagship products — Story TV in micro-drama and Master in micro-learning — have together placed two Eloelo apps in India's Top 10, reaching over 100 million Indians who consume short-form vertical content every day.
Story TV is India's leading micro-drama platform, with 10M+ users and 500+ original titles produced in partnership with studios like Balaji Telefilms and Applause Entertainment. Master is India's #1 micro-learning app, scaling to 5M+ users in 50 days with 1,000+ bite-sized courses across AI, finance, English, and life skills. 
The Founder's Office is the strategic nerve center of the Group — where vision meets execution, and where the next generation of vertical-first consumer experiences for Bharat is being built.

Role Overview
Program Manager – Founder's Office is a high-impact, high-visibility role designed for an entrepreneurial self-starter who thrives in ambiguity and complexity. You will act as a force multiplier for the Founder/CEO — driving strategic initiatives across Eloelo Group's micro-drama and micro-learning businesses, managing cross-functional programs, and ensuring organizational priorities are executed with speed and precision.
This role demands equal parts strategic thinking, operational excellence, and creative-business fluency. You will build pace, not just plans.

Key Responsibilities
Strategic Execution & Program Management
Own and drive the execution of key strategic initiatives originating from the Founder's Office across the Group's micro-drama and micro-learning verticals.
Build and maintain a dynamic program roadmap — tracking milestones, dependencies, risks, and outcomes across content slates, product launches, growth campaigns, and market expansions.
Translate high-level strategic goals (e.g., scaling Story TV to 100M users, establishing Master as Bharat's lifelong learning companion) into structured plans with clear OKRs, timelines, and accountability matrices.
Conduct regular progress reviews with business unit heads across both verticals and flag deviations proactively to the Founder.
Founder's Office Operations
Manage the Founder's bandwidth across multiple CEO mandates by prioritizing inbound requests, preparing briefing materials, and ensuring follow-through on commitments.
Draft high-quality communication on behalf of the Founder — memos, board presentations, investor updates, partnership pitches, and strategic notes around category creation in micro-drama and micro-learning.
Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to ensure the Founder is well-prepared for key meetings, talent signings, studio partnerships, and category-defining moments.
Maintain strict confidentiality and exercise sound judgment in handling sensitive information — slate strategy, deal terms, financials, and talent contracts.
Cross-Functional Coordination & Governance
Serve as the primary coordination node between the Founder's Office and all functional teams — Content & Studios, Product, Engineering, Growth, Marketing, Finance, HR, and BD — across both verticals.
Lead structured governance mechanisms including weekly leadership syncs, monthly business reviews, quarterly planning, and all-hands preparation.
Identify and resolve organizational bottlenecks — whether a content pipeline stall, product release slip, or hiring gap — escalating only what requires Founder-level attention.
Champion a culture of accountability, documentation, and knowledge-sharing as the Group scales rapidly across teams and geographies.
Special Projects & Business Development
Lead and manage special projects at the Founder's direction — new vertical launches, language expansion (Hindi to regional), creator and talent deals, IP partnerships, studio M&A, and international plays in short-form content.
Conduct industry and competitive analysis across the global short-form ecosystem — ReelShort, DramaBox, Kuku TV, Flick TV in micro-drama; and the broader EdTech and creator-led learning landscape — synthesizing insights into executive-ready formats.
Support fundraising, investor relations, and M&A activities, including data room management, due diligence coordination, and narrative development.
Build relationships with external partners — production houses, talent agencies, distribution platforms, content studios, and investors — on behalf of the Group.

What We're Looking For
Qualifications & Experience
4–8 years of experience in program management, management consulting, strategy, or a Chief of Staff / Founder's Office role
MBA or equivalent postgraduate qualification from a reputed institution preferred; strong academic pedigree a plus.
Demonstrated experience managing complex, cross-functional programs in a fast-paced environment.
Prior experience in a high-growth consumer internet, media, OTT, EdTech, or creator-economy business is highly desirable.
Comfort working with content production cycles, creator/talent ecosystems, or short-form video formats is a strong plus.
Exposure to business operations, P&L management, or 0→1 venture building is a strong differentiator

Skills & Competencies
Exceptional written and verbal communication — you can distill complexity into clarity and craft narratives that move people.
Strong analytical and structured thinking — comfortable with retention cohorts, content economics, financial models, and strategic frameworks.
High emotional intelligence — you can hold space with a creative director, a tech lead, a talent manager, and an investor in the same afternoon, and bring all four to the same outcome.
Mastery of productivity and project management tools (Notion, Asana, Google Workspace, MS Office Suite).
Ability to context-switch rapidly between strategic planning and ground-level execution without losing focus.
Bias for speed — the short-form world moves in days, not quarters, and you keep up.
Discretion, integrity, and a deep sense of ownership — you treat the organization's goals as your own.

What You'll Gain
Direct and daily exposure to the Founder's strategic thinking and decision-making across two category-defining consumer apps.
A rare vantage point on how India's micro-drama and micro-learning categories are being built in rear time.
Real ownership of programs touching tens of millions of users.
A fast track to senior leadership — previous Founder's Office incumbents have moved into business head and VP roles within the Group.
A collaborative, merit-driven culture that celebrates initiative, speed, and bold thinking.
Competitive compensation commensurate with experience, including performance-linked incentives.

items.jobDescription:
Position Overview
We are a seed-funded startup focused on leveraging state-of-the-art AI technologies to revolutionize the credit industry. Our team comprises experts in machine learning and software engineers from top-tier US tech companies like Apple and Amazon. With our product already in the market, first clients onboarded, and sufficient runway secured, we are passionate about using AI to enhance access to credit information for businesses. We are seeking a highly skilled and innovative Founder’s Office - Operations & Product who will partner directly with the founders to unblock growth-critical initiatives, design scalable processes, and craft the narrative for raising. This high-trust role blends the analytical rigor of consulting with the execution speed of a startup, offering unparalleled exposure to every function of the business.

Key Responsibilities
Operations Excellence: Map and streamline end-to-end workflows (onboarding → underwriting → reporting); codify SOPs and drive a 30 % reduction in process TAT.
Strategic Planning: Run quarterly OKR cycles, build board-ready dashboards, and provide competitive intelligence to inform product and market bets.
Problem-Solving Sprints: Own mission-critical projects such as renegotiating data contracts, piloting a new city rollout, or standing up a compliance playbook—end to end.
Investor & Stakeholder Management: Craft fundraising memos, maintain dataroom hygiene, and present monthly KPI packs to investors and advisors.

Qualifications
MBA (preferred) plus 3-7 years in strategy consulting, startup ops, or growth roles.
Track record of launching or optimizing cross-functional projects in high-growth tech / SaaS environments.
Strong analytical toolkit (Excel/Sheets modeling, SQL, BI dashboards) and ability to convert data into executive-ready insights.
Bias for action, comfort with ambiguity, and humility to alternate between board decks and frontline firefighting

What We Offer
Competitive salary and benefits package.
A front-row seat to the zero-to-one journey of an AI-driven fintech, with a clear path to VP Ops or Chief of Staff as we scale.
A collaborative and dynamic work environment.
Show more Show less

Location: Okhla Phase 2, New Delhi
Timings: 11:30 AM – 8:30 PM
Working Days: Monday to Saturday (Off on the last Saturday of every month)
Experience: 6 months – 2 years
Department: CEO’s Office
Employment Type: Full-time
Growth Path: Opportunity to transition into the Executive Assistant role within 12 months
The Founder’s Office at StarClinch is the inner circle working directly with the CEO to streamline execution, remove cross-functional bottlenecks, and align teams toward company priorities.
This is a high-speed, dynamic role—not a routine 9-to-5 job. It demands sharp thinking, speed, ownership, and proactive problem-solving. You’ll be expected to wear multiple hats, leverage AI tools, and work directly with a sharp, detail-driven founder.
If you’re someone who enjoys structure but thrives in chaos, communicates well, and wants to operate at the center of a fast-growing startup—this role is for you.

Key Responsibilities


Execution & Operations
Solve bottlenecks across departments—artist onboarding, revenue ops, support, and hiring
Create systems, workflows, and SOPs from scratch and monitor their adoption
Track business metrics, identify red flags, and report updates to the founder regularly
Coordinate across internal teams to ensure deadlines are met
Business & Strategic Projects
Conduct research for market trends, competitor benchmarks, new features, and pricing models
Build dashboards and data summaries using Excel/Sheets or Notion
Work closely with tech, sales, artist relations, and marketing teams to align execution with goals
Own ad hoc, high-priority projects that need founder-level oversight
Founder Support & Communication
Draft emails, investor updates, decks, and presentations
Take ownership of meeting agendas, follow-ups, and implementation
Ensure seamless communication and alignment across all internal and external stakeholders
Think two steps ahead of the founder and proactively clear roadblocks









Requirements


Must-Haves


2–5 years of experience in a coordination, operations, or founder’s office role
Excellent written and spoken English
High attention to detail, strong organizational ability, and multitasking skills
Comfort with using tools like ChatGPT, Notion AI, or similar workflow/automation tools
Ability to think independently, learn quickly, and adapt in real-time



Benefits


What You’ll Get


Direct mentorship from the founder with deep exposure to startup operations
Diverse learning across business, product, marketing, and strategy
Autonomy to build and own internal systems, processes, and special projects
Fast-track growth to leadership roles (Chief of Staff, Business Head, etc.)
The satisfaction of building something meaningful and industry-defining

Title: Founder's Office Intern - Generalist (On-Site | Gurgaon)
Location: Gurgaon (On-Site, Shared Accommodation Provided)
Type: Internship (Full-Time, 6 days/week, 10 hours/day | Minimum 3 months)
Stipend: ₹45,000/month (₹10,000 cash + ₹35,000 housing expenses)

About Us
We're a spiritual lifestyle startup led by an IIT Roorkee '19 graduate. Achieved 15x growth in 3 months to ₹2Cr+ revenue run rate. Backed by founders of Bewakoof, Upside Learning, ex CTO of slice, IIT Roorkee, IIT Bombay, Stanford alums looking to scale rapidly in 2026. As we close our seed round, we're looking for a Founder's Office Intern to work directly with the founder. This is an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on exposure to various aspects of a startup, from operations to strategy, and play a direct role in scaling the business.

Responsibilities
As a Founder's Office Intern, your work will be diverse and impactful. Your key responsibilities will include:
Product Management: Focus on expanding new products, and making the current ones better.
P & L: Find ways to expand the revenue from the current business lines.
Automation: Automate marketing material flow and other processes using AI tools to improve efficiency.
Research: Conduct research on various topics as required by the founder, such as market trends, competition, and product strategies.
Ecommerce Platforms: Handle operations and growth on platforms like Amazon, Flipkart, and others.
Online Presence: Contribute to the growth of the startup's online presence across platforms.
Offline Distribution: Help execute distribution strategies to expand offline presence.
Adhoc Sales & Operations: Support sales and operational tasks as needed.
General Startup Tasks: Take on various tasks as required by the startup, ensuring smooth operations and growth.
Customer Interaction: Engage with past customers to gather insights and brainstorm new ideas for growth.

What We Offer
Live with the Founder & Founding Team: Stay in the villa alongside the founder and core team – an unparalleled opportunity to learn directly from leadership and build deep connections.
Accommodation: Shared room in our Gurgaon villa, with all amenities included (food, cook, maid, and electricity).
Hands-on Learning: Direct exposure to strategy, marketing, automation, e-commerce, and startup operations while working closely with the founder.
Growth Opportunity: After 3 months, stipend will increase, and the role can convert to a founding member position. Last 2 hires we did for the same role have moved to higher pay in a period of 4 months, one being made Chief of Staff with equity in the company as well.
Opportunity to work on projects in a fast-paced and dynamic startup environment, see your work create real value.

Requirements
Strong interest in startups, innovation, and working in a fast-paced environment.
Organizational skills to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines effectively.
Understanding of brand building, and marketing.
Analytical mindset for problem-solving and creating efficient workflows.
Proficiency in tools like Shopify, Excel/Google Sheets, and familiarity with ecommerce platforms like Amazon and Flipkart is a plus.
Interest in AI tools and automation to streamline workflows and increase efficiency.
Strong communication skills (written and verbal) for coordinating with the founder, team, and external stakeholders.
Ability to adapt to changing priorities and take ownership of tasks.
Minimum commitment of 3 months.

Details
Location: Gurgaon (you'll live with the team in the villa where we operate). Separate floors for men and women.
Work Hours: 6 days a week, 10 hours/day.
Joining: ASAP

Reports to: Chief Executive Officer

Start Date: Immediate

Location: Bengaluru

The Chief of Staff will partner closely with the Chief Executive Officer to drive organizational execution, streamline systems, and oversee cross-functional initiatives in a rapidly growing organization. This role requires a high-agency, strategic, and execution-oriented leader who thrives in ambiguous environments, spots organizational gaps early, and independently drives solutions end-to-end. The ideal candidate brings strong experience in organizational building, systems implementation, and cross-team coordination in a growing or fast-scaling environment. The Chief of Staff will serve as a key bridge between the CEO, leadership team, and external stakeholders, ensuring that priorities translate into action and that the organization operates with clarity, accountability, and momentum.

Key Responsibilities

Act as a strategic thought partner to the CEO, helping define organizational priorities and ensuring they are clearly communicated and executed across teams.
Translate strategy into structured execution plans with clear ownership, milestones, and accountability, and track progress across key organizational initiatives.
Proactively identify organizational gaps, bottlenecks, and opportunities, and independently design and drive solutions end-to-end.
Lead and monitor the execution of high-priority initiatives across operations, fundraising, HR, technology, and communications, ensuring cross-team alignment and timely delivery.
Build and implement systems for project management, decision tracking, internal communication, and information flow to strengthen organizational effectiveness.
Introduce and manage enterprise tools such as CRMs, project management platforms, knowledge repositories, and AI-enabled productivity tools, ensuring adoption and consistent use across teams.
Support leadership team processes including annual planning, progress reviews, and resolution of cross-functional bottlenecks in collaboration with the CEO, Directors, and Program Managers.
Represent the CEO Office in meetings with donors, advisors, partners, and senior stakeholders, ensuring clear communication and diligent follow-through.
Support fundraising and external engagement through proposal development, narrative building, data consolidation, and coordinated donor communication.
Strengthen organization-wide communication and contribute to outward-facing storytelling that effectively conveys the organization’s work, impact, and vision.
Lead special strategic projects requiring cross-department collaboration, structured problem-solving, and high execution ownership.

Required Skills

At least 15+ years of experience in a Development Sector Organization leading cross-functional projects, systems-building, or strategic execution in a growing organization (social enterprise, startup, consulting, climate/sustainability sector preferred).
Proven ability to operate with high agency and ownership, identifying problems early and driving solutions from concept through execution.
Exceptional project and operations leadership, with a proven ability to bring structure, clarity, and accountability to complex and unstructured settings.
Highly proactive, execution-oriented self-starter who anticipates needs, drives initiatives forward, and works independently with minimal oversight.
Outstanding communication skills, including writing, facilitation, and executive-level presentations.
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities while collaborating effectively across diverse, multidisciplinary teams.
Strong analytical and organizational skills, with hands-on experience implementing and managing digital systems (CRMs, project management platforms, and knowledge-management tools).
Strong external-facing capability, with confidence engaging donors, partners, advisors, and senior stakeholders.
Comfort using and experimenting with technology and AI tools to improve productivity, decision-making, and organizational systems.
Entrepreneurial, collaborative, and mission-driven, with strong curiosity and interest in wildlife conservation, climate action, and rural development.

Application Process

Letter of Intent (1 – 2 pages), describing personal interest and experience.
Professional References (minimum of 3 with contact details).
Resume/CV with relevant work experience.

items.jobTitle: Chief of Staff / 社長室（CEO直下・全社戦略推進担当）
items.companyName: 株式会社GA technologies / GA technologies Co., Ltd.
items.jobDescription:
経営の意思決定を成果へと変える。グループ全体の非連続成長を牽引する社長室メンバーを募集！

Description

会社概要

GA technologiesは 「テクノロジー × イノベーションで驚きと感動を生み、世界を前進させる。」 をミッションに掲げ、

創業5年での上場後も年間30%超の成長を続け、現在は売上高2,000億円を超える成長を遂げています。

不動産業界は65兆円という巨大なマーケットでありながら、今なおアナログな慣習が色濃く残るレガシーな産業であり、

当社はデータとテクノロジーを駆使した自社プラットフォーム「RENOSY（リノシー）」をはじめとする多様なサービスを展開しています。

私たちは、さらなるグローバル展開と事業拡大を見据えた組織の再定義を進める、いわば第二創業期にあります。

すでに米国・中国を含む7カ国で不動産取引事業を展開しており、10社を超えるグループ会社とも連携しながら事業を推進しています。

巨大産業の変革にグループ横断で挑んでいくような、スケールの大きな戦略実行に興味関心のある方のご応募をお待ちしています。

▶︎ 事業や会社に関する紹介資料：外部リンク

ミッション

CEO直下で、グループ全体の戦略立案から実行までを一気通貫でリードし、事業成長を最大化させることがミッションです。

入社後は、CEOの意思決定支援からスタートし、その後は特命案件やM&A/PMI、事業KPIの最適化などの重要プロジェクトを段階的にお任せします。

具体的な業務内容

CEOの意思決定支援
経営課題に関する情報整理、論点設計、および資料作成。
グループ横断プロジェクトの企画・推進
部門間の連携不足を解消し、プロジェクト管理プロセスを標準化・浸透させるPMO業務。
事業戦略・中期経営計画の立案と実行
粗利、生産性、資本効率などの事業KPI設計およびモニタリング。
M&A・PMIのサポート
グループ拡大に伴う投資判断支援および買収後の統合プロセス（PMI）の推進。
IR・経営ストーリーの設計支援
投資家向けの経営メッセージの具体化およびストーリー構築のサポート。
特命案件の実行
領域を問わず、経営上重要な優先度の高い案件が発生した際の対応。
チーム体制

本ポジションは、CEO直下の立ち上げ組織になります。

社内のあらゆるステークホルダーと密に連携しながら、全社的な意思決定を支援いただきます。

本ポジションの魅力

◎ 「壁打ち相手」で終わらない、成長企業のCEO直下での戦略実行経験

本ポジションでは、単なる戦略の企画やアドバイザーにとどまらず、自ら手を動かして「成果を出す」ことが求められます。

経営陣の意思決定をいち早くキャッチし、複数部署を巻き込む全社横断プロジェクトとして自ら推進・完遂させる、手触り感のある事業運営を経験できます。

◎ 領域を限定せず、経営や事業の中枢へと繋がる複線的なキャリア

立ち上げ組織のメンバーだからこそ、自分の役割を限定せずに立ち回ることが可能です。

まずはCEOの意思決定支援からスタートし、実績に応じて特命案件、M&AやPMIの推進、IRストーリーの構築など、

経営インパクトの大きい領域への段階的なキャリアの広がりがあります。

◎ 巨大産業で「非連続な成長」を仕掛ける面白さ

GDPの10%以上を占める巨大な不動産市場において、テクノロジーを起点とした事業拡大のフェーズに中枢で参画できます。

不確実な状況下でも自ら問いを立て、事業をグロースさせる、いわゆる攻めの経営を最前線でリードする醍醐味があります。

Requirements

事業会社での経営企画・事業企画・PMO・新規事業推進、またはコンサルティングファームでのプロジェクト推進経験（目安3年以上）
複数部署を巻き込むプロジェクトをオーナーとして推進し、成果創出までやり切った経験
KPI設計、予実管理、PL管理、業務改善のいずれかを通じて、事業成長や生産性向上に貢献した経験
当社ミッション・バリュー（GA VALUES）への強い共感

Preferred Experiences

SaaSやプラットフォームビジネス、データ分析・AI領域での経験
事業の中長期の戦略立案や、事業計画の策定に携わった経験
経営層向けの論点整理、資料作成、数値分析を通じて、意思決定を支援した経験
データ分析やAIツールといった先進的なツールを業務利用した経験
グローバルビジネスへの知見や経験

We are looking for

役割を限定せず、必要なことを自ら取りに行くことができる方
CEOと同じ高い視座を志向しつつ、事業の現場に入り込んでいける方
スピード感を重視し、実行による成果に価値を置ける方

Working Conditions

Salary

年収：1,000万円〜1,500万円

月額 83.4万円〜125万円（30時間分の固定残業代含む：183,100円〜274,400円）

※スキル・経験・能力に応じて決定いたします

※超過分は別途支給

Location

▍東京本社

〒106-6290 東京都港区六本木3丁目2番1号

住友不動産六本木グランドタワー39・40・42階

▍アクセス

・南北線「六本⽊⼀丁⽬」駅 直結

・都営⼤江⼾線・⽇⽐⾕線「六本⽊」駅 徒歩5分

・南北線・銀座線「溜池⼭王」駅 徒歩8分

・⽇⽐⾕線「神⾕町」駅 徒歩10分

※就業場所変更の範囲について

経営状況、配属先の状況等を総合的に勘案し、会社の指定する事業所へ変更となる場合ございます。

Job Type 正社員 Work hours

▍勤務時間

月～金曜日 フルフレックスタイム制（7時00分～22時00分 の間でコアタイムなし）

月の総労働時間は、1日の標準労働時間8時間×月毎の労働日数を乗じた時間とする。

▍休憩・休日

休憩：1日の勤務時間が6時間を超える場合、休憩45分 8時間を超える場合、休憩60分

休日：完全週休2日制 （配属部署は原則土日祝日は休み）

Probation period あり(3ヵ月) Benefits

▍各種社会保険完備

▍福利厚生

・住宅手当（会社から半径3km以内で2万円もしくは3万円）

・フルフレックス制度（部署ごとに内規あり）

・時短勤務制度（1日最低4時間勤務以上）

・在宅勤務制度（育児・介護・妊娠中の体調安定などを目的）

・家族手当（配偶者や18歳未満の子を扶養している場合毎月1万円以上を支給）

・保育手当（保育園、幼稚園、認定こども園に入園させている社員に対し、給与支給時に1万円を手当として支給）

・自己研鑽制度（書籍の購入費用や外部セミナーへの参加費用などを会社が負担）

・資格手当（宅建、建築士など）

・エンジニア資格取得支援制度（資格の難易度に応じたお祝い金・受験費用を支給）

・資格の総合スクール「LEC」法人会員（指定講座を特別価格で受講可能）

・オンライン英会話割引（英会話研修サービスを特別価格で受講可能）

・社員持株会（少額の資金で自社株式を取得できる制度）

・確定拠出年金（入社時に加入可能）

・スポーツジム「ジョイフィット」法人会員

・ホットヨガスタジオ「LAVA」法人会員

・ジョブリターン制度（結婚・出産・育児・介護・病気療養・就学等の事情で退職した社員が、退職理由が解消した際に再びGAグループに戻りやすくなる制度）

・企業主導型保育園（社員の家庭・子育てを支援するため、企業が設置する保育所を利用可能）

▍その他休暇制度

・出産準備休暇

・ベビーケア休暇

・子の看護休暇

・介護休暇

・慶弔休暇

・年末年始休暇（12/29〜1/3）

・年次有給休暇

Company Description
Servotech Renewable Power System Limited, a publicly listed company on the NSE, is a leading innovator in renewable energy and sustainable technology solutions. With over two decades of experience, Servotech specializes in the manufacturing, procurement, and distribution of advanced solar products, energy-efficient lighting solutions, and medical devices. Recently expanding into the electric vehicle (EV) market, the company aims to establish a robust EV charging infrastructure across India to accelerate the nation’s drive toward cleaner energy. Known for its cutting-edge technology and innovation, Servotech has solidified its reputation as a trusted and credible provider of high-quality solutions, consistently exceeding client expectations and setting new industry benchmarks.

Role Description
This is a full-time, on-site Chief of Staff position based in Delhi, India. The Chief of Staff will collaborate closely with the executive team to manage the organization's strategic priorities, streamline operations, and oversee cross-functional initiatives. 

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES 
MD Office Governance & Execution Control 
Convert MD priorities into weekly/monthly execution plans 
Run structured review cadences (WBR / MBR) Track key decisions → ensure closure, not discussion 
Prepare sharp, data-backed executive briefs 
Strategic Initiative Ownership 
Own and drive company-wide strategic programs: Revenue scale-up initiatives o EV / Solar expansion programs o Cost optimization & margin improvement 
Ensure: o Clear ownership o Defined timelines o Measurable KPIs 
Act as single point of accountability for execution 
Cross-Functional Alignment 
Break silos between: o Sales, Operations, Supply Chain, Finance, R&D
Resolve execution bottlenecks in real time 
Drive alignment between department goals & company targets 
Business Performance & KPI Tracking 
Design and monitor company-wide dashboards 
rack: o Revenue vs target o Project execution status 
Collections & working capital o Operational efficiency metrics 
Highlight: o Risks o Delays o Underperformance → with action plans, not just reporting 
Operational Excellence & Process Improvement 
Identify process inefficiencies across functions 
Standardize workflows and introduce execution discipline 
Reduce: o Delays o Dependency gaps o Manual inefficiencies 
Digital & AI Transformation 
Lead adoption of: o AI tools for reporting & decision-making 
Automation in workflows (HRMS, CRM, MIS, dashboards) 
Build data-driven decision culture 
Drive measurable improvements through digital initiatives
Leadership Influence & Stakeholder Management 
Act as extension of MD in execution forums 
Challenge function heads where needed (constructively)
Ensure commitment → accountability → delivery cycle

Role: Chief of Staff - Program Operations
Join NIAT as Our Chief of Staff – Program Operations
Are you ready to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of tech education in India? At the NxtWave Institute of Advanced Technologies (NIAT), we're seeking a dynamic Chief of Staff to lead our Program Operations. This is your opportunity to be at the forefront of educational innovation, guiding the next generation of tech leaders. 
About NIAT
NIAT stands as NxtWave’s flagship four-year, on-campus program in Computer Science Education. Our curriculum is among India’s most advanced, meticulously aligned with industry demands. Headquartered in Hyderabad's vibrant tech landscape, our Hyderabad campus is surrounded by global giants like Google, Microsoft, Apple, Infosys, and TCS, offering our students unparalleled exposure to the world of technology. 
With our phenomenal success in Hyderabad’s first two cohorts, NIAT has proven to be the ultimate launchpad for future tech leaders. Now, we’re taking this revolution nationwide. This is your moment to step up, drive change, and be at the helm of an educational movement that’s shaping India’s tech landscape.
As we expand across the country, we need visionary leaders who can build, innovate, and elevate our impact to new heights. If you’re driven by a passion for student success and the power of cutting-edge education, this is your once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to be at the forefront of the next big wave in tech education.
Are you ready to lead this transformation? Join us and make history. 🚀
Why Join Us?
Innovate Education: Be part of a revolutionary institution that's redefining computer science education in India. 
Dynamic Environment: Work alongside CEO, top-tier management, mentors and industry professionals in a fast-paced, growth-oriented setting. 
Career Advancement: Seize opportunities for professional development and career growth within a pioneering educational ecosystem. 
Your Role
As the Chief of Staff – Program Operations, you will:
Strategize and Lead: Develop and implement strategies to enhance program operations, ensuring seamless collaboration across teams. 
Oversee Performance: Manage Profit & Loss (P&L) and lead cross-functional teams, aligning campus initiatives with our central program objectives. 
Drive Student Success: Collaborate with academic and student success teams to monitor and boost student progress and outcomes. 
Enhance Engagement: Continuously refine processes to elevate student engagement, improving pedagogy, content, and class delivery. 
Elevate Campus Experience: Lead initiatives to enrich the student experience, including campus branding and marketing efforts. 
Foster Industry Connections: Work closely with placement and training cells to establish industry partnerships, securing robust internship and placement opportunities. 
Ensure Operational Excellence: Oversee on-ground operations for assessments, events, and program-related activities to ensure the smooth execution of our curriculum. 
What We’re Looking For
Results-Driven Leader: You thrive in fast-paced environments and are committed to achieving excellence. 
Process Innovator: You balance long-term process improvements with short-term goal attainment. 
Student-Centric Mentor: You possess empathy towards students and are dedicated to fostering their success. 
Skilled Communicator: You have excellent communication and interpersonal skills, adept at engaging with multiple stakeholders. 
Qualified Professional: You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Strategy, or a related field. 
Strategic Thinker: Previous experience in strategy or business planning is a plus. 
Local Insight: Being a native of the assigned campus's geographic state is preferred, as we have campuses in Hyderabad, Pune, Kolhapur, Mangalore, Bangalore, Chennai, Vijayawada, Jaipur, and Delhi. 
Location & Work Details
Working Days: Monday to Saturday (6-day workweek) 
Work Timings: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM 
Compensation: Competitive CTC based on experience and qualifications 
Work Location: During training period(2-3 months) Hyderabad and post training on the assigned Campuses ( Hyderabad/ Chennai/ Bangalore / Pune / Delhi)
If you're passionate about mentoring and shaping student success, this is your opportunity to make an impact! Apply now and be a part of the NxtWave revolution!

items.jobTitle: Chief of Staff to the Chairman
items.jobDescription:
SUMMARY

The Chief of Staff serves as the Chairman’s strategic partner, operational anchor, and execution engine across a wide portfolio of businesses, investments, and new ventures. This role supports the Chairman’s highest priorities by driving clarity, velocity, and alignment across projects, ensuring that vision is consistently translated into action. The Chief of Staff will streamline decision-making, manage complex multi-stakeholder initiatives, oversee information flow, and proactively remove barriers so the Chairman can operate at maximum impact. This position also includes managing select personal scheduling, meetings, and commitments to protect the Chairman’s time and enable focus.

This role is responsible for driving high-priority AI-enabled initiatives from concept through outcome. This role will operate as a systems builder and execution driver—owning complex, cross-functional initiatives end-to-end, accelerating engineering velocity, and enforcing a culture of urgency and accountability. The ideal candidate translates vision into systems, pushes teams to ship at speed, and maintains an “always-on” posture to ensure momentum never stalls. This role requires strong technical fluency, experience implementing AI-enabled workflows, and the confidence to hold senior contributors accountable without hesitation.

It is ideal for a highly intelligent, resourceful operator who thrives in a fast-paced environment, navigates ambiguity with confidence, and brings a disciplined, solutions-oriented approach to every challenge.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategic Partnership

Serve as a trusted advisor and thought partner to the Chairman, helping shape priorities, evaluate opportunities, and support informed decision-making across businesses and projects.
Translate high-level vision into clear plans, timelines, structures, and next steps that drive measurable progress.
Anticipate needs, identify blind spots, and proactively surface insights or recommendations to keep initiatives on track.

Technical Execution & AI Systems Delivery

Lead AI-enabled initiatives from concept to live deployment, ensuring measurable business impact.
Design, prototype, and implement AI workflows, automation systems, and operational tools that increase leverage and speed across ventures. 

Operational Leadership

Implement systems, processes, and tools that improve workflow, eliminate inefficiencies, and optimize the Chairman’s time.
Oversee information flow to and from the Chairman, ensuring the right inputs reach the right people at the right moment.
Establish operating rhythms, including dashboards, reviews, reporting structures, and communication cadences across ventures.

Project and Venture Management

Establish aggressive execution timelines and hold teams accountable to them.
Drive a high-urgency operating cadence with tight feedback loops, clear ownership, and minimal bureaucracy.
Escalate risks immediately and directly when deliverables or response times fall short.
Lead high-impact projects from concept through execution, coordinating across teams, partners, and external vendors.
Manage special initiatives related to new business development, investment diligence, strategic partnerships, and corporate innovation.
Track progress, risks, deliverables, and KPIs, ensuring alignment with the Chairman’s goals and expectations.

Stakeholder and Relationship Management

Act as an extension of the Chairman in select meetings, communications, and negotiations, ensuring seamless representation and continuity.
Prepare briefs, talking points, memos, and follow-ups for internal and external engagements.
Help maintain and strengthen relationships with senior executives, partners, investors, boards, founders, and strategic collaborators.

Communications and Analysis

Produce executive-level summaries, recommendations, presentations, and research that support strategic choices.
Synthesize information across businesses, markets, and teams into concise, actionable insights.
Drive follow-through to ensure decisions become outcomes.

QUALIFICATIONS

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Communications, Public Policy, or a related field; advanced degree preferred.
7 to 12 years of experience supporting a senior executive, founder, principal, or chairman across multiple ventures or a complex operating environment.
Demonstrated experience building or implementing real AI workflows, automation systems, or AI-enabled operational tools—not just experimentation or prompt usage.
Proven record operating as a strategic generalist and project leader with exceptional judgment.
Ability to thrive in a fast-moving, entrepreneurial environment with shifting priorities.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills with a polished, executive presence.
Highly organized, detail-intensive, and able to manage complex workflows across many concurrent initiatives.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to synthesize complexity quickly.
High integrity, emotional intelligence, and discretion in all interactions.
Advanced proficiency in productivity platforms, project management tools, and modern tech solutions that enhance executive efficiency.
Resourceful, proactive, and unflappable under pressure.
Show more Show less

items.jobDescription:
‍

‍

Overview

YXS Capital is an award-winning capital platform backing companies redefining how the world builds, powers, moves, and computes. With a top-decile returns, YXS Capital has backed category leaders such as HubSpot, Canva, Mercury, Payfit, and Wise. We don't just deploy capital, we engineer outcomes through proven operational expertise, direct enterprise networks, and advanced market insights. Let's move the world forward, together.

Role Description

We are hiring a Chief of Staff to work directly with the Managing Partners across partnership initiatives, external coordination, and firm-wide execution. This is a high-trust, high-responsibility role for someone who operates with strong judgment, ownership, and the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously. You will serve as a core extension of leadership, supporting strategic initiatives, coordinating across internal and external stakeholders, and ensuring alignment across the firm’s most important priorities. This role sits at the center of decision-making and requires both execution ability and strategic awareness. This role is designed for candidates who want exposure to how a high-performance investment platform is built and operated, while contributing directly to outcomes across partnerships, communications, and internal initiatives.

Responsibilities

Partnership Initiatives: Support the development and execution of strategic partnerships across enterprise, government, and institutional stakeholders. Coordinate initiatives aligned with the firm’s investment and value creation strategy.
External Coordination & Communication: Manage communication with LPs, partners, portfolio companies, and key stakeholders. Prepare materials, briefings, and follow-ups to ensure consistency and alignment.
Marketing & Brand Support: Contribute to brand-building initiatives including events, conferences, private dinners, and digital presence. Support the development of presentations, reports, and external-facing materials.
Strategic Execution: Drive execution of high-priority initiatives across the firm. Maintain visibility across ongoing projects and ensure timelines and deliverables are met.
Internal Coordination: Align workstreams across investment, operations, and external functions. Ensure information flows efficiently and priorities are executed without friction.
Ad Hoc & Cross-Functional Support: Contribute across the firm on operational, strategic, and internal initiatives as priorities evolve.

‍What We're Looking For

Relevant Experience: 2–6 years in consulting, investment banking, venture capital, or high-performance startup environments.
Strong Organizational Ability: Ability to manage multiple workstreams simultaneously and operate in a fast-paced environment.
Communication Skills: Exceptional written and verbal communication. Comfortable interacting with senior stakeholders.
Judgment and Discretion: Ability to handle sensitive information and operate with professionalism and sound judgment.
Ownership Mindset: Self-directed, accountable, and proactive in driving work forward without constant oversight.
Interest in Venture Capital: Strong interest in technology, investing, and the broader venture ecosystem.

Is This You?

Apply If

You want to operate at the center of decision-making and firm strategy.
You enjoy coordinating complex initiatives across multiple stakeholders.
You are highly organized and execute at a consistently high standard.
You want exposure to partnerships, investing, and firm-building.
You thrive in high-performance environments with significant responsibility.

‍Don't Apply If

You prefer narrowly defined roles with limited scope.
You are uncomfortable managing multiple priorities simultaneously.
You prefer predictable schedules or low-intensity environments.
You are looking for a purely analytical or purely marketing role.
You want a remote-only position.

Compensation & Benefits

Competitive Base Salary: Benchmarked to top-tier roles at this level.
Performance Bonus: Discretionary, tied to execution and impact.
Exposure: Direct access to Managing Partners and strategic initiatives.
Full Benefits Package: Health, dental, vision, and 401(k).

‍At YXS Capital, we believe that exceptional technology is built by diverse minds. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other protected status. Employment is contingent upon the successful completion of a background check, administered in accordance with applicable laws.

‍

‍

Convert ideas into clear action plans with timelines

We're hiring a Senior Manager – Founder's Office to work directly with the Founder/CEO and the central Centre of Excellence (COE) team at Pepper Content. This role partners closely with our US business and leadership, so you'll be working US hours (5:00 PM – 2:30 AM IST) from our Mumbai office. This is a high-ownership role for someone who thrives in ambiguity, runs hard at problems, and turns founder-level priorities into shipped outcomes. You'll own cross-functional programs spanning AI transformation, GTM, operations, and strategy — and you'll be measured on what gets done, not what gets discussed.

Location & Hours

Base location: Mumbai (in-office).

Working hours: 5:00 PM – 2:30 AM IST, aligned to US business hours, Monday–Friday.
This is non-negotiable — the role requires real-time collaboration with the Founder and US-based stakeholders. Please apply only if you're comfortable with this schedule.

What You'll Do

Drive end-to-end program management for the Founder's most critical initiatives — set goals, define milestones, unblock teams, and ensure on-time, on-quality delivery.
Lead AI transformation programs across the company: identify high-leverage workflows, partner with product/engineering/ops to deploy AI-first solutions, and measure impact on speed, cost, and quality.
Partner with the central COE team to scale playbooks, processes, and tooling across business units — bringing operational rigour to areas that are still founder-led or ad-hoc.
Build dashboards, reviews, and operating cadences (weekly/monthly business reviews, OKR tracking, exec updates) that give the Founder real-time visibility into what's working and what isn't.
Step in as a force multiplier — own special projects end-to-end, from M&A diligence and fundraising prep to new market entry, pricing experiments, or customer escalations.
Translate founder vision into crisp briefs, plans, and execution roadmaps that other teams can run with.
Act as the primary bridge between the India team and US stakeholders — ensuring nothing falls through the cracks across time zones.

What We're Looking For

5–9 years of experience in a mix of consulting (top-tier MBB/Big-4), high-growth startup operations, founder's office, or chief of staff roles.
Demonstrated ability to drive large, cross-functional programs at scale — you've owned things end-to-end, not just coordinated.
Strong bias for action: you default to shipping, not deliberating. You'd rather get a v1 out and iterate than hold a third meeting.
Hands-on familiarity with AI tools (LLMs, agents, automation platforms) and a clear point of view on how AI changes how work gets done. Bonus if you've actually deployed AI workflows in a business setting.
Sharp first-principles thinking, structured problem-solving, and the ability to go from boardroom strategy to spreadsheet-level execution in the same day.
Excellent written and verbal communication — you can write a one-pager that lands with the founder, the board, or a frontline team.
High ownership, low ego, and the operational rigour to follow up on the 100th detail without losing sight of the bigger picture.
Comfortable working US hours from Mumbai on a sustained basis — prior experience working with US teams/clients is a strong plus.

Why This Role

Direct line to the Founder and the leadership team — the work you do shapes company direction.
Front-row seat to AI transformation in a content + marketplace business at scale.
Deep exposure to the US market, which is Pepper's fastest-growing segment.
Fast track to leadership roles within Pepper as the company grows.
Show more Show less
