items.companyName: D. E. Shaw India Private Limited
items.jobDescription:
The D. E. Shaw group is a global investment and technology development firm with more than $90 billion in investment capital as of March 1, 2026, and offices in North America, Europe, and Asia. Since our founding in 1988, our firm has earned an international reputation for successful investing based on innovation, careful risk management, and the quality and depth of our staff. We have a significant presence in the world's capital markets, investing in a wide range of companies and financial instruments in both developed and developing economies.

We are looking for an intellectually curious, tech-savvy, and highly articulate professional for the Office of the Operating Committee (OC) to join our firm’s office in Hyderabad, India. D. E. Shaw India (DESIS) is led by a two-member Operating Committee and the OC works collectively to manage day-to-day operations and chart the strategic course for the firm. This collaborative leadership model sets us apart and reflects our core principles.

WHAT YOU'LL DO DAY-TO-DAY:

You will support the OC Chief of Staff in program managing firmwide goals — planning, tracking milestones, and reporting progress in a structured and periodic manner — while establishing rapport and coordinating with senior business unit heads across India and the firm's global offices on strategic priorities and action items. In this role, you will research and synthesize data from disparate sources to develop recommendations, apply quantitative and financial modelling skills and leveraging technology — including Gen AI tools and, where relevant, code — to automate workflows, build analytical tools, and improve the efficiency and quality of outputs. Additionally, you will produce high-quality written materials including business proposals, firmwide communications, blogs, and quarterly and semi-annual reports, while also maintaining the OC's internal knowledge repository, ensuring accurate version control, archiving, and accessibility of reports, presentations, and data insights.

WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

Basic qualifications:

A bachelor's degree (MBA preferred) and 5-10 years of professional experience, with the maturity to respect and adhere to the highest ethical and confidentiality standards
Exceptional analytical foundation — comfortable building models, working with data, and translating quantitative findings into clear recommendations
The ability to demonstrate experience in financial modelling and the ability to structure a cost-benefit analysis independently without deferring to specialists
Genuine, practiced fluency with Gen AI tools as a core daily productivity multiplier — for research, writing, analysis, and problem-solving
The ability to use Gen AI for scripting or coding to build lightweight tools, automate repetitive workflows, and improve the quality and speed of output
The comfortability with prompt engineering — able to craft precise, effective prompts that produce reliable outputs and critically interrogate AI-generated content rather than accepting it at face value; familiar with agentic AI workflows, able to define goals for an AI agent, review and improve its outputs, and delegate multi-step tasks to AI-assisted pipelines without needing engineering support
The ability to design and deploy simple agentic workflows — for example, chaining tools or AI steps to automate multi-stage processes such as research synthesis, report generation, or data monitoring — and to build reusable, context-rich AI workspace environments for recurring tasks
Excellent written communication skills with the ability to produce detailed proposals, crisp executive-facing documents, and clear firmwide articles, calibrating tone and depth across audiences from the OC to all-staff
The skill to be adept at context-switching across multiple projects with volatile priorities — organized, structured, and reliable, with a reputation for owning work and seeing it through; comfortable operating behind the scenes in support of senior leadership while building solid relationships across levels
Exceptional analytical and communication skills with hands-on command of Gen AI tools
The ability to be self-assured, collaborative, and comfortable operating with volatility, ambiguity, and shifting priorities

Interested candidates can apply through our website: https://www.deshawindia.com/recruit/jobs/Adv/Link/MgSnrMrgOCDesMgmApr2026

We encourage candidates with relevant experience looking to restart their careers after a break to apply for this position. Learn about Recommence, our gender-neutral return-to-work initiative.

The Firm offers excellent benefits, a casual, collegial working environment, and an attractive compensation package. For further information about our recruitment process, including how applicant data will be processed, please visit https://www.deshawindia.com/careers.

Members of the D. E. Shaw group do not discriminate in employment matters on the basis of sex, race, colour, caste, creed, religion, pregnancy, national origin, age, military service eligibility, veteran status, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, or any other protected class.

items.jobDescription:
Job Title*: Nursing Head / Nursing Lead
Location*: Kolkata
Department*: Nursing
Employment Type*: Full-Time
Reporting To*: Chief Medical Officer / CHMO / Medical Director

*Role Summary*

The Nursing Head is responsible for ensuring high-quality patient care, efficient nursing operations, and compliance with clinical standards. This role oversees nursing staff, drives patient safety initiatives, and ensures smooth coordination across clinical departments.

*Key Responsibilities*

*1. Clinical Quality & Patient Care*

Ensure delivery of high-quality nursing care aligned with clinical protocols

Monitor quality indicators, patient outcomes, and incident reports

Drive continuous improvement in patient safety and care standards

*2. Nursing Operations & Manpower Management*

Manage staffing, duty rosters, and workforce planning

Lead recruitment, onboarding, and retention of nursing staff

Maintain skill matrix and allocate resources effectively

*3. Infection Control & Compliance*

Implement infection control protocols to prevent HAIs

Ensure compliance with NABH/JCI guidelines and internal policies

Oversee biomedical waste (BMW) management and hygiene practices

*4. Medical Records & Documentation*

Ensure accurate and timely documentation of patient records

Maintain compliance with regulatory and accreditation standards

*5. Patient Flow Management*

Oversee admissions, transfers, and discharges

Coordinate with doctors and operations team for seamless patient movement

*6. Patient Experience & Service Excellence*

Ensure compassionate, patient-centric care delivery

Monitor patient satisfaction and implement improvements

Maintain discipline, grooming, and professional conduct of staff

*7. Inventory & Resource Management*

Manage nursing inventory, equipment, and supplies

Ensure timely maintenance and availability of critical resources

*8. Cost Control & Efficiency*

Optimize staffing costs and reduce overtime

Prevent revenue leakage and ensure proper billing coordination

*9. Training & Development*

Conduct regular training as per L&D calendar

Mentor and develop nursing staff for leadership roles

Ensure continuous skill enhancement and compliance training

Key Result Areas (KRAs)

100% adherence to quality indicators & incident reporting

High patient satisfaction scores (≥4.5/5)

100% compliance in medical records and audits

Zero tolerance for infection control violations

Effective cost management and operational efficiency

Minimum 4 hours/month training per staff

*Qualifications & Experience*

GNM / B.Sc Nursing (M.Sc Nursing preferred)

8–15 years of clinical experience

Minimum 3–5 years in a leadership/administrative role

Experience In Hospital Or Home Healthcare Setup Preferred

*Key Skills*

Leadership & team management

Clinical expertise and patient care standards

Strong communication & interpersonal skills

Knowledge of NABH/JCI accreditation

Problem-solving and decision-making ability

Skills: nursing head,nursing supervisor,senior staff nurse 
Show more Show less

THE OPPORTUNITY
We are a fast-scaling, multi-venture promoter group operating across specialty chemicals, medical devices, biotech, and government innovation partnerships. The Managing Partner oversees multiple entities, a PhD programme at IIT Bombay, global travel, and high-stakes stakeholder relationships spanning industry, government, and academia.
We are looking for an exceptional individual to serve as Chief of Staff, Executive Assistant, and Personal Secretary — a single, high-trust role that combines strategic support, flawless execution, and personal efficiency management. This is not a back-office admin position. This is the right hand of a founder-leader who moves fast, thinks big, and expects excellence.
WHO YOU ARE
A polished, articulate, and exceptionally well-groomed professional who commands presence in any room — from boardrooms to government offices to global conferences.
Sharp, composed, and naturally charismatic with outstanding communication skills in English (Hindi and Marathi are strong advantages).
A multitasker who thrives under pressure — you juggle complex calendars, last-minute travel, investor meetings, and personal errands without breaking a sweat.
Hungry for growth. You see this role not as a job but as an MBA-on-steroids — direct exposure to venture building, deal-making, policy, and innovation.
Willing and eager to travel extensively — domestic and international — sometimes at short notice.
Comfortable with long, irregular hours. You understand that supporting a high-output leader means being available when it matters, not just 9-to-5.
Tech-savvy and digitally fluent: Google Workspace, MS Office, AI tools, and quick to learn any new platform.
Age 23–30. We value energy, ambition, and coachability over years on a resume.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Strategic & Operational Support
Manage the Managing Partner’s calendar, priorities, and daily rhythm across multiple ventures.
Prepare briefs, presentations, MIS dashboards, and talking points for meetings with investors, government officials, and partners.
Track action items across entities, follow up with internal teams (CEOs, CFOs, R&D leads), and ensure nothing falls through the cracks.
Draft and manage correspondence — formal letters, emails, WhatsApp communications — on behalf of the MP.
Travel & Event Management
Plan and coordinate complex domestic and international travel: flights, hotels, visas, itineraries, and ground logistics.
Organise dinners, investor events, and networking engagements with impeccable attention to detail.
Accompany the MP on select trips, conferences, and government meetings as a professional representative.
Personal & Lifestyle Management
Manage household coordination, personal appointments, family logistics, and vendor relationships.
Handle confidential personal and financial matters with absolute discretion.
Networking & Stakeholder Interface
Serve as the first point of contact for external stakeholders — screen calls, manage access, and represent the MP’s office professionally.
Build and maintain a CRM of key relationships: government contacts, investors, industry leaders, academic collaborators.
QUALIFICATIONS
Graduate or Postgraduate (MBA/Mass Communication/Hotel Management/Liberal Arts preferred).
1–4 years of experience in EA/PA/CoS roles, hospitality, consulting, or founder-support functions. Exceptional freshers from premium institutes will be considered.
Outstanding personality, grooming, and interpersonal presence.
Impeccable written and spoken English. Fluency in Hindi; Marathi is a plus.
Valid passport; willingness to travel 30–40% of the time.
WHAT WE OFFER
Direct mentorship and daily exposure to a serial entrepreneur operating across deeptech, policy, and global business.
Rapid career trajectory — top performers move into operational leadership, venture management, or business development roles within 18–24 months.
Compensation: ₹8–15 LPA (negotiable for exceptional candidates) + travel perks + performance bonuses.
A front-row seat to venture building, government partnerships, and global innovation ecosystems.
HOW TO APPLY
Send your CV along with a 2-minute video introduction (on your phone is fine) to careers@conchemlabs.com. In your video, tell us: who you are, why this role excites you, and one thing you’ve done that you’re genuinely proud of.
This role is for someone who wants to be extraordinary. If that’s you, we want to hear from you.

items.jobDescription:
About Us

Stronghold Investment Management ("Stronghold," "SIM," or "the Firm") is a technology-driven, vertically integrated investment manager focused on real-assets and related private markets. Stronghold seeks to deliver best-in-class risk-adjusted returns through an investment framework that features comparatively rapid transaction velocity, deep technical expertise, creative financial structuring, and objective and algorithmic decision making.

Stronghold was founded by Ryan Turner in 2016 and has deployed over $1.6 billion in capital across 10+ investment vehicles.

Ultimately, Stronghold intends to apply its technology-intensive investment approach to a variety of specialist real assets verticals, including:

Oil & Gas
Renewables
Specialty Real Estate (commercial, datacenters, marinas)
Telecommunications and Technology Infrastructure
Billboards and Fixtures
Mining & other commodities
Utilities
Secondaries

Role Overview

This is a hands-on, cross-functional role supporting the Managing Director, India in running the day-to-day operational, financial, compliance, and administrative functions of Stronghold Investment Management's India entity. You'll work across Finance, Tax & Transfer Pricing, HR & Labour Compliance, Legal & Corporate Secretarial, IT & Infrastructure, and General Office Administration. Not as a specialist in each, but as the person who ensures nothing falls through the cracks.

You'll coordinate between the Bangalore operations team and US executive leadership, external advisors, and banking and vendor partners, translating priorities into execution and keeping stakeholders aligned.

We're looking for a self-starter with 4 to 7 years of experience who thrives in ambiguity, takes initiative without waiting to be told, and has a bias for improving how things work. Experience with AI tools, particularly Claude, Claude Code, or Cowork, is expected.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Initiatives: Support the MD in establishing and enhancing India operations. Drive implementation of scalable processes leveraging industry best practices.
Operational Excellence: Manage and improve day-to-day processes across site operations, administration, and business management. Identify bottlenecks and drive continuous improvement.
Governance & Compliance: Coordinate Transfer Pricing compliance, regulatory filings, and Board Meeting logistics. Maintain governance structures and prepare business reports and dashboards for leadership review.
Financial Management: Support the monthly financial reporting cycle, annual budget preparation, and Power BI dashboard maintenance for the India entity. Help implement financial controls for budgetary discipline.
Business Operations & Office Management: Manage Bangalore office facilities and daily operations. Coordinate with external advisors and review vendor contracts for cost-effectiveness.

Required Qualifications

4 to 7 years of experience in business operations, office management, or a similar cross-functional role, preferably in technology, financial services, or fintech.
Demonstrated ability to work independently, manage multiple workstreams, and drive tasks to completion with minimal supervision.
Strong working knowledge across at least two of: finance/accounting, compliance, HR operations, or vendor/office management.
Comfortable working with senior leadership and external stakeholders; strong written and verbal communication skills.
Proficient in reporting and data tools (Excel, Power BI, or similar) with the ability to build dashboards and prepare executive-level summaries.
Experience using AI tools in a professional context, particularly Claude, Claude Code, and Cowork.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience supporting senior executives or working in a chief of staff or business operations capacity.
Exposure to GCC (Global Capability Centre) setup or scaling operations for a US-headquartered company.
Familiarity with Indian regulatory and compliance frameworks (transfer pricing, labour law, corporate secretarial).
Certification in Lean, Six Sigma, or other process improvement methodologies.
MBA or postgraduate degree in Business Administration, Operations, or a related field.
Show more Show less

Who We Are

80% of the workers across the globe are Deskless. These are the people who keep our lights on and gas flowing, build roads and bridges, run our manufacturing factories, ensure that we get healthcare service, and provide us with reliable phone and internet connectivity. As entrepreneurs, have we considered solving their problems and making them more productive?

Zinier is a company on a mission to empower frontline workers - and the people supporting them - to achieve greater things for themselves and the world around them. With the majority of workers worldwide being deskless, Zinier recognizes the need for Technology Equity to improve the lives and productivity of these workers who keep the world up and running.

We have global team headquartered in Silicon Valley, with a hybrid workforce across the United States, Canada, Europe, Latin America, Singapore, and Bangalore, India, with leading investors that include Accel, ICONIQ Capital, Founders Fund, Newfund Capital, NGP Capital, Tiger Global Management, and Qualcomm Ventures LLC.

What We Are Looking For

If you are someone who has a passion for technology and startups, has a natural knack of taking ownership and solving diverse problems, believes in making a difference for the world’s 2.7 B Deskless Workers engaged in field operations, and wants to grow your career in a venture-funded Silicon Valley tech company - you have come to the right place. We would like to meet you.

This role will report to our Global Chief Transformation Officer in Bangalore and work closely with Zinier’s Co-Founder and CEO on some of the most important strategic initiatives across the company.

You will work across the entire business, from product, commercial, technology, services to operational transformation, gaining a front-row seat to how a global B2B PaaS company scales.

If you thrive in fast-paced environments, enjoy solving complex problems, and want to work directly with company leadership on high-impact initiatives, this role offers a rare opportunity to do exactly that.

What The Role Offers

You will partner closely with Global Chief Transformation Officer, our Co-Founder and CEO on enhancing their effectiveness by managing key initiatives, facilitating internal communication, and aligning organizational priorities. Specifically,

Driving Efficiency: Support the development of company-wide metrics, dashboards, and reporting frameworks to drive efficiency. Drive company objectives and tracking KPIs to measure performance. Coordinate cross-team initiatives and ensure key leadership priorities are executed effectively.
Lead Transformational Projects: Lead special projects from concept through execution in a fast-moving startup environment. Work cross-functionally with Product, Sales, Customer Success, and Professional Services teams to drive strategic initiatives forward
Opportunity Analysis: Analyze operational and business data to identify opportunities to improve performance, efficiency, and scalability. Identify operational bottlenecks and recommend solutions that improve execution and team alignment
Decision Making and Communication: Drive efficiency in key leadership meetings through effective prioritization of business and operational imperatives. Support the preparation of strategic presentations, internal reviews, and leadership updates. 

What You’ll Bring To The Role

MBA with 3–7 years of experience in management consulting, business operations, chief of staff or similarly analytical roles. 
Experience working with B2B enterprise software, or high-growth technology companies is strongly preferred. 
Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication and presentation skills. 
Ability to drive clarity with effective communication and trust building.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and drive in a fast-paced environment. 
Ability to understand context and priorities, build key proposals for fast paced decision making.
Ability to facilitate communication across the organization, understanding the soft aspects of the role.
Intellectual curiosity
High ownership mentality, with a bias toward execution
Be Hungry. Be Humble. Be Honest. And Hustle.
Show more Show less

Magma Holding Company is a fast-growing holding company building and scaling businesses in the outsourcing, offshoring, and global services ecosystem. Through Magmadigitalventures.com, we operate and incubate multiple service-led businesses delivering back-office operations, business process outsourcing (BPO), sales support, marketing operations, and end-to-end business solutions to global clients.

We are hiring a high-agency Chief of Staff / Founder’s Office Associate / Operations Manager to work directly with the Founder and leadership team. This is a 360-degree execution role covering hiring, sales, marketing, operations, and strategy. If you are a hustler who thrives in fast-paced environments, takes ownership, and wants to build businesses from 0 to 1 and 1 to 10, this role is for you.

Role Overview
As Chief of Staff / Founder’s Office, you will act as the Founder’s right hand and execution engine across all portfolio businesses. This is a cross-functional leadership role that combines strategy, operations management, talent acquisition, business development, and performance tracking. You will work across multiple verticals and ensure high accountability and strong execution culture across teams.

Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:

Partnering closely with the Founder on company strategy and execution
Driving end-to-end hiring across operations, sales, marketing, and delivery teams
Building recruitment funnels and scaling talent acquisition processes
Supporting and executing sales strategy including lead generation, CRM management, and client outreach
Contributing to business development initiatives and global client acquisition
Planning and executing marketing initiatives including LinkedIn growth, outbound campaigns, content, and partnerships
Creating SOPs, dashboards, and reporting frameworks for operational excellence
Tracking KPIs across revenue, hiring, profitability, and client success
Improving cross-functional coordination and accountability
Identifying inefficiencies and implementing scalable systems and automation
Supporting expansion planning, new vertical launches, and growth initiatives

Ideal Candidate Profile
We are looking for someone who combines structured thinking with strong hustle. You should be comfortable operating in ambiguity while maintaining high ownership and accountability. This role is ideal for someone who wants to transition into a future Business Head, Operations Head, or COO role.

You should have:

2–5 years of experience in Founder’s Office, Chief of Staff, Business Operations, Strategy, Consulting, Startup Operations, Growth, or Business Development roles
Experience in outsourcing, BPO, consulting, agency, or high-growth startups preferred
A strong operator mindset with bias for action
Experience handling hiring, sales pipelines, marketing execution, or operations
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
High ownership, accountability, and execution focus
Comfort working in fast-paced, ambiguous environments
Mumbai-based or willing to relocate (this is an in-person role in Worli)

Why Join Magma Holding Company?
This is not a narrow, siloed job. This is a career-defining opportunity where you will gain direct exposure to business building and scaling multiple service-led companies under one holding structure. You will work closely with leadership and have the autonomy to implement ideas and drive real impact.

What you can expect:
Direct exposure to the Founder and core strategic decisions
End-to-end exposure across hiring, sales, marketing, operations, and strategy
Rapid career progression into Business Head, Operations Head, or COO roles
High learning curve in scaling outsourcing and global services businesses
Entrepreneurial, high-performance culture
Competitive salary with performance-linked incentives

items.jobTitle: Founders Office Generalist — HR & Recruitment Workflows
items.jobDescription:
Our client is a fast-scaling AI + operations company. 1,000+ employees, 2,700+ contractors across 15 countries, hiring at hundreds of people per week — and accelerating.

Two departments are feeling the growth most: HR and Recruitment.
On the recruitment side — sourcing is manual. Screening is ad-hoc. The pipeline from "applicant" to "Day 1" is fragmented across multiple tools with no single owner. Nobody can tell you cost-per-hire, conversion rate at each stage, or where top candidates are dropping off.
On the HR side — contracts go out late. The inbox has thousands of unresolved messages. Payment issues escalate because there's no triage system. Compliance work gets buried under admin. Tribal knowledge holds together processes that should be documented and automated.

Neither department needs more effort. They need someone to install the systems that make the effort count.

The Role
You join the Founders Office — a small, high-agency team that reports directly to the Chief of Staff and embeds in different parts of the business on 2–3 month sprints. Think internal SWAT team. Your first sprint: HR & Recruitment Ops.

Three jobs. That's it.
Install systems that actually work. A recruitment pipeline that tracks 500 hires a week without dropping names. A contract workflow that fires the moment someone passes qualification. An HR inbox that separates real problems from noise so the real ones get solved the same day. An onboarding sequence from signed contract to Day 1 — documented, automated, and breakage-proof. You don't suggest improvements. You build the thing, ship it, and train people on it.
Fix what others can't. The compliance manager is stuck on a policy gap — you unstick him. The recruiter has 40 contracts that should've gone out Friday — you find the bottleneck and remove it. A payment batch failed and 200 contractors are asking where their money is — you don't just answer the emails; you fix the process so it doesn't fail again. When a candidate falls through the cracks, you don't fix the case. You fix the system that let it happen.
Make both departments better at their actual job. HR should be focused on compliance, contractor management, and risk — not chasing spreadsheets. Recruitment should be focused on sourcing and closing — not copying data between five tabs.

Every system you build gives them back hours. Every hour you give back compounds across thousands of people.

Required: AI & Automation Fluency
This is non-negotiable. You must be able to:
Use Claude Pro, Gemini Pro, or equivalent as a daily working tool — as a thinking partner, drafter, analyzer, and code writer, not as a search engine
Build automations in Google Apps Script, Make.com, Zapier, or similar — without waiting for an engineer
"Vibe code" your way through small tools, scripts, and prototypes when no off-the-shelf tool fits
Read enough of a JSON payload, webhook response, or basic API call to debug what's broken
Pick the right tool for the job — manual when manual is faster, automated when volume demands it
If you've never touched a script or a no-code automation, this isn't your role.

You're a Fit If
You've built systems for HR or recruitment — not just filled roles or processed contracts, but built the process underneath
You think in workflows: what triggers what, where things stall, what should be automated vs. what needs a human
You can take a messy, manual process and turn it into something anyone can run without you
You've worked in high-volume environments — hundreds or thousands of contractors, not a team of 20
You ship first, polish later — MVPs over masterplans
You use AI as a force multiplier, not a crutch
You can talk to a compliance manager about policy gaps and a recruiter about pipeline velocity in the same afternoon
You prioritize results over hours logged

You're Not a Fit If
✗ You think recruitment ops means posting jobs and waiting for applications
✗ You think HR ops means answering emails and filing contracts
✗ You need a fully built system handed to you before you can operate
✗ You've never written or read an Apps Script, a Make scenario, or a basic API call
✗ You've never looked at a process and thought "why does anyone do this manually?"
✗ You think AI tools are a fad or "not enterprise-ready"
✗ You've spent your career in stable, enterprise environments where initiatives go through six layers of approval ✗ You believe a 9-to-5 is a feature
✗ You freeze when data is incomplete instead of building with what you have and flagging gaps

Details
Engagement: Full-Time
Location: Remote — core overlap with Manila time (PHT)
Reports to: Chief of Staff
Tools you'll work with: Claude Pro, Gemini Pro, PandaDoc, Make.com, Typeform, Google Workspace (incl. Apps Script), Slack — plus whatever you build

This isn't a role for someone who wants to do recruiting. This isn't a role for someone who wants to do HR.

This is a role for someone who wants to engineer how an organization hires and manages 2,700+ people — and make it look easy.

Apply if you read this and immediately started thinking about where the systems are broken.
Show more Show less

About EXL EXL is the trusted partner for data-driven industries like insurance, banking, healthcare, retail, and logistics. We combine data, advanced analytics, digital technology, and industry expertise to transform businesses and enhance operations. Trusted by top industry leaders, including nine of the top ten U.S. insurance companies and six of the top ten U.S. healthcare payers, we align with your goals, whether leveraging AI or embedding analytics into workflows. Clients rely on EXL for our global talent, rapid digital solutions, and our team of dedicated data scientists. With over 25 years of experience and global pool talent of 55,000+ colleagues we deliver fast, reliable results. Our values pave the way for your success EXL was founded on the core values of innovation, collaboration, excellence, integrity and respect. We have the innate ability to work together with our clients to improve business outcomes, operations, and customer experience. JOB DESCRIPTION Position Title Manager Role Type Individual Contributor Company EXL Service Grade / Level C1 Division / Department Office of Chief of Staff to EVP & Chief Growth & Strategy Office Location India/US Reporting to SAVP, Chief of Staff to EVP & Chief Growth & Strategy Office JOB PURPOSE & SUMMARY The Manager, Chief of Staff – Growth & Strategy Office (CGSO) will collaborate closely with the Chief of Staff (CoS) to support the Chief Growth & Strategy Officer (CGO) and the executive leadership team. This includes driving the execution of strategic initiatives, improving operational efficiency, and fostering alignment across departments. The individual will serve as a key support figure to both the CGSO and Senior Leadership Team, managing strategic projects, communications, and cross-functional collaboration. Additionally, the manager will oversee critical projects and initiatives for the Growth Markets IMU, ensuring seamless execution and alignment with broader organizational objectives. This role requires close collaboration with cross-functional teams to drive strategic outcomes and support the CGSO in achieving key goals for the Growth Markets IMU. The ideal candidate will have strong organizational, analytical, and program management skills, with a background in strategy and business operations. PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTABILITIES

Strategic Project & Program Support Work closely with the Chief of Staff to help manage and track the execution of key growth strategies and initiatives across the organization. Coordinate with internal teams and stakeholders to ensure that strategic projects are completed on time and meet business objectives. Assist in defining and managing timelines, resources, and deliverables for key growth initiatives.
Operational Coordination & Process Improvement Ensure smooth operational processes within the CGSO and Growth Markets IMU by tracking progress against strategic goals and assisting in problem-solving to remove roadblocks. Develop and maintain clear communication and reporting systems to track the status of projects and strategic objectives. Contribute to optimizing internal workflows to increase the team’s efficiency and impact.
Stakeholder Engagement & Communication Collaborate with internal teams, senior leadership, and business units to ensure consistent messaging and alignment on strategic priorities. Prepare presentation materials, meeting agendas, and follow-up action items for the Chief of Staff and CGO. Assist in drafting internal and external communications for the CGO’s office, ensuring they align with the broader strategy and tone of the organization.
Data Analysis & Reporting Conduct secondary research on market trends, competitor activity, and industry benchmarks to support strategic decisions. Analyze key performance metrics related to growth strategies and assist the Chief of Staff in reporting results to senior leadership. Assist in building and maintaining KPIs and dashboards to monitor the success of key strategic projects.
Project Monitoring & Follow-Up Lead the coordination and follow-up of key action items from executive meetings, ensuring accountability and timely execution. Track and report on the progress of strategic initiatives, providing regular updates to the Chief of Staff. Assist in managing any emerging priorities or urgent strategic requests from the CGO’s office or Growth Markets IMU.
Cross-Functional Collaboration Serve as a liaison between the Chief of Staff and other departments to ensure that key initiatives are progressing as planned. Collaborate with business unit leaders, marketing, and operations teams to facilitate cross-functional alignment and to ensure proper execution of projects. Support cross-departmental meetings and initiatives to ensure alignment on the company’s growth agenda. KEY EXPECTATIONS Strong organizational and project management skills to ensure that all initiatives are well-coordinated and executed. Ability to manage competing priorities, work under pressure, and drive projects to completion. Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, with an ability to present complex concepts clearly and concisely. Ability to create and maintain strong working relationships with senior leaders and other key stakeholders. Focus on detail, accuracy, and consistency in all communications and deliverables. SKILLS AND KNOWLEDGE Technical Skills Proficient in MS Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, Word). Comfortable with data analysis and creating reports or dashboards. Familiarity with project management tools (e.g., Asana, Trello, Jira) is good to have, but not mandatory. Soft Skills Strong interpersonal and communication skills to effectively collaborate with senior leaders and internal teams. Analytical and problem-solving mindset to navigate complex projects. Highly organized with the ability to prioritize tasks and manage time effectively. Ability to work under minimal supervision and handle ambiguity with a solution-oriented approach. Competencies Strategic Thinking: Ability to align day-to-day activities with long-term strategic goals. Project Management: Experience in managing complex projects, with an ability to coordinate across functions. Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, particularly in preparing presentations and reports. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Ability to foster collaboration and build relationships across different teams. Attention to Detail: Ensuring accuracy and quality in deliverables, with a keen eye for detail. Educational Qualifications Master’s in business administration, Strategy, or a related field from a top tier B-School Relevant and total years of Experience

Job Description

Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International?

Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It Matter.

You work with functions and business units to develop strategic roadmaps and to plan agreed-to outcomes. In this role, you ensure that commitments are met, and serve as the point of contact for Mondelēz International Digital Services.

How You Will Contribute

You will offer your support on issues and challenges that arise in the processes used in the shared services team and/or with third-party suppliers. In addition, you will support internal and external continuous improvement initiatives and see business projects through to completion. You will also contribute to operational processes by ensuring that key performance indicators and performance management targets are met and by sharing best practices and supporting the super key user community.

What You Will Bring

A desire to drive your future and accelerate your career. You will bring experience and knowledge in:

Shared services
Building and maintaining effective partnerships with internal stakeholders and suppliers
Customer service experience
Communicating, organizational, problem-solving, and analytical and multi-tasking
Operating effectively in a changing environment and using initiative
Using data to make recommendations and decisions
Key performance indicators, managerial reporting, forecasting and budgeting


More About This Role

This role enables MDS’ portfolio of strategic and critical partners to successfully service the MDS organization throughout the E2E engagement cycle.
Provides an ecosystem of relationship management expertise and supporting programs, elevating partner’s performance and executional excellence whilst creating a platform for dialogue and opportunity for both Partner and MDS mutually beneficial outcomes. 
Through implementation of the Partner Excellence Program, the SPM team takes a holistic approach to manage our Strategic & Critical Operational Partners as an eco-system and build strategic partner management CoE through fit-for-purpose initiatives, activities and support
Enable value-based partnerships through partner eco-system approach based on relevance to MDS strategy and objectives and categorization of the Strategic Partner Profile. 
Demonstrated, reliable governance process for Partnership Performance, executional excellence, commercials and compliance. 
SPM as “chief of staff” and trusted relationship driver enabling strong, mutually beneficial relationships across all stakeholders within the Partner eco-system, supporting genuine investment on both sides. 
SPM expertise and advice in de-escalation issues and mediator in problem management, preventing impact on performance, costs, and compliance. 


What you need to know about this position:

Act as the interface support between MDS Strategic Partner Management and Strategic Partner teams for key activities including:
SPOC for commercial activities, collaborate with AP team (Accounts payable), MDLZ stakeholders and Partners for monitoring and resolving commercial issues (PO consumption, pending invoice, PO creation etc.) 
Support the deployment and day to day running of effective partner governance with key partners.
Provide administrative support to strategic partner management governance activities; including document control, maintaining an organised and centralized repository of all governance and commercial records, meeting management, reporting, supporting evidence.
Utilize SharePoint and collaboration tools whilst undertaking tasks.
Effectively support the Strategic Excellence Programs as relates to the Partner Excellence Program (e.g. Partner Excellence Plans, Partner Excellence Maturity Matrix, Partner Health Dashboard).
Learn and align to various SPM tools like Joint Business Planning, Partner Classification, Factsheets, Delivery and Obligation tracking
Support and collaborate with MDS leads, Procurement, Finance as required to enable robust relationship governance.
Support wider team initiatives such as team meetings, town halls etc.


What extra ingredients you will bring:

A desire to drive your future and accelerate your career and the following experiences, skills and knowledge:
Ability to develop effective partnership with internal and external stakeholders to drive Partner Excellence; must be a good team player. 
Structured approach and relevant (C.I. and process) analytic ability in order to identify and structure issues and discuss solutions.
Excellent communication, presentation, training and interpersonal skills.
Creative, flexible, and solution-oriented and results-focused.
Ability to align and prioritise business requirement and devise actionable solutions 
Interest in technology and industry fluency preferred.
At least 2 years' experience in a vendor management environment.
Able to leverage Stakeholder engagement and have the ability to work with colleagues from operations up to executive level with guidance.


Education / Certifications:

University degree in Finance, Business Management, or similar discipline
3 to 4 years business related experience, preferable in a IT, Shared Services, Procurement or BPO environment


Job specific requirements:

Progressive Work Experience in IT and or BPO Outsourced environment in Vendor Mngt, Project Management, Process improvement or other quality programs.
Experience of working in a global business services environment or internal service delivery 
Experience in complex organizations and matrix reporting relationship.
Demonstrated experience in interactions with multiple teams to complete tasks with internal and external partner colleagues
Must possess "soft skills": personal effectiveness, creative problem solving, ability to interact with all levels of organizations and cross culturally.
Proven project management skills, meeting facilitation, written/oral communication and presentation skills. Able to liaise between internal stakeholders and partners
Demonstrate a clear understanding of commercial considerations.
Technical Work Experience (MS Project, PowerPoint, Excel, collaboration tools like SharePoint)


Travel requirements: Minimal

Work schedule: Global

No Relocation support available

Business Unit Summary

At Mondelēz International, our purpose is to empower people to snack right by offering the right snack, for the right moment, made the right way. That means delivering a broad range of delicious, high-quality snacks that nourish life's moments, made with sustainable ingredients and packaging that consumers can feel good about.

We have a rich portfolio of strong brands globally and locally including many household names such as Oreo, belVita and LU biscuits; Cadbury Dairy Milk, Milka and Toblerone chocolate; Sour Patch Kids candy and Trident gum. We are proud to hold the top position globally in biscuits, chocolate and candy and the second top position in gum.

Our 80,000 makers and bakers are located in more than 80 countries and we sell our products in over 150 countries around the world. Our people are energized for growth and critical to us living our purpose and values. We are a diverse community that can make things happen—and happen fast.

Mondelēz International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Job Type

Regular

Relationship Management

Global Business Services

items.jobDescription:
Position Description

Position Title: Library Manager 

Reports To: Chief Business Officer 

Employment Type: Full-time

Position Summary

The Library Manager provides strategic and operational leadership for the UNSW India Campus library, ensuring the delivery of high-quality services, resources, and programs that support academic, research, and community objectives. This role oversees procurement, negotiation, and vendor management, ensuring compliance with Indian and UNSW standards. The Library Manager fosters a culture of innovation, professional development, and stakeholder engagement, and is responsible for building a library service that meets the needs of a diverse student and faculty population in a rapidly evolving higher education environment.

Accountabilities

Develop and implement the library’s strategic plan, aligned with UNSW India’s vision and goals.
Lead the management of library operations, including collections, information services, and user experience.
Oversee procurement processes for library resources, negotiating contracts and managing vendor relationships in compliance with Indian regulations and UNSW policies.
Ensure compliance with local, national, and international standards for library operations and resource management.
Lead the development and implementation of policies, systems, and controls for library services, including digital transformation and access management.
Manage budgets, financial planning, and reporting for library operations and capital projects.
Foster a culture of continuous improvement, innovation, and professional development among library staff.
Engage with academic and professional leaders to support teaching, research, and learning initiatives.
Build and maintain strong relationships with external partners, professional associations, and community stakeholders.
Communicate library insights, performance, and strategic priorities to senior leadership and governance bodies.
Lead risk management, including copyright compliance, data privacy, and information security.
Oversee internal controls, fraud prevention, and compliance monitoring in library operations.
Develop sustainable models for resource acquisition, digital transformation, and community engagement.
Assess and implement commercial ventures or partnerships suited to the library’s mission and values.
Ensure high standards of integrity, judgement, and alignment with UNSW and public sector academic values.

Skill & Experience

Master’s degree in Library and Information Science or equivalent (required).
Professional librarian accreditation (e.g., ALA, ALIA, ILA) preferred.
Training or certification in procurement, negotiation, or project management (desirable).
7+ years of senior library management experience in complex or academic environments, in India or international higher education.
Experience in leadership, team development, and change management.
Proven experience in procurement, contract negotiation, and vendor management.
Demonstrated capability in budget management and operational planning.
Experience working with regulatory frameworks and compliance in library settings.
Deep knowledge of library governance, information management, and digital transformation.
Strong strategic and analytical thinking with ability to manage ambiguity and change.
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and communication skills.
High cultural intelligence and experience working with diverse teams.
Strong integrity, judgement, and alignment to UNSW and public-sector academic values.

Key relationships

Chief Business Officer – for strategic direction, performance oversight and reporting.
Academic Staff – for advice and consultation.
Professional Staff – including student services, admissions, and learning support for student experience and academic administration.
Industry and External Partners – for collaborative projects, guest lectures, and student engagement.
Regulatory Bodies – including UGC and accreditation agencies for compliance and quality assurance.

For any queries related to your application, email at careers@daskalos.com.

items.jobTitle: Business Manager & Chief of Staff, RQA India
items.jobDescription:
About This Role

Business Overview

The Risk & Quantitative Analysis (RQA) group provides independent oversight of BlackRock’s fiduciary and enterprise risks. RQA advances the firm’s risk management practices and strengthens business and investment outcomes through deep risk expertise, data-driven insights, and constructive challenges.

We are seeking a highly organized, analytically strong, and execution-oriented Business Manager & Chief of Staff to support the Head of BlackRock Gurgaon office and the Head of RQA in India. This individual will help drive the operating rhythm, strategic priorities, and day-to-day business management of RQA India while partnering closely with the wider COO and business management infrastructure across BlackRock.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy, governance, talent, communications, finance, and execution. It requires someone who can move seamlessly between leadership presentations, budgeting and reporting, hiring and headcount planning, leadership meeting coordination, event execution, and ad hoc strategic initiatives. This is a high-visibility role with significant exposure to senior leadership across RQA, India leadership, Finance, HR, Recruiting, Communications, Workplace Experience, and partner functions.

What You Will Be Doing

Act as a strategic partner to the Head of BlackRock Gurgaon office and the Head of RQA in India on priorities, operating cadence, decision support, and follow-through on strategic initiatives. 
Help shape and manage the RQA India business agenda, including annual objectives, business reviews, leadership offsites, town halls, site initiatives, and cross-functional coordination. 
Own the management cadence for RQA India leadership meetings, governance forums, committee updates, and working groups; prepare agendas, presentations, briefing notes, minutes, and action tracking. 
Develop high-quality PowerPoint presentations, talking points, dashboards, and written materials for leadership meetings, internal communications, business reviews, site leadership forums, and global stakeholder engagements. 
Support headcount and hiring management for RQA India, including workforce planning, requisition tracking, hiring approvals, onboarding coordination, and reporting on recruiting progress. 
Coordinate with global and India COO / business management teams to ensure alignment on management reporting, governance, business controls, approvals, policies, and operational initiatives. 
Drive execution of site-level and industry-facing initiatives for BlackRock India, the Gurgaon office, and RQA India, including leadership visits, employee engagement activities, functional events, industry engagements, offsites, and internal forums. 
Manage end-to-end event execution for relevant RQA India and Gurgaon office activities, including planning, stakeholder coordination, communications, budget oversight, logistics, post-event follow-up, and lessons learned. 
Support people and culture priorities across RQA India, including onboarding, team engagement, recognition, learning events, and connectivity with the wider India platform. 
Lead or support special projects focused on process improvement, standardization, automation, and operational scale. 
Exercise sound judgment, discretion, and confidentiality in handling sensitive information and senior leadership matters. 

What We Are Looking For

6-10 years of relevant experience in business management, chief of staff, COO, strategy, finance / FP&A, operations, or related roles within asset management, financial services, banking, consulting, or another complex matrixed organization. 
Bachelor’s degree required; MBA or other postgraduate qualification preferred. 
Strong business and financial acumen, with experience in budget management, forecasting, headcount planning, business reviews, and management reporting. 
Advanced Excel skills, including building models, dashboards, scenario analyses, pivot tables, and working with large datasets; Power Query, Power Pivot, Power BI, or Tableau experience is a plus. 
Highly adept with PowerPoint, with the ability to create polished senior-management presentations, distill complex topics into clear narratives, and present data in a concise and compelling format. 
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to communicate clearly, influence across levels, and tailor messages to different stakeholders. 
Strong presentation and storytelling skills; comfortable preparing leaders for meetings and, where appropriate, presenting directly to senior stakeholders. 
Proven ability to coordinate complex workstreams and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, high-visibility environment. 
Strong project management and organizational skills with excellent follow-through and attention to detail. 
Demonstrated ability to build trusted relationships across senior leadership and partner functions, including Finance, HR, Recruiting, Workplace Experience, Communications, and Technology / Operations. 
Experience planning and executing events, offsites, leadership meetings, or industry engagements end-to-end. 
High degree of ownership, judgment, professionalism, and discretion. 
Comfortable working with ambiguity, bringing structure to open-ended problems, and driving execution without extensive oversight. 
Self-starter with energy, resilience, and a positive approach to business challenges; someone who demonstrates emotional ownership and a One BlackRock mindset. 

Preferred Experience

Experience supporting a senior executive, business head, COO team, or country / site leader in a chief of staff or business manager capacity. 
Prior exposure to risk management, quantitative businesses, enterprise risk, control, governance, or related functions. 
Familiarity with recruiting, workflow, procurement, and reporting tools such as Workday, Coupa, ServiceNow, Jira, Excel-based trackers, or similar platforms. 
Experience creating operating dashboards and management packs for global leadership. 
Exposure to vendor management, third-party oversight, or expense approval processes. 
Experience in India site leadership, location strategy, employee engagement, or workplace and campus initiatives is beneficial. 

Why this role matters

This role will help build scale, connectivity, and execution discipline across RQA India while strengthening the link between local priorities, global RQA leadership, and BlackRock’s broader COO and India operating model. It is a strong opportunity to shape the growth, culture, and operating effectiveness of RQA in India while partnering closely with senior leaders across the firm.

Our Benefits

To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.

Our hybrid work model

BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.

About BlackRock

At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.

This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.

For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock | Twitter: @blackrock | LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock

BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, family status, gender identity, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected attributes at law. 
Show more Show less

Based in Noida/Bangalore

Our Company

Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen.

We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours!

Role Overview: 

We are hiring a Lead - Business Operations and Strategy to drive strategic planning, execution, and high-impact initiatives for Adobe’s India Education team. You will work closely with leadership to help shape the division’s direction, ensure alignment across teams, and execute initiatives that improve operational effectiveness, scalability, and overall business performance. A key part of this role is the ability to translate complex business performance into clear, executive-level narratives, enabling leadership to make informed, timely decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Planning & Execution
Partner with leadership to define business priorities and strategic roadmaps. 
Translate strategy into execution plans with clear milestones, owners, and success metrics. 
Drive annual and quarterly planning cycles. 
Track progress and ensure delivery against key business goals. 
Chief of Staff Support to Leadership
Support decision-making through structured problem-solving and insights. 
Ensure follow-through on key initiatives by driving alignment, accountability, and execution. 
C-Suite Reporting & Executive Communication
Own preparation of executive-level reporting and business reviews for senior leadership and C-suite stakeholders. 
Translate business performance into clear, concise, and insight-driven narratives. 
Build high-quality presentations, dashboards, and review materials that drive decision-making. 
Anticipate leadership questions and provide forward-looking insights, not just retrospective updates. 
Strategic Initiatives 
Independently lead high-priority, cross-functional initiatives. 
Drive initiatives from problem definition → solution design → execution → impact tracking. 
Work across Sales, Marketing, Product, Finance, and Partnerships teams. 
Business Operations & Performance Management
Establish and manage operating rhythms:
Business reviews
Performance tracking
Planning cadences
Drive organizational efficiency and execution discipline. 
Ensure visibility and alignment across stakeholders. 
Data, Analytics & Decision Support 
Build and own data frameworks and dashboards for tracking:
Business performance
Key KPIs
Strategic initiatives
Move beyond reporting to:
Identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Generate actionable insights
Partner with data teams (if applicable) or independently drive analytics for decision-making. 
Enable a data-driven culture within the team. 
Financial Planning & Business Insights
Partner with Finance on:
Forecasting and planning
Budget tracking
Evaluate performance of key initiatives and provide ROI-driven recommendations. 
Stakeholder Management & Influence
Manage complex stakeholder environments across functions. 
Influence decisions through:
Structured communication
Data-backed insights
Strong executive presence
Drive alignment across teams without formal authority. 

Who You Are

10+ years of experience in:
Management consulting (preferred)
Business operations/strategy
Chief of Staff/planning roles
Strong analytical skills with the ability to build frameworks, interpret complex data and turn insights into actionable strategies. 
Experience with setting up dashboards to track KPIs and business metrics. 
Strong experience in C-suite reporting. 
Proven ability to independently drive initiatives end-to-end. 
Proficiency in financial modelling, budgeting, and forecasting. 
Experience with project management and leading cross-functional teams to drive

results.

Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, and data visualization tools (e.g.,

Tableau, Power BI).

Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to

influence and collaborate with senior leaders and cross-functional teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to prioritize and manage multiple projects. 

About Adobe

Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.

Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.

Let’s Adobe together

At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture, focus on people, purpose and community, Adobe for All, comprehensive benefits programs, the stories we tell, the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015.

AI Use Guidelines for Interviews:

Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.

At Adobe, we empower employees to innovate with AI — and we look for candidates eager to do the same. As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience.

items.jobTitle: Head of Career Advancement Services and Industry Engagement
items.jobDescription:
Position Description

Position Title: Head of Career Advancement Services and Industry Engagement 

Reports To: Chief Operations Officer Employment Type: Full-time

Position Summary

The Head of Career Advancement Services and Industry Engagement is a senior leadership role responsible for building and sustaining the industry ecosystem that connects UNSW Bengaluru students with meaningful career opportunities at leading organisations worldwide. The role has a clear dual mandate: to forge and grow high-value industry partnerships, and to equip every student with the career readiness, professional networks, and placement outcomes they need to thrive.

Working closely with academic departments, corporate partners, alumni networks, and student support teams, the leader aligns the university’s career services ecosystem with the evolving demands of global industry, ensuring that a UNSW Bengaluru education translates directly into professional success. This is a role for a strategic, entrepreneurial leader who can build at scale, influence at the executive level, and inspire a high-performing team.

Accountabilities

Develop and maintain strategic partnerships with industry, government, and professional bodies.
Coordinate internship programs, work-integrated learning, and graduate placement initiatives.
Represent UNSW Bengaluru in industry forums and networking events to enhance visibility and reputation.
Design and deliver career development programs, workshops, and resources for students.
Monitor and report on graduate outcomes and employability metrics.
Collaborate with academic teams to integrate employability skills into curricula.
Oversee student support services including orientation, counseling, and wellbeing programs.
Ensure timely resolution of student queries and grievances in line with policy.
Develop initiatives to enhance student engagement and campus life.
Ensure adherence to UNSW policies and Indian regulatory requirements for student services and industry programs.
Prepare reports for internal governance and external accreditation bodies.
Manage career services platforms and student engagement systems.
Maintain accurate records of internships, placements, and student support activities.
Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team across career development, industry engagement, and student services functions.
Foster a culture of collaboration, service excellence, and continuous improvement

Skill & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Education, Human Resources, or related field (Master’s preferred).
10+ years of experience in career services, industry engagement, corporate relations, or placements, with at least 3 years in a senior leadership role.
Understanding of higher education frameworks, employability trends, and industry partnership models.
Familiarity with Indian regulatory requirements and global best practices in student services.
Strong relationship-building and stakeholder management skills.
Excellent communication, organizational, and problem-solving abilities.
Ability to lead teams and manage multiple priorities.
Proficiency in career services platforms and student engagement systems.
Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and student success.

Key relationships

Chief Operations Officer – for strategic direction, performance oversight and reporting.
Academic Staff – for advice and consultation.
Professional Staff – including student services, admissions, and learning support for student experience and academic administration.
Industry and External Partners – for recruiter relationships, campus hiring programs, employer engagement events, internships, and placement coordination.
Regulatory Bodies – including UGC and accreditation agencies for compliance and quality assurance.

Emeritus provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.
