items.jobTitle: Chief of Staff to Group Chief Technology Officer
items.jobDescription:
Overview

We’re looking for a passionate Chief of Staff for our Chief Technology Officer & EVP, Products & AI, Nishchay Shah. 

Nishchay wears several hats : P&L and business owner of Paperpal , and the driving force behind the organisation’s product and AI direction. He leads a large team of experts working in product management & marketing, software development, UX, DevOps, Digital innovation, and Machine Learning.

As a Chief of Staff to Nishchay, you will turn strategy into action by connecting the right people and keeping initiatives moving seamlessly. You will thrive in this role if you understand the various drivers & levers of business, enjoy working with people, are a tech enthusiast and have a knack of getting things done. So, if you’re someone who excels at the intersection of strategy and execution, and you get energy from making an entire organization run better, lets chat!

CACTUS is a remote-first organization and we embrace an accelerate from anywhere culture. You may choose to work from home in Mumbai; however, you will be required to travel to our Andheri East office for business requirements, meetings, or company/team events, or on short notice as needed.

Responsibilities

Drive the organization’s operating cadence and ensure effective execution through structured meetings, tracking, and follow-ups. 
Act as the central hub for data, partnering with Finance and BI to track key metrics, budgets, and business health. 
Support leadership with high-quality pre-reads, presentations, and decision-making materials. 
Maintain visibility across initiatives and teams, ensuring clarity on ownership, progress, and priorities. 
Lead OKR cycles end-to-end and drive accountability by unblocking issues and pushing decisions forward. 
Navigate stakeholder dynamics, align cross-functional teams, and act as a bridge between leadership and the organization. 
Own communication and external engagement, including presentations, strategic narratives, partnerships, and special projects. 

Qualifications And Prerequisites

Must-Haves

5+ years of experience in program management, strategy, consulting, or a chief-of-staff-type role in digital product businesses. 
Exceptional project management skills; you live and breathe timelines, dependencies, and deliverables. Tools like Jira, Asana, or Notion are second nature. 
Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills, pivot tables, lookups, data modelling, dashboards. You don’t just use spreadsheets, you think in them. 
Comfort working closely with Finance, you can track budgets, understand P&L basics, and pull together the numbers needed for decisions. 
Outstanding presentation design skills, you can take a messy brief and turn it into a crisp, visually compelling deck in PowerPoint, or any other decks. 
Strong oratory and communication skills, you can hold a room, tell a story, and make complex things simple. 
Proven ability to influence without authority, you get things done through relationships, not reporting lines. 
Relentless follow-through. You don’t just start things, you finish them. 

Good to have

Experience in edtech, SaaS, or academic publishing. 
Familiarity with OKR frameworks and strategic planning methodologies. 
Comfort with data, able to pull insights from dashboards, analytics tools, and raw data sets. 
A curious, exploratory mindset, you’re the kind of person who reads widely, connects dots others miss, and always has a “what if” question ready. 
Prior experience working directly with a CEO, CTO, or senior executive in a high-growth environment. 

Application Process

Before applying, please ensure you meet the role requirements listed above and have legal authorization to work in the country where this role is advertised. Our selection process typically involves an initial screening by a recruiter, a technical assessment, and two to three interview rounds. For this role, you will have a recruiter screening round followed by a technical discussion and HRBP round.

Equal Opportunity

Our hiring practices reflect our commitment to providing equal opportunities and creating an environment where everyone can thrive, develop, and succeed. We celebrate the uniqueness of our team members and prohibit discrimination of any kind, based on race, color, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Accelerating from Anywhere

As a remote-first organization, these are essential attributes we look for in all our candidates.

Taking ownership of your work with minimal supervision, showing strong ability to organize, prioritize and deliver results independently. 
Documenting work that brings everyone on the same page. 
Maturity to choose between synchronous and asynchronous collaboration. 
Effectively collaborating with colleagues across different time zones by setting dedicated hours for collaboration and keeping team members updated through your MS Teams status. 

About CACTUS

Established in 2002, Cactus Communications (cactusglobal.com) is a leading technology company that specializes in expert services and AI-driven products which improve how research gets funded, published, communicated, and discovered. Its flagship brand Editage offers a comprehensive suite of researcher solutions, including expert services and cutting-edge AI products like Mind the Graph, Paperpal, and R Discovery. With offices in Princeton, London, Singapore, Beijing, Shanghai, Seoul, Tokyo, and Mumbai and a global workforce of over 3,000 experts, CACTUS is a pioneer in workplace best practices and has been consistently recognized as a great place to work. 
Show more Show less

Cube is an AI-powered marketing startup founded by alumni of Stanford, Wharton, and IITs, and headquartered in Bengaluru. Backed by Sequoia and other leading investors, we’re building the future of marketing technology.

🌐 Website: https://cubehq.ai

We’re looking for a Chief of Staff to join our Bangalore office. In this role, you’ll work closely with the founders across high-priority initiatives, helping drive execution, strategy, and growth in a fast-paced, high-impact environment.

📌 Application Process
1️⃣ Fill this form (mandatory): https://forms.gle/oKej7DVY3QEHXy4C8
2️⃣ Call with the Hiring Team
3️⃣ Final call with the Founder

🎯 Qualifications
Graduates from Tier-1 institutions (preferably IIT / NIT / IIIT / BITS or equivalent)
Based in Bangalore and open to working from our HSR Layout office
Strong analytical thinking, structured problem-solving, and execution mindset
Clear communicator with the ability to work cross-functionally and handle ambiguity
If you’re excited by ownership, thrive in fast-moving environments, and want a front-row seat to building and scaling a company, we’d love to hear from you.

Location: Bangalore (HSR Layout)
Compensation: As per industry standards
Show more Show less

Role Overview

We are seeking a skilled Chief of Staff who will be pivotal in driving strategic growth initiatives, enhancing operational efficiency within the leadership team, ensuring comprehensive OKR implementation, and assisting the founder with day-to-day operations and strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in management consulting, business development, strategic planning, and operational management.

Key Responsibilities

Collaborate with the Co-Founders and leadership team on company objectives, translating them into actionable workstreams
Develop and communicate strategic road maps, corporate strategies, and business strategies
Identify inefficiencies in existing processes, design improvements, and ensure these are rolled out effectively
Lead or support cross-functional initiatives, setting timelines, milestones, and clear deliverables along with managing the orphan-functions in the organisation
Lead, steer and facilitate the meetings with top management, understand the project planning, retrospective and backlog creation upon the different teams’ priorities, company strategy and vision
Consolidate data from various sources to create dashboards and executive summaries
Highlight trends, surface potential issues, and propose solutions for improvement. In addition, provide the leadership team with clear, actionable insights to inform decision-making.
Support the development of frameworks that reinforce a high-performance culture—e.g., goal-setting, feedback loops, and recognition programs
Tackle urgent or high-priority projects at short notice, using a combination of data analysis, stakeholder input, and creative thinking

Qualifications

6+ years of total experience with a minimum of 3 years of experience in management consulting and 3+ years of experience into business operations, strategy, or a related role
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Engineering, or a related field. MBA or similar experience is a plus
Familiarity with Blockchain/Web3 domain is highly desirable
Exposure to BI Tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) is beneficial
Familiarity with Project Management tools and agile methodologies
Excellent written and verbal skills; able to simplify complex information for diverse audiences.
Proactive in identifying and addressing challenges across the functions in the organisation
Show more Show less

Director – Public Policy & Government Affairs (AI)
Josh Talks AI | India
Location: Delhi/NCR
Reporting to: Founders

About Josh Talks AI
Josh Talks AI is building the foundational layer for real-world voice AI systems, starting with India.
We work at the intersection of:
Large-scale conversational voice datasets
Evaluation systems and benchmarks (Voice of India)
Research and domain-specific AI infrastructure

Our work enables leading AI labs and enterprises to build voice systems that perform reliably across India’s linguistic and real-world diversity.
As AI adoption accelerates, there is a growing need for credible, real-world evaluation and measurement frameworks. Josh Talks AI is building that layer.

About the Role
We are looking for a Director – Public Policy & Government Affairs to help shape how voice AI and multilingual AI systems are understood, evaluated, and adopted across India.

This role sits at the intersection of:
Government engagement
Policy and regulatory ecosystems
Industry partnerships
Research and institutional collaboration

You will work closely with leadership, research, and communications teams to build meaningful partnerships with public institutions and ecosystem stakeholders.

What You Will Do
1. Government & Institutional Partnerships (State-Level)
Build and manage relationships with state governments and public institutions
Work on state-level collaborations around Voice of India (VOI), our benchmark for measuring model performance, including language-specific or state-focused reports
Support the planning of state-level launches, discussions, and knowledge-sharing forums
Engage with relevant stakeholders (e.g., IT departments, digital missions, language bodies) to drive awareness and adoption of evaluation frameworks

2. Strategic Central Government Engagement
Build relationships with key central government bodies such as:
Ministry of Electronics & IT (MeitY)
India AI Mission/ Digital India/ IndiaAI ecosystem
Other relevant national initiatives
Support partnerships that align VOI and related work with national priorities around AI, language technology, and digital inclusion
Contribute to policy conversations on:
Multilingual AI
Evaluation standards
Responsible and inclusive AI deployment

3. Strategic Policy Engagement
Track and engage with developments in AI, data, and digital policy
Represent Josh Talks AI in relevant forums, consultations, and policy discussions
Help translate ecosystem learnings into constructive inputs for policy and institutional stakeholders

4. Ecosystem & Industry Partnerships
Develop partnerships across sectors such as:
Financial services
Healthcare
Customer experience / BPO
Support the development of domain-specific initiatives and benchmarks in collaboration with industry and institutional partners

5. External Stakeholder Engagement
Build relationships with:
Academic institutions and researchers
Think tanks and policy organizations
Civil society groups working on language, accessibility, and inclusion
Facilitate collaborative discussions, roundtables, and joint initiatives

6. Cross-Functional Collaboration
Work closely with:
Communications team (external narratives and launches)
Research team (credibility and technical alignment)
Business team (partnership opportunities)

7. Program & Partnership Execution
Support the planning and execution of:
State-level engagements and VOI-related initiatives
Institutional partnerships and collaborations
Multi-stakeholder discussions and events
Help build a structured, long-term approach to engagement across government and policy stakeholders

What We’re Looking For
10–15 years of experience in:
Public policy
Government relations
Regulatory or ecosystem roles in tech, digital infrastructure, or adjacent sectors
Strong understanding of:
India’s policy landscape
Government functioning at state and central levels
Experience working with:
Ministries, public institutions, or policy bodies
Multi-stakeholder environments

Preferred Qualities
Ability to translate complex technical ideas into accessible policy narratives
Strong relationship-building and stakeholder management skills
Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments
Interest in AI, language technology, and digital inclusion

Why This Role Matters
Voice is becoming a primary interface for how people interact with technology especially in a country as linguistically diverse as India.
This role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of AI, policy, and real-world impact, helping shape how these systems are built and evaluated for millions of users.
Show more Show less

About OML

Only Much Louder Entertainment is a market-leading, full-service creative and entertainment business network that has instigated pop culture for over two decades by creating iconic moments across television, digital content, fan experiences, branded entertainment, and in the artist-creator community. Celebrated for creating India's first multi-genre music festival, the NH7 Weekender, Only Much Louder has built India’s independent music and comedy scene from the ground up. We won the first-ever Branded Content and Entertainment Cannes Lion for ‘Dewarists’ and have been the first homegrown content business to have a strong footprint in over 22 countries.

Our Businesses Include

Move Over: OML’s creative agency on a mission to bury the boring. Turning ads into entertainment people actually want to watch, share, and talk about. We help brands act like creators and build fandoms, not just followings.
Hypothesis: One of India’s fastest growing creator-first & influencer marketing agencies, specializing in data-driven solutions to connect brands with the right audiences
OML Talent: OML’s legacy business representing over 90+ of the most influential creators, comedians, writers, and storytellers shaping India’s entertainment landscape.
OML Studios: India’s premier production house developing original IPs for leading OTT platforms. Also helps brands conceptualise and produce original IPs, manage syndication and licensing.
EighteenSixtyTwo: Bacardi’s offsite content and advertising agency, with campaigns in 20+ countries, delivering top-tier creative and content marketing solutions.
OML Sports: A creative content studio crafting high-impact ads, social media campaigns, and sportainment content for leading teams and leagues. We bring sports stories to life with innovative formats and audience-first storytelling.

About The Role

As a Founder’s Office Intern, you will get a front-row seat during a critical planning phase. This is a high-intensity, short-term role designed for someone who wants to see how a business is built from the inside out. You won't be getting coffee; you’ll be getting data, building frameworks, and helping us decide where the company goes next.

Key Responsibilities

Embark on a journey through our company goals to translate them into practical action plans and trackable progress.
Navigate the world of senior leadership by participating in key internal meetings and preparing strategic documents as an extension of the CEO’s office.
Master the art of identifying risks and bottlenecks early, helping the team solve them to maintain business momentum.
Unleash your creativity by preparing pitch-perfect presentations and notes that bring clarity to senior leadership.
Dive deep into commercial intelligence by building weekly reports across sales, pipeline, and revenue to support sharper decision-making.
Collaborate seamlessly with cross-functional teams spanning sales, finance, and product to deliver outcomes quickly.
Transform ambiguous or fast-moving situations into structured solutions that push the boundaries of our efficiency.
Keep your finger on the pulse of new business ideas and growth opportunities within the creator and media landscape.

What We Are Looking For

You are currently pursuing or recently completed a degree from a Tier 1 or Tier 2 Business School with a consistently strong academic record. 
You have an ownership mindset and don’t wait to be told what to do. 
You are comfortable with numbers, business plans, and commercial thinking. 
You are AI-native and always looking for smarter, more efficient ways to work. 
You believe "it’s not my job" isn't part of the vocabulary and are ready to hustle 
Previous internship experience within a startup ecosystem or a Founder’s Office is a significant plus. 

If you're ready to embark on an exhilarating journey filled with creativity, innovation, and endless possibilities, then we want to hear from you!

Our Values

We build opportunities for our artists, brands and fans; and it’s important for us to be reliable and efficient 
We demonstrate honesty, excellence and commitment to success 
‘It’s not my job’ is not part of our vocabulary, we even stuff envelopes 
We make mistakes, but only to learn from them to build resilience 
We take care of ourselves and our colleagues 
We are our own managers 
We are committed to equality and inclusion 
We are mindful of the strain on the Earth's resources and are committed to reversing the trend with our behaviors where possible 

Thank you!

Thank you for taking the time to go through the opportunity we have to offer.

If you apply, please do not share your photo, age, gender, religion or ethnicity in your resume. You do not even need to share your last name. (We want to eliminate biases on any of these parameters while we decide on our shortlisted candidates)

We will get in touch with you if your profile gets shortlisted. 
Show more Show less

This role is for one of the Weekday's clients

Salary range: Rs 6000000 - Rs 7800000 (ie INR 60-78 LPA)

Min Experience: 8 years

Location: Mumbai

JobType: full-time

We are seeking a highly strategic and execution-focused Chief of Staff to work closely with senior leadership in driving business priorities across corporate finance, digital transformation, and growth initiatives. This role sits at the intersection of strategy and execution, acting as a force multiplier for leadership by ensuring alignment, speed, and impact across key business functions. You will play a critical role in shaping organizational priorities, driving high-impact projects, and enabling data-driven decision-making. The position requires a blend of financial acumen, operational rigor, and strategic thinking, along with the ability to navigate complex business environments and influence stakeholders across levels. Based in Mumbai, this is a high-visibility role ideal for someone looking to work closely with leadership in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

Partner closely with senior leadership to drive strategic initiatives across corporate finance, digital transformation, and business growth
Support financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance tracking to ensure alignment with business goals
Lead and manage cross-functional projects, ensuring timely execution, stakeholder alignment, and measurable outcomes
Drive digital transformation initiatives by identifying opportunities to improve processes, systems, and overall operational efficiency
Translate business priorities into actionable plans, tracking progress through structured dashboards, KPIs, and MIS reporting
Act as a central coordination point across departments, ensuring seamless communication and execution of key initiatives
Conduct financial and business analysis to support decision-making, investment planning, and growth strategies
Prepare executive-level presentations, reports, and insights for leadership and board discussions
Identify risks, inefficiencies, and improvement opportunities, and drive corrective actions proactively
Support organizational scaling by building processes, frameworks, and governance mechanisms that enable sustainable growth

What Makes You a Great Fit

8+ years of experience in strategy, consulting, corporate finance, or business operations roles
Strong expertise in corporate finance, including financial planning, analysis, and business performance management
Proven experience driving or supporting digital transformation initiatives within organizations
Demonstrated ability to manage high-impact projects and work closely with senior leadership
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across functions
High ownership, structured thinking, and ability to operate effectively in fast-paced, ambiguous environments
Experience in high-growth or consulting-driven environments is highly preferred
A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with a strong focus on execution and business impact
Show more Show less

WHO ARE WE?
We are a bunch of super enthusiastic, passionate, and highly driven people, working to achieve a common goal! We believe that work and the workplace should be joyful and always buzzing with energy!

CloudSEK, one of India’s most trusted Cyber security product companies, is on a mission to build the world’s fastest and most reliable AI technology that identifies and resolves digital threats in real-time. The central proposition is leveraging Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning to create a quick and reliable analysis and alert system that provides rapid detection across multiple internet sources, precise threat analysis, and prompt resolution with minimal human intervention.

Founded in 2015, headquartered at Singapore, we are proud to say that we’ve grown at a frenetic pace and have been able to achieve some accolades along the way, including:

CloudSEK’s Product Suite:
CloudSEK XVigil constantly maps a customer’s digital assets, identifies threats and enriches them with cyber intelligence, and then provides workflows to manage and remediate all identified threats including takedown support.
A powerful Attack Surface Monitoring tool that gives visibility and intelligence on customers’ attack surfaces. CloudSEK's BeVigil uses a combination of Mobile, Web, Network and Encryption Scanners to map and protect known and unknown assets.
CloudSEK’s Contextual AI SVigil identifies software supply chain risks by monitoring Software, Cloud Services, and third-party dependencies.

Key Milestones:
2016: Launched our first product.
2018: Secured Pre-series A funding.
2019: Expanded operations to India, Southeast Asia, and the Americas.
2020: Won the NASSCOM-DSCI Excellence Award for Security Product Company of the Year.
2021: Raised $7M in Series A funding led by MassMutual Ventures.
Awards & Recognition: Won NetApp Excellerator's "Best Growth Strategy Award," CloudSEK XVigil joined NVIDIA Inception Program, and won the NASSCOM Emerge 50 Cybersecurity Award.
2025: Secured $19 million in funding led by Tenacity Ventures, Commvault.

About the Role: 
We’re looking for a high-agency operator to work directly with the Founder and Chief of Staff on high-impact GTM and strategic initiatives.
This role sits at the intersection of strategy and execution, ideal for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments.
You’ll collaborate with leaders across Sales, Marketing, and Product to drive market expansion, sharpen cybersecurity positioning, and accelerate revenue growth.
Key Responsibilities
Drive founder’s office initiatives across GTM strategy, sales acceleration, and global expansion.
Lead strategic partnerships and ecosystem programs to strengthen market presence
Support fundraising and board discussions with analytical insights and GTM metrics
Identify and assess whitespace opportunities across cybersecurity domains for potential market entry.
Program-manage cross-functional growth projects end-to-end

Ideal Profile
4 - 6 years of experience in GTM strategy, consulting, or founder’s office roles, with some exposure to cybersecurity
Strong analytical and business acumen, with the ability to translate strategy into actionable outcomes
Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills.
Self-starter comfortable with ambiguity, velocity, and ownership.

Benefits of Joining CloudSEK
We provide an environment where you can develop and enhance your skills while delivering meaningful work that matters. You’ll be rewarded a competitive salary as well as a full spectrum of generous perks and incentives which include:
Flexible working hours.
Food, unlimited snacks and drinks are all available while at office.

And, the finest part is yet to come! Every now and then we ensure to unwind and have a good time together, which involves games, fun, and soulful music. Feel free to show off your artistic side here!

CloudSEK is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

items.jobDescription:
Chief of Staff Location –Ahmedabad 
Reports to – CEO 

About Group Bayport Group Bayport, headquartered in Atlanta (USA), is a rapidly growing global e-Commerce B2B2C, B2B and B2C organization that has re-defined the business of delivering high-quality custom products through a unique blend of cutting-edge digital technologies, robust manufacturing capabilities and global supply chain. An industry leader in print technology, Group Bayport operates in the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, UK, and India through its family of brands – BannerBuzz, Covers & All, Vivyx Printing, Circle One, Giant Media, Neon Earth, Optamark and Northcape. While our advanced technology and customer focus are certainly our salient attributes, our true strength comes from our team of 1500+ people. Having scaled to USD 150mn in 11 years, we believe we are at the cusp of exponential and disruptive growth ahead. For more about Group Bayport, please visit www.groupbayport.com Job Overview The Chief of Staff (Manufacturing & Operational Excellence) will partner closely with the CEO to provide strategic oversight and insight into Bayport’s manufacturing and supply chain functions. The role requires a deep understanding of manufacturing and quality excellence, with the ability to identify opportunities, anticipate risks, and guide improvements that strengthen efficiency, cost effectiveness, and sustainable profitability across a global, multi-plant footprint.

Key Responsibilities 
• Partner with the CEO to review manufacturing and supply chain performance, surfacing insights, risks, and improvement opportunities.
• Apply Operational Excellence frameworks (Lean Six Sigma, TQM, TPM) to assess processes and recommend enhancements in efficiency, quality, and cost. 
• Engage with plant and functional leadership to challenge assumptions, validate plans, and shape improvement priorities aligned with business objectives.
• Lead focused reviews and diagnostic projects on key areas such as quality, productivity, cost optimization, or supply chain effectiveness. 
• Track and analyze KPIs at an executive level, highlighting trends and ensuring leadership has the visibility to act decisively. 
• Provide benchmarking and external best practices to help Bayport’s manufacturing operations stay competitive and future-ready.
• Facilitate cross-functional problem-solving when critical issues span manufacturing, supply chain, and commercial teams.
• Represent the CEO’s office in internal and external engagements, reinforcing Bayport’s commitment to manufacturing excellence and customer-first quality.

Qualifications 
• 6–10 years of experience in manufacturing, quality, or operational excellence, ideally with exposure to multi-plant or global operations.
• Engineering degree (Mechanical, Industrial, or related) from a reputed institute (IITs/NITs) and/or MBA from Tier 1 Institutes like IIM Mumbai. 
• Recognized expertise in Lean, Six Sigma (Black Belt preferred), TQM, TPM, or Business Excellence frameworks. 
• Demonstrated track record in process improvement, cost optimization, and quality transformation initiatives. 
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex operations into actionable insights.
• Excellent communication and executive presence, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and foster collaboration across geographies.
• Familiarity with e-commerce-driven manufacturing or supply chain models is an advantage.

Why Join Group Bayport? 
• Work directly with the CEO on strategic priorities shaping the next phase of Bayport’s growth
. • Influence manufacturing and supply chain excellence across six countries and a diverse portfolio of 10,000+ SKUs. 
• Opportunity to drive meaningful improvements in quality, efficiency, and profitability without being confined to routine operations.
• Collaborate with senior leaders in a dynamic, high-growth, multi-country business. 
• Build a career path that combines deep functional expertise with strategic exposure at the highest levels of leadership. Group Bayport is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all employees.
Show more Show less

About the Company - At Aeroprime Group, we’ve experienced a strong period of growth as the direct result of increased efficiency and productivity. To continue this trajectory, we’re searching for a highly qualified chief of staff to work closely with our executive team. From Day 1, this person will have an immediate impact on our productivity by streamlining strategic initiatives, overseeing program management, and communicating objectives to departments. The ideal candidate will have proven success in a business management role, with a special focus on executive-level advising and interdepartmental collaboration.

About the Role - Objectives of this role:

Oversee strategic business initiatives, from development through successful execution, under the guidance of senior leadership and department heads.
Assist and communicate with executives in decision-making, program management, and initiative implementation.
Review, design, and execute improvements to organizational structure; find knowledge and skills gaps and help address them.
Improve current processes and optimize organizational procedures for efficiency and productivity.

Responsibilities -

Serve as liaison with staff, executives, senior leaders, and Directors regarding company climate, employee well-being, project updates, proposals, and planning.
Oversee daily operations in collaboration with senior managers and department leaders.
Develop and build on relationships with all employees and clients for increased efficiency and responsiveness of existing operations, and help define new operational strategies by working with Directors, and other executives on special projects.
Serve as a subject-matter expert, handling inquiries, developing action plans, and assisting with preparation and dissemination of communications.

Qualifications -

Five or more years of experience in a business management or executive role.
Experience in organizing and directing multiple teams and departments.
Experience in planning and leading strategic initiatives.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Versatile abilities and extreme dedication to efficient productivity.

Required Skills -

Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in business administration or similar field.
Experience in data analysis and management.
Consulting experience, with a focus on operations management.
Proven success in a project coordination role.
Nimble business mind, focused on developing creative solutions.
Strong project-reporting skills, with focus on interdepartmental communications.

items.sector: Airlines and Aviation and Business Consulting and Services

items.jobDescription:
This is not a standard “Operations” or "Executive Assistant to a Founder" or role, nor is it a strategic "think tank" role. It is an execution role.

Our client is an AI-powered services company scaling from 700 to 1,000+ people. We serve unicorn fintechs and data providers used by the Mag-7. The stakes are high, the pace is unreasonable for most, and the standards are binary: It is done, or it is not.

We are looking for a Force Multiplier. Your job is to take the intent of the CEO and Chief of Staff and convert it into operational reality.

THE TRADE-OFF
What you give: Your full focus, 6 days a week. Your ego (you will be corrected often). Your comfort zone (you will build systems for problems you’ve never seen before.)
What you get: A PhD in Scaling. You will sit in executive meetings, be involved in negotiations with enterprise clients, and see exactly how a $100M company is built from the inside. You will grow more in 12 months here than in 5 years at a "ordinary" corporate job.

THE SCOPE OF SERVICES (What you will actually do)
1. The Gatekeeper (20%)
Calendar Architecture: You do not just "schedule meetings," or “manage the calendar.” You defend the CEO’s time asset. You ruthlessly decline, reschedule, and shorten meetings to prioritize revenue-generating activities.
Inbox Triage: You process communication at the executive level. You draft responses that require zero editing. If the CEO has to correct your grammar or tone, you have failed.
2. The Operator (80%)
System Construction: You will not just "manage" projects; you will build the machine. If the CEO says "Fix Recruitment," you build the flow, the dashboard, and the reporting cadence.
Meeting ROI: You own the agenda and the follow-up. You ensure no meeting ends without distinct owners, deadlines, and deliverables.
Shadow & Synthesis: You sit in high-level discussions. Your output is not "notes"; it is a synthesized Action Plan. You translate "Executive Vision" into "Staff Orders."

THE DNA (Who survives here)
High Speed, Low Drag: You figure things out. You do not ask "How do I do this?"; you ask "I have three solutions, which one do you prefer?"
Data over Feelings: You are comfortable with direct feedback. You do not take correction personally; you take it professionally and fix the system.
Tech-Native: You use AI to speed up your workflow. You are faster than your peers because you leverage tools, not just effort.
Discretion is Currency: You will see sensitive data. A single breach of trust is a single-strike exit.

THE PROFILE 
Experience: You might be a burned-out auditor/lawyer seeking impact, a former founder, or a "Star Player" in a slow company who is bored.
Intelligence: You are undeniably the smartest person in your current friend group
Hunger: You are in a season of life where work is your primary focus.
COMPENSATION
Base: Six-Figure PHP Monthly.
Performance: Bonuses for hitting operational milestones
Growth: This is a track to Senior Leadership. If you survive 2 years, you will have the skills to run a department.
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: COO, VP / Execution Operator - Operatif Index
items.jobDescription:
COO, VP / Execution Operator - Operatif Index

About Us
Operatif is a curated network connecting founders, boards, and institutional investors with operators who have built, scaled, and managed businesses through complexity.
The platform replaces resumes with execution data, profiling leaders based on measurable outcomes across revenue, EBITDA, capital deployment, and scale.

The Mandate
This is not a job opening.
This is an Index Call for Operatif’s Execution Operator Bench, used by Private Equity and Growth Equity funds to identify operators who can step into portfolio companies and drive outcomes.
We are building a high-fidelity layer of COO-level execution leaders calibrated for $50M–$1B+ environments, where value is created through systems, scale, and disciplined execution.

The Operator We Are Indexing
We index execution leaders operating at COO-level scope, including:
COOs & Business Heads with full ownership of execution, revenue, and cost structures
P&L Leaders / Country Heads who have scaled markets or business units
High-ownership Operators (VP / Director / Head level) who have already been running functions like a COO, and are ready for full mandates
This is not about title.
This is about ownership, scale, and outcomes.

What We Do NOT Index
Operatif is not a network for:
Chief of Staff or coordination roles
Program / operations managers without P&L ownership
Functional leaders without measurable business impact
Advisory-only profiles without recent execution exposure
If you have not owned a number and changed it materially, this is not the right platform.

The Admission Standard (Hard Signals Only)
Operatif replaces narratives with execution proof.
To be indexed, you must demonstrate verifiable signals:
Revenue Scale: Built or scaled revenue meaningfully (0→1, 1→10, or expansion)
EBITDA Impact: Delivered margin expansion, cost restructuring, or efficiency gains
Capital Calibration: Deployed or optimized capital in growth or constrained environments
Operating Systems: Built repeatable systems across GTM, supply chain, or org design
Strategic Execution: Led integrations, restructures, or value capture initiatives

How We Evaluate Operators
We don’t evaluate titles.
We evaluate:
What you owned
What you changed
What can be validated
Your Operatif Tear Sheet reflects this through execution KPIs and institutional confidence layers.

Why Join the Operatif Index?
Direct Visibility: Your execution profile is surfaced to PE/VC partners for COO, Board, and strategic mandates
Signal over Narrative: Structured Tear Sheets replace resumes
Deployment Fit: AI-driven mapping aligns your execution archetype with the right capital situations

How to Apply for Indexing
Operatif is a closed, invite-only system.
"Request access" → "I'm an Operator" at operatif.io

Operatif is the intelligence layer for execution.
If you’ve built systems, scaled businesses, and driven outcomes, you belong here.
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: Deputy General Manager - Customer Interaction Suite
items.jobDescription:
About The Company

Tata Communications Redefines Connectivity with Innovation and IntelligenceDriving the next level of intelligence powered by Cloud, Mobility, Internet of Things, Collaboration, Security, Media services and Network services, we at Tata Communications are envisaging a New World of Communications

JOB DESCRIPTION – Chief Of Staff, Customer Interaction Suite (CIS)

Date: 28 Feb 2026

Organization: Tata Communications

About Tata Communications

Tata Communications is a global digital ecosystem enabler, present in over 200 countries and territories, serving more than 7,000 customers worldwide including 300+ Fortune 500 companies. The company connects 4 out of 5 mobile subscribers globally and operates one of the world’s most advanced wholly owned subsea fiber networks carrying around 30% of the world’s internet routes. Its capabilities span cloud, security, IoT, mobility, media, and next‑generation connectivity services.

Role Summary

Serve as the strategic operator and trusted counsel to the Function Head – Customer Interaction Suite (CIS). Orchestrate the cross‑functional operating rhythm and translate strategy into measurable execution across CIS’s core pillars: CPaaS, CCaaS, and Platform — with AI at the heart of everything we build and scale.

Reporting

Reports to: Function Head – Customer Interaction Suite

Strategic Context (external signals to inform the role)

CPaaS demand is shifting toward rich, secure, and conversational experiences; CCaaS leaders are embedding agentic AI; platform thinking (data, orchestration, governance) underpins sustainable differentiation. 
Regulatory compliance (opt‑in, routing, data protection) and fraud mitigation materially impact deliverability and margins. 
New channels like Network APIs (e.g., verification, QoD) are leveraged as an additional channel to elevate trust, personalization, and performance across CIS offers. Key Outcomes (12–24 months)
Strategy to execution: OKR cadence that delivers quarterly progress on CIS growth priorities; measurable improvements in gross margin mix and cash conversion. 
Market‑in operating model: leadership receives timely, decision‑ready intelligence that informs portfolio bets across CPaaS, CCaaS, and Platform. 
Operating excellence: predictable delivery against milestones; board‑grade narratives, dashboards, and actioned risks. Core Responsibilities
External Intelligence & Insighting
Continuously scan the market across CPaaS, CCaaS, Platform (AI‑first), and relevant channels to identify trends, opportunities, and risks. 
Synthesize competitive, regulatory, pricing, and ecosystem signals into concise decision briefs with clear “so‑what” and recommended actions. 
Partner with Product, GTM, Finance, and Legal to translate insights into portfolio moves (accelerate, pivot, pause) and quarterly plans. 
Prepare leadership updates and board‑grade summaries; maintain an evidence repository and source transparency.
Strategy, Planning & Execution Governance
Translate CIS strategy into quarterly OKRs and program charters across CPaaS, CCaaS, and Platform; chair weekly execution forums; enforce accountability. 
Land cross‑functional initiatives (e.g., rich‑channel scale‑up, agentic AI enablement, platform data/insight workflows); remove roadblocks and align dependencies. 
Define and track KPIs/benefits; manage risks, decisions, and change across functions and regions.
Board & Leadership Deliverables
Quarterly CIS Strategy & Outcomes deck: external pulse, portfolio status, financial impact, and next‑quarter bets. 
Steward MoMs, action trackers, and risk registers; always uphold confidentiality and precision.
Financial & Performance Stewardship
Partner with Finance to model revenue/margin/cash under multiple channel and pricing scenarios; track ROCE and deleveraging steps. 
Define SLAs (quality, deliverability, compliance) and program KPIs (scope, schedule, benefits) to enable objective reviews.
Operating Rhythm & Communication
Own the leadership calendar (QBRs/MBRs/operating reviews); ensure agenda discipline and decision closure. 
Draft high‑impact communications (reports, emails, decks) with structured clarity and executive tone; maintain stakeholder trust. Candidate Profile

Essential Attributes

Structured strategic thinker with exceptional problem solving; bias for action and ownership. 
Ability to turn ambiguous goals into execution plans across functions and geographies. 
Financial acumen (valuation basics, ROCE drivers, margin levers) and metrical rigor. 
Operational excellence under pressure; impeccable integrity and confidentiality. 
Fluency with AI concepts in CPaaS/CCaaS/Platform contexts; understanding of Network APIs as a channel/capability rather than a core pillar. Desirable Attributes
Board‑caliber storytelling; ability to simplify complexity for senior stakeholders. 
Track record in data‑driven change, metrics creation, and insight‑to‑action cycles. 
Change leadership across conflicting priorities, deft conflict resolution. Technical & Tools Proficiency
Microsoft Word: advanced document structuring, track changes, style guides, templates, cross‑referencing; creation of board‑ready briefs. 
Microsoft PowerPoint: executive storytelling, data‑driven visuals, master templates, animations used sparingly for clarity; version control for steering packs. 
Microsoft Excel: data modeling, pivot tables, lookups/index‑match, scenario analysis, charts, and basic financial modeling (unit economics, sensitivity tables). 
Collaboration & Productivity: Microsoft Teams, SharePoint/OneDrive governance, Planner/Project or equivalent for RAID/action tracking. 
Analytics & Visualization (plus): Power BI or equivalent for dashboards; ability to specify metrics and work with analysts/engineering for data pipelines. 
Documentation & Compliance: clear records management, audit‑ready logs for decisions/consents, and source traceability for external intel. Qualifications & Experience
B.Tech and MBA preferred. 
5–8 years post‑MBA with demonstrable experience in strategy/ops/PMO or product/GM roles in adjacent domains (CPaaS/CCaaS/telco/cloud). Place of Posting

Chennai / Bangalore / Mumbai

Future Career Path

Head/Director of Product Management, Group Product Manager, Principal PM. 
Strategy & Operations leader or BU Chief of Staff with P&L scope.
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: Director – Head of CIB Consulting & Transformation (CIBC&T) India
items.jobDescription:
Job Title: Director – Head of CIB Consulting & Transformation (CIBC&T) India

Department: CIB Consulting & Transformation (CIBC&T) India

About Business Line/Function

Created in 2009, BNP Paribas CIB Consulting & Transformation (CIBC&T) is an in-house management consultancy with a strong presence in EMEA (~100 consultants in Paris, Lisbon, and London) and a global reach thanks to an integrated network in APAC & Americas (~100 consultants).

The team works on strategic initiatives defined by CIB Senior Management. It has a strong track record with 600+ projects accomplished to date. CIBC&T is a competitive alternative to leading, external consulting firms, covering the full consulting value chain: from framing, diagnosis, and delivery of actionable recommendations, to designing effective implementation and enabling change.

BNP Paribas CIB Consulting & Transformation (CIBC&T) India is a newly created team, globally part of CIB Consulting & Transformation based in Paris and locally linked to the business management scope of BNP Paribas’ center of expertise India Solutions Private Limited (ISPL). Its goal is to advise BNP Paribas’ Management, participating in the framing, design and implementation of high-impact projects and operating models transformations aimed at supporting global Functions & CIB Business lines and notably the development of the local territory and (global) center of expertise (India Solutions Private Limited – ISPL).

Position Purpose

As a senior member of the CIBC&T Extended Leadership Team, the Head of CIBC&T India will manage the India based CIBC&T team and bring top-notch consulting with a dual mandate: by leading complex consulting missions locally for the Indian territory and center of expertise India Solutions Private Limited (ISPL) and by contributing, when appropriate, to missions for BNP Paribas CIB for APAC regions or more globally with CIBC&T EMEA.

Heading a newly created team, the Head of CIBC&T India will play a key role in the build-up of the team and progressively establishing the local CIBC&T franchise.

Functionally reporting to the Head of CIBC&T EMEA, the Head of CIBC&T India will work closely with the CIBC&T EMEA Extended Leadership Team to build up the team in India and establish the franchise locally with clients and partners and globally amongst CIB stakeholders. He/She will also work in close collaboration with CIB Consulting APAC and CIB Transformation APAC. Some travel might be required on an ad-hoc basis.

Responsibilities

CIB Consulting & Transformation (CIBC&T) India team management:

Build up CIBC&T India team, supporting the hire and integration of talents into the team.
Inspire, manage, train on-the-job and act as a Manager Coach for CIBC&T India team members.
Build the infrastructure and ensure alignment with CIBC&T EMEA processes & governance (recruiting, trainings, appraisal process, project feedback, etc.).
Manage India budget in coordination with CIBC&T EMEA COO and ensure alignment of cost allocation with the project activities of the team leveraging on appropriate processes (e.g., billing / invoicing as relevant).
Rely on CIBC&T India team members to appropriately deputise on his/her behalf and generally support his/her accountabilities.
Engage in collaboration with and promote adequate visibility on the team’s activities to the Head of CIBC&T EMEA and ISPL Chief of staff.

Share Expertise On Consulting Skills & Methodologies

Be recognized as a reference point for consulting skills and methodologies, and act as a structuring pillar of the team.
Share consulting expertise with team members and provide feedback & coaching on a regular basis.
Encourage a culture of excellence and best practice sharing among the team.

Deliver Impactful Content

Lead or supervise several complex consulting missions in parallel, bringing value and adapting the approach and recommendations- leveraging on his/her knowledge of consulting and the business.
Ensure the high quality, timely and smooth execution of work.
Connecting the dots with other projects locally and across the CIB C&T network and previous experiences, proposing solutions to clients and teams, and securing high quality deliverables with actionable recommendations to Senior Management.
Frame new requests", leveraging deep consulting & transformation skills, CIB knowledge and new market trends and practices.
Manage the team’s pipeline of projects, actively sourcing local projects, ensuring alignment of priorities with CIB senior management (with CIBC&T EMEA and local management) and ensure as applicable the success of co-staffing missions with partner teams.
Engage the team as applicable on AI and data management project topics in collaboration and coordination with specialist teams (e.g., CIB Analytics Consulting, CIB AI or Data Office).
Set up delivery standards ensuring alignment with CIBC&T EMEA while adapting for local mandates to the Indian context.

Build Long-term Relationships With Clients & Partners

Initiate relationships, establish connectivity with clients and partners and build up the CIBC&T brand and franchise in the Indian territory and center of expertise.
Provide meaningful support and advice locally to the Indian territory and globally to CIB Management to achieve their ambitions.
Secure trustworthiness (legitimacy and credibility) to become a trusted advisor with clients at all levels.
Anticipate the needs of internal clients and partners, provide relevant responses and solutions to meet them.
Use positive influence to constructively challenge clients on transformation initiatives, at senior management levels, in alignment with the overarching CIB strategy.
Go beyond existing frameworks and test innovative project operating modes & communicate on knowledge of innovative / disruptive market best practices.
Jointly with CIBC&T teams, contribute to reinforcing the C&T network through best practices sharing (content, methodologies, projects, etc.)
Establish relationships and foster coordination and collaboration with regional APAC Consulting and Transformation teams.

Work with the Extended Leadership team to enrich the CIBC&T EMEA strategy, aligned with CIB overall strategic plan and more specifically taking into account local views.

Technical & Behavioral Competencies

Behavioral Skills

Excellent communication and influencing skills, with the ability to negotiate, collaborate and influence at senior management level.
High adaptability in changing environments, a capacity to bring value and propose meaningful solutions.
Ability to initiate and establish sustainable client relationships and build up and activate networks.
Expert organisational skills.
Excellent problem-solving skills.

Business Skills

Significant experience working in a top-tier Consulting firm or internal Consulting team.
Strong working knowledge of strategic consulting and transformation in a Financial Institution, with the ability to lead complex strategic consulting projects across business & functions.
Strong knowledge of Corporate and Institutional Banking (CIB) business activity and products is recommended.
Strong knowledge of operations, information technology and finance functions is a plus.

Transversal Skills

Strong management skills with the ability to build up a team and inspire, train and upskill team members.
Ability to think strategically and operationally.
Comfortable operating in full autonomy, self-driven.

Language Skills

English (fluent), French (recommended)

Specific Qualifications

Skills Referential (Required knowledge, skills and abilities)

Technical Skills

Ability to manage a project
Ability to understand, explain and support change
Ability to anticipate business / strategic evolution
Ability to develop others & improve their skills
Ability to inspire others & generate people's commitment
Behavioral Skills

Ability to collaborate / Teamwork
Ability to deliver / Results driven
Decision Making
Client focused
Education Level: A master’s degree from a top-tier institution

Experience Level: At least 15 years

Location: Mumbai

About BNP Paribas Group

BNP Paribas is the European Union’s leading bank and key player in international banking. It operates in 65 countries and has nearly 185,000 employees, including more than 145,000 in Europe. The Group has key positions in its three main fields of activity: Commercial, Personal Banking & Services for the Group’s commercial & personal banking and several specialized businesses including BNP Paribas Personal Finance and Arval; Investment & Protection Services for savings, investment, and protection solutions; and Corporate & Institutional Banking, focused on corporate and institutional clients. Based on its strong diversified and integrated model, the Group helps all its clients (individuals, community associations, entrepreneurs, SMEs, corporates and institutional clients) to realize their projects through solutions spanning financing, investment, savings and protection insurance. In Europe, BNP Paribas has four domestic markets: Belgium, France, Italy, and Luxembourg. The Group is rolling out its integrated commercial & personal banking model across several Mediterranean countries, Turkey, and Eastern Europe. As a key player in international banking, the Group has leading platforms and business lines in Europe, a strong presence in the Americas as well as a solid and fast-growing business in Asia-Pacific. BNP Paribas has implemented a Corporate Social Responsibility approach in all its activities, enabling it to contribute to the construction of a sustainable future, while ensuring the Group's performance and stability.

About BNP Paribas India Solutions

Established in 2005, BNP Paribas India Solutions is a wholly owned subsidiary of BNP Paribas SA, European Union’s leading bank with an international reach. With delivery centers located in Bengaluru, Chennai and Mumbai, we are a 24x7 global delivery center. India Solutions services three business lines: Corporate and Institutional Banking, Investment Solutions and Retail Banking for BNP Paribas across the Group. Driving innovation and growth, we are harnessing the potential of over 10000 employees, to provide support and develop best-in-class solutions.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

At BNP Paribas, we passionately embrace diversity and are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where all employees are valued, respected and can bring their authentic selves to work. We prohibit Discrimination and Harassment of any kind and our policies promote equal employment opportunity for all employees and applicants, irrespective of, but not limited to their gender, gender identity, sex, sexual orientation, ethnicity, race, colour, national origin, age, religion, social status, mental or physical disabilities, veteran status etc. As a global Bank, we truly believe that inclusion and diversity of our teams is key to our success in serving our clients and the communities we operate in. As a global Bank, we truly believe that inclusion and diversity of our teams is key to our success in serving our clients and the communities we operate in. 
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: Group COO Chief of Staff Office - VP
items.jobDescription:
Join us as a "Group COO Chief of Staff Office - VP” at Barclays. Operating as part of a global strategic team, you will collaborate with colleagues in London, New York, and other hubs to deliver work that influences the whole organization. The role requires sound understanding of banking, financial markets and the latest technology developments. This role provides the opportunity to make a strong immediate contribution for applicants who are proactive, tenacious and eager to learn about multiple products and clients.

To be a successful “Group COO Chief of Staff Office - VP " role provides direct exposure to Group-wide leadership and works at the intersection of strategy, transformation, technology, and operational excellence. You will help shape critical materials for senior decision‑making, drive cross‑business initiatives, and generate insights that guide how the Group executes its priorities.

You may be assessed on the key critical skills relevant for success in role, candidate is expected to work in a high intensity, results orientated environment as part of the Group Chief Operating Officer Chief of Staff Office, a team that partners across the entire Barclays Group to support strategic priorities, enterprise initiatives, and business performance.

Basic/ Essential Qualifications


Consulting, strategy, CEO/COO/Group Office roles, or strategic roles in financial services.
Strong analytical and conceptual problem‑solving skills.
Ability to turn complex information into crisp storylines for senior stakeholders.
Experience working with cross‑functional and global teams.
Comfort with ambiguity and the ability to create structure where none exists.
Strong understanding of financial services, technology, and emerging themes such as digital transformation and GenAI.
Advanced data, MI, PowerPoint and Excel skills.
A proactive, collaborative, and commercially minded operator.
The candidate needs to driven, self-starter working on high stakes and time sensitive activities and hence is required to be meticulous and efficient.
Ability to synthesize data into actionable insights and can use public information sources to research market trends, recent news and competitor activity.


Desirable Skillsets/ Good To Have


Experience in working with global offices and the ability to collaborate across multiple regions
Hands-on experience working in cross-functional/multi geographical settings
Understanding of Finance & Accounting concepts
Prior experience of consulting or chief of staff


This role will be based out of Mumbai.

Purpose of the role

To enable the success of senior executives by helping navigate complex challenges, make informed decisions and deliver against their strategic objectives.

Accountabilities


Strategic support to senior executives, such as the CEO or other top-level leaders including assistance in the development and execution of in year and multi-year business strategy, research, and analysis to support decision-making and act as delegate for the senior executive in specific situations.
Management of Colleague engagement planning in line with the strategic direction, managing communication channels and ensuring effective coordination across different departments and teams.
Management of key projects and strategic initiatives on behalf of senior executives, ensuring strategic projects are on track, monitoring of progress, and provision of regular updates to the executive team.
Improvement of operational efficiency, alongside functional partners, within the organisation including the identification of areas for improvement, streamlining of processes, and implementation of consistently excellent best practices to enhance productivity and effectiveness.
Development of performance reporting for key metrics that reinforce the strategic objectives of the division and support the external commitments made. Analyse and draw insight to drive performance. Involvement in aspects of financial analysis and budget management, including assistance in financial planning, forecasting, cost management and monitoring of financial performance against targets.
Support to senior leadership to ensure appropriate resourcing across the business/function including planning and forecasting, partnering with HR to drive talent development and succession planning including the identification of high-potential employees, provision of mentorship and guidance, and support to leadership development and diversity initiatives.
Support to the senior executive with risk and control oversight, escalation, crisis management and risk mitigation, support the development of contingency plans, coordination of responses to emergencies, and ensure business continuity.
Implementation of a robust governance framework which supports the board of directors, executive forums and governance committees through the preparation of board materials, organisation of meetings, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.


Vice President Expectations


To contribute or set strategy, drive requirements and make recommendations for change. Plan resources, budgets, and policies; manage and maintain policies/ processes; deliver continuous improvements and escalate breaches of policies/procedures..
If managing a team, they define jobs and responsibilities, planning for the department’s future needs and operations, counselling employees on performance and contributing to employee pay decisions/changes. They may also lead a number of specialists to influence the operations of a department, in alignment with strategic as well as tactical priorities, while balancing short and long term goals and ensuring that budgets and schedules meet corporate requirements..
If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others..
OR for an individual contributor, they will be a subject matter expert within own discipline and will guide technical direction. They will lead collaborative, multi-year assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will train, guide and coach less experienced specialists and provide information affecting long term profits, organisational risks and strategic decisions..
Advise key stakeholders, including functional leadership teams and senior management on functional and cross functional areas of impact and alignment.
Manage and mitigate risks through assessment, in support of the control and governance agenda.
Demonstrate leadership and accountability for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work your team does.
Demonstrate comprehensive understanding of the organisation functions to contribute to achieving the goals of the business.
Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategies.
Create solutions based on sophisticated analytical thought comparing and selecting complex alternatives. In-depth analysis with interpretative thinking will be required to define problems and develop innovative solutions.
Adopt and include the outcomes of extensive research in problem solving processes.
Seek out, build and maintain trusting relationships and partnerships with internal and external stakeholders in order to accomplish key business objectives, using influencing and negotiating skills to achieve outcomes.


All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave. 
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: Group COO Chief of Staff Office - AVP
items.jobDescription:
Join us as a " Group COO Chief of Staff Office - AVP” at Barclays. Operating as part of a global strategic team, you will collaborate with colleagues in London, New York, and other hubs to deliver work that influences the whole organization. The role requires sound understanding of banking, financial markets and the latest technology developments. This role provides the opportunity to make a strong immediate contribution for applicants who are proactive, tenacious and eager to learn about multiple products and clients.

To be a successful “Group COO Chief of Staff Office - AVP " role provides direct exposure to Group-wide leadership and works at the intersection of strategy, transformation, technology, and operational excellence. You will help shape critical materials for senior decision‑making, drive cross‑business initiatives, and generate insights that guide how the Group executes its priorities.

You may be assessed on the key critical skills relevant for success in role, candidate is expected to work in a high intensity, results orientated environment as part of the Group Chief Operating Officer Chief of Staff Office, a team that partners across the entire Barclays Group to support strategic priorities, enterprise initiatives, and business performance.

Basic/ Essential Qualifications


Consulting, strategy, CEO/COO/Group Office roles, or strategic roles in financial services.
Strong analytical and conceptual problem‑solving skills.
Experience of managing complex and big projects / programs / initiatives.
Strong understanding of financial services, technology, and emerging themes such as digital transformation and GenAI.
Ability to automate or run processes efficiently.
Advanced data, MI, PowerPoint and Excel skills.
Experience working with cross‑functional and global teams.
A proactive, collaborative, and commercially minded operator.
The candidate needs to driven, self-starter working on high stakes and time sensitive activities and hence is required to be meticulous and efficient.


Desirable Skillsets/ Good To Have


Experience in working with global offices and the ability to collaborate across multiple regions.
Hands-on experience working in cross-functional/multi geographical settings.
Understanding of Finance & Accounting concepts.
Prior experience of consulting or chief of staff.


This role will be based out of Mumbai.

Purpose of the role

To enable the success of senior executives by helping navigate complex challenges, make informed decisions and deliver against their strategic objectives.

Accountabilities


Strategic support to senior executives, such as the CEO or other top-level leaders including assistance in the development and execution of in year and multi-year business strategy, research, and analysis to support decision-making and act as delegate for the senior executive in specific situations.
Management of Colleague engagement planning in line with the strategic direction, managing communication channels and ensuring effective coordination across different departments and teams.
Management of key projects and strategic initiatives on behalf of senior executives, ensuring strategic projects are on track, monitoring of progress, and provision of regular updates to the executive team.
Improvement of operational efficiency, alongside functional partners, within the organisation including the identification of areas for improvement, streamlining of processes, and implementation of consistently excellent best practices to enhance productivity and effectiveness.
Development of performance reporting for key metrics that reinforce the strategic objectives of the division and support the external commitments made. Analyse and draw insight to drive performance. Involvement in aspects of financial analysis and budget management, including assistance in financial planning, forecasting, cost management and monitoring of financial performance against targets.
Support to senior leadership to ensure appropriate resourcing across the business/function including planning and forecasting, partnering with HR to drive talent development and succession planning including the identification of high-potential employees, provision of mentorship and guidance, and support to leadership development and diversity initiatives.
Support to the senior executive with risk and control oversight, escalation, crisis management and risk mitigation, support the development of contingency plans, coordination of responses to emergencies, and ensure business continuity.
Implementation of a robust governance framework which supports the board of directors, executive forums and governance committees through the preparation of board materials, organisation of meetings, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.


Assistant Vice President Expectations


To advise and influence decision making, contribute to policy development and take responsibility for operational effectiveness. Collaborate closely with other functions/ business divisions.
Lead a team performing complex tasks, using well developed professional knowledge and skills to deliver on work that impacts the whole business function. Set objectives and coach employees in pursuit of those objectives, appraisal of performance relative to objectives and determination of reward outcomes
If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others.
OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes.
Consult on complex issues; providing advice to People Leaders to support the resolution of escalated issues.
Identify ways to mitigate risk and developing new policies/procedures in support of the control and governance agenda.
Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work done.
Perform work that is closely related to that of other areas, which requires understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function.
Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy.
Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively.
Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience.
Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes.


All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

items.jobTitle: Founder's Chief of Staff and Operations Manager
items.jobDescription:
Founder's Chief of Staff & Operations Manager

Position: EA + Personal Manager + Brand Operations

Location: Gurgaon, Haryana

️ Working Days: Monday to Friday

Experience Required: 2+ years

About The Role

We are looking for an exceptional, high-energy professional to serve as the backbone of a founder-led business and personal brand. This is not a conventional EA or PA role — it sits at the intersection of both, combining sharp operational execution with strategic thinking, brand management, and lifestyle coordination.

You will be the founder's extended brain — anticipating needs, driving execution, representing the founder with authority in negotiations, and ensuring that both personal and professional commitments are managed with precision and care.

Key Responsibilities

Business & Operations

Own and manage the founder's professional calendar — meetings, pitches, networking events, and business commitments
Coordinate with team members, vendors, collaborators, and external partners on behalf of the founder
Lead vendor negotiations — rates, contracts, and timelines — with assertiveness and commercial acumen
Manage project timelines, deliverables, and accountability across multiple workstreams
Handle all high-priority business communications requiring the founder's attention
Support recruiting efforts — coordinate with talent acquisition partners, screen candidates, and manage interview scheduling
Manage and track brand collaboration deals, agreements, and partnership deliverables

Personal Brand & Lifestyle Management

Maintain the founder's personal calendar including social commitments, dinners, personal appointments, and events
Track all personal branding assignments and ensure timely payment follow-ups across brand collaborations
Manage corporate and personalized gifting — sourcing, budgeting, vendor coordination, and execution
Plan, coordinate, and manage both personal and professional events — from intimate dinners to large gatherings
Travel coordination — logistics, bookings, itineraries — and accompany the founder to key events and functions when required
Act as the first point of contact across personal commitments, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks

Negotiations & Representation

Negotiate rates, terms, and conditions with vendors, collaborators, and service providers on the founder's behalf
Represent the founder's interests assertively across collaborations, brand deals, and operational matters
Maintain and nurture key professional relationships with discretion and consistency

Experience

WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

2+ years managing a founder's, CEO's, or C-suite executive's schedule and operations
Demonstrated experience in vendor management, event coordination, or brand operations
Familiarity with the demands of a fast-moving, entrepreneurial environment

Personality & Skills

Bold and assertive — able to negotiate, push back, and advocate firmly on the founder's behalf
Exceptionally organized with an eye for detail and zero tolerance for dropped balls
Proactive mindset — anticipates needs rather than waiting to be told
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English — crisp, professional, and articulate
Discreet, trustworthy, and able to handle sensitive personal and professional information with complete confidentiality
Energetic, flexible, and available beyond standard working hours when required
Young, driven, and hungry to grow within a dynamic founder-led environment

Technical Proficiency

Proficient with Google Workspace (Calendar, Drive, Gmail, Docs, Sheets)
Comfortable with scheduling tools, project management platforms, and communication apps
Ability to pick up new tools quickly

What We Offer

A front-row seat to building a business from the ground up — exposure across strategy, operations, and brand
Competitive salary, negotiable based on experience and capability
Direct mentorship and close collaboration with the founder
A high-trust, high-responsibility role with real ownership from day one
Growth opportunities as the business scales

Candidates with prior founder-facing experience are strongly encouraged to apply.

items.jobDescription:
Location: Jakarta, Indonesia

About the Company
Founded in 2019, we are a Jakarta-based fintech startup on a mission to digitize financial services for Indonesia’s micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs). We’re building tools and products that enable millions of small businesses to thrive in a digital-first economy.
With a strong product-market fit in Indonesia, we are now gearing up to launch in new Southeast Asian markets, starting with the Philippines — and we’re looking for someone exceptional to help us lead the charge.

About the Role
As Chief of Staff, you’ll work closely with the Founders and Leadership Team to drive strategic initiatives and lead new market and business launches. This role is a mix of strategy, execution, and hands-on problem solving, giving you a front-row seat to how high-growth startups are built and scaled across markets.
If you’re someone who loves turning ideas into real business outcomes, thrives in ambiguity, and eventually sees yourself founding or leading a company, this role is your crash course in doing exactly that.

What You’ll Do
Drive New Market Launches: Lead research, market entry strategy, and on-ground execution for expansion into the Philippines and other Southeast Asian markets.
Launch New Business Verticals: Partner with founders to identify, test, and scale new revenue streams across regions.
Improve Organisational Efficiency: Design and implement systems and processes to help teams move faster and smarter.
Cross-functional Leadership: Collaborate with teams across product, growth, operations, and finance to ensure strategic alignment and operational excellence.
Be a Thought Partner: Work directly with the CEO/founders on high-priority projects, board-level discussions, and investor communication.

What We’re Looking For
3–10 years of experience in consulting, business analysis, founder’s office, or a high-growth startup.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex problems.
Exceptional communication skills — written, verbal, and interpersonal.
Highly organized and proactive, comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously.
Curiosity and hunger for growth — you thrive in uncertainty and love learning new things.
Global mindset with a willingness to relocate to Jakarta and travel across Southeast Asia.
Passion for technology, startups, and building zero-to-one businesses.

Why Join Us
A front-row seat to the next wave of fintech innovation in Southeast Asia.
Work directly with founders and senior leaders, learning the ins and outs of scaling a company.
A fast-paced, high-growth environment where your work directly impacts business outcomes.
A springboard for future founders — this role is designed for people who want to build something of their own someday.
