items.jobTitle: Chief of Staff to the CEO
items.jobDescription:
About the Role
This is a high-ownership, execution-heavy role working directly with the CEO. You will own the operating rhythm, analytical backbone, and strategic planning of the company, giving the CEO leverage to focus on customers, product, and growth.
You will be in the room for every important decision, from pricing million-dollar enterprise deals to planning market expansion. You will work across sales, finance, product, and engineering, giving you a cross-functional understanding of how a high-growth B2B AI company actually operates.


What You Will Own
Monthly MIS and business metrics: Build and maintain company-wide and function-level tracking. Identify what is improving, what needs attention, and why. You will be the person who catches a number that doesn't look right before anyone else does.
Investor updates and board preparation: Own the monthly investor narrative, covering business performance, functional highlights, and strategic context. You will develop a deep understanding of how to communicate with institutional investors.
Business Planning: Working with the founders/leadership to build a long-term Business plan and tracking month-on-month progress on both revenue and cost side.
Strategic projects: Work on pricing analysis, market sizing, competitive positioning, and GTM planning as the company scales its Voice AI Calling product into new segments and geographies.
Competitive intelligence: Research companies in adjacent spaces (conversational AI, BFSI SaaS, collections tech), map their positioning, and bring actionable insights back to leadership.
VC relationship management: Build and manage a pipeline of prospective investors relevant to GreyLabs' future fundraising. Handle introductions, follow-ups, and relationship tracking.
Leadership reviews: Drive biweekly 1:1s and functional reviews with department heads, document decisions and action items, and hold people accountable for follow-through.


What Makes This Role Hard and Interesting
You will model unit economics for a product where a single client contract can be worth over a crore a year. You will help the leadership team think through expansion into new geographies. You will build the analytical frameworks that determine how the company allocates resources across two distinct product lines with very different margin profiles. The problems here are not templated; they require genuine first-principles thinking.


What We Are Looking For
2 to 4 years of experience as an Analyst or Associate at a VC or PE fund, boutique investment bank, or strategy consulting firm
BTech and MBA from Tier 1 institutes preferred
Strong analytical and written communication skills
High ownership and comfort with ambiguity
Familiarity with startup operating rhythms is a plus but not required

Where This Role Goes
This is designed as a path to functional leadership. After 18 to 24 months of building cross-functional expertise, the natural evolution is into a role such as Director of Finance, Investor Relations, and Strategy, owning the company's financial planning, capital strategy, and strategic initiatives as a member of the leadership team. Your trajectory will be shaped by what you build in this role.


Why GreyLabs
60+ enterprise clients, and a product line that went from zero to our fastest-growing revenue stream in under a year
You will work directly with the CEO and founding team from day one
AI adoption in Indian BFSI is accelerating faster than almost any other sector, and GreyLabs is at the centre of it
A concrete path to functional leadership, not a vague promise, with a named destination and a timeline

We’re committed to creating a fair, respectful, and inclusive workplace. From hiring to growth opportunities, our decisions are based on merit, skills, and potential, never on gender, identity, background, or any other personal characteristic. Talent has no labels here, and everyone is welcome to grow and thrive with us.
Show more Show less

About The Role & Team

We are looking for an exceptional Chief of Staff to the CEO who will work at the center of Meesho’s leadership system - partnering closely with the CEO and CXOs/GMs to drive company-wide strategy, operating rigor, and high-impact cross-functional initiatives.

This is not a support role - it is a leadership role.

The Chief of Staff will act as a force multiplier for the CEO - helping evolve the company’s operating system, driving mission-critical strategic projects, shaping culture, and increasing the velocity and quality of executive decision-making. The role will continuously evolve as the company’s priorities evolve.

This is a front-row seat to building and scaling a public technology company - and requires extreme ownership, structured thinking, execution speed, and leadership maturity.

What You’ll Do

CEO Leverage & Executive Partnership
Act as a strategic partner and thought partner to the CEO
Help prioritize, structure, and drive the CEO’s highest-impact initiatives
Improve CEO decision velocity and clarity through structured inputs and analysis
Prepare strategic briefs, decision frameworks, and executive narratives
Represent the CEO in key forums and internal leadership discussions when needed
Company Operating System & Governance
Help design and continuously evolve the company’s operating cadence and management systems
Drive cross-functional governance rhythms (reviews, planning cycles, performance tracking)
Improve clarity, alignment, and accountability across leadership teams
Ensure follow-through on CEO and leadership priorities
Strategic & Cross-Functional Projects
Lead and execute high-impact, cross-org strategic initiatives
Work on projects that cut across business, product, tech, and operations
Incubate and evaluate new business opportunities and adjacencies
Structure ambiguous problems and convert them into executable plans
Work closely with CXOs and GMs to unblock and accelerate key priorities
Culture & Leadership Influence
Help reinforce leadership principles and cultural expectations
Drive leadership communications and alignment initiatives
Influence how decisions get made and how leaders collaborate
Help raise the bar on thinking quality and execution discipline
Role Characteristics
Scope will evolve with company priorities and CEO agenda
High ambiguity, high ownership, high trust role
Requires comfort operating without formal authority but with high influence
Combines strategy, execution, governance, and leadership leverage
Mix of thinker + builder + operator


What You’ll Need

Must Have
5-7 Years of work experience
Strong structured problem solving and executive communication skills
Demonstrated experience working on complex, ambiguous business problems
Proven ability to drive cross-functional execution
High ownership and bias toward action
Comfort working with senior leadership teams
Strong Advantage
Experience building or scaling tech products or businesses
Startup or high-growth company operating experience
Prior experience in founder’s office / CEO office / strategy roles
Experience working closely with product and technology teams
Exposure to marketplace, e-commerce, or platform businesses
Leadership Traits We Value
Hungry, high-drive leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-intensity environments with strong first-principles thinking and high learning velocity
Low ego, high accountability, operating with discretion and sound judgment
Exceptional clarity of thought and communication, with strong influence without authority
Highly trusted by senior stakeholders for reliability, judgment, and impact
Why This Role Matters
Direct impact on CEO effectiveness and company trajectory
Opportunity to shape how a public tech company operates and scales
Exposure to the most important strategic decisions in the company
Platform to build enterprise-level leadership capability
Launchpad for future leadership roles within the organization
Show more Show less

About BiteSpeed

Hey there! We are a Sequoia-backed SaaS startup building an AI-native Marketing, Support & Sales suite for e-commerce brands.We’re currently working with 3000+ e-commerce brands globally across 50+ countries and are fortunate to have raised $5.5M+ along the journey with marquee investors like Sequoia Capital India, Kunal Shah, Gaurav Munjal & more backing us.

Check out more about us here - https://www.bitespeed.co/ and do read through our 200+ odd 5-star reviews to get a sense of what our customers say about us here - https://apps.shopify.com/bitespeed-fb-messenger-chatbot (we openly brag about this 😉)

We’ve had some solid investors back us (making it easier for you to stalk us since you’d do this anyway):-

BiteSpeed Raises USD 3.5M Funding, Led by Peak XV’s Surge- https://www.businesswireindia.com/e-commerce-ai-startup-bitespeed-raises-usd-3-5m-funding-led-by-peak-xvs-surge-92455.html
E-commerce AI start-up BiteSpeed raises $3.5 million funding led by Peak XV’s Surge- https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/info-tech/e-commerce-ai-start-up-bitespeed-raises-35-million-funding-led-by-peak-xvs-surge/article68863058.ece
BiteSpeed Raises $1.9 Million Seed Funding From Sequoia India's Surge- https://www.entrepreneur.com/en-in/business-news/bitespeed-raises-19-million-seed-funding-from-sequoia/418414

About The Role

This is a true generalist business role that involves working closely with our CEO on growth and revenue initiatives. It’s an intense, always on, high impact role. If you enjoy all things business & sales, juggling multiple initiatives and like rolling up your sleeves to get the job done this might be the role for you :).

The role spans across unlocking new 0-1 motions in international markets, starting and scaling new channels like partnerships and events, overseeing sales and account management strategy & revenue operations.

What You’ll Do

Partner with sales teams on revenue maximisation initiatives ranging from pricing and packaging strategy to optimising demos and pitches for different segments of customers.
Setting up tooling and operational processes for sales monitoring and revenue planning.
Partner with CS teams on upselling and churn management strategy.
Own new 0-1 initiatives in international markets for expansion.
Own new channel initiatives like partnerships and event marketing.
Work closely with CEO on enterprise deals.

What Makes You a Good Fit

2+ years of early stage startup experience on the business side.
Love all things business and sales and don’t shy away from getting your hands dirty to get the job done.
Comfortable with early stage startup imperfections and able to operate in disorder.
Extremely high agency and adaptability to make things happen.

Salary and Location

Location: Bangalore

Expected CTC: We pay top of market for the right folks and also offer generous equity (ESOP) to everyone in the team

Our Way Of Life - https://www.notion.so/bitespeed/Way-Of-Life-At-BiteSpeed-44d9b9614d9641419da910189b1e9f8e.

Our Purpose

At BiteSpeed, work is personal. You could blame this on us being existential, but most of us are spending the best years of our lives doing this and we want to be purposeful about the kind of workplace we’re trying to create.

Our purpose is about why we’re here and what we care about:-

Personal Transformation
Wealth Creation
Winning Together

Our Values

Our values are about how we do what we do. Values define the right thing to do. We hire, reward and sometimes have to let go based on our values.

We have 5 core values:-

Go Above And Beyond
Making Things Happen
Say It Like It Is
Progress Over Perfection
Don’t Take Yourself Seriously, Take Your Work Seriously

Perks & Benefits

Small things we’ve done to ensure we take care of our wellness, learning & keep things fun:-

Health Insurance - Health insurance cover and accident coverage for extra cushion and mental peace when rainy days hit us.
Quarterly Off-sites - Quarterly off-sites are a core part of the BiteSpeed culture. Our off-sites range from intense quarter planning sessions to crazy mafia nights and competitive cricket matches (with a lot of trash talking).
Cult Fitness Membership - All work and no play makes jack a dull boy. Cult Fit and Cult Play passes to make sure we hit the gym more often.
Personal Development - We sponsor courses, conference tickets, books on a case to case basis to ensure we’re constantly growing.
Salary In Advance - Trust first, by default. We pay out salaries in the first week of the month.

How to apply?

Know someone who might be a great fit? Refer them to us, if they end up joining we'll send you an Apple Airpods Pro as a gesture of thanks!

Development of performance reporting for key metrics that reinforce the strategic objectives of the division and support the external commitments made. Analyse and draw insight to drive performance. Involvement in aspects of financial analysis and budget management, including assistance in financial planning, forecasting, cost management and monitoring of financial performance against targets.
Support to senior leadership to ensure appropriate resourcing across the business/function including planning and forecasting, partnering with HR to drive talent development and succession planning including the identification of high-potential employees, provision of mentorship and guidance, and support to leadership development and diversity initiatives.
Support to the senior executive with risk and control oversight, escalation, crisis management and risk mitigation, support the development of contingency plans, coordination of responses to emergencies, and ensure business continuity.
Implementation of a robust governance framework which supports the board of directors, executive forums and governance committees through the preparation of board materials, organisation of meetings, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.

Vice President Expectations

To contribute or set strategy, drive requirements and make recommendations for change. Plan resources, budgets, and policies; manage and maintain policies/ processes; deliver continuous improvements and escalate breaches of policies/procedures..
If managing a team, they define jobs and responsibilities, planning for the department’s future needs and operations, counselling employees on performance and contributing to employee pay decisions/changes. They may also lead a number of specialists to influence the operations of a department, in alignment with strategic as well as tactical priorities, while balancing short and long term goals and ensuring that budgets and schedules meet corporate requirements..
If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others..
OR for an individual contributor, they will be a subject matter expert within own discipline and will guide technical direction. They will lead collaborative, multi-year assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will train, guide and coach less experienced specialists and provide information affecting long term profits, organisational risks and strategic decisions..
Advise key stakeholders, including functional leadership teams and senior management on functional and cross functional areas of impact and alignment.
Manage and mitigate risks through assessment, in support of the control and governance agenda.
Demonstrate leadership and accountability for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work your team does.
Demonstrate comprehensive understanding of the organisation functions to contribute to achieving the goals of the business.
Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategies.
Create solutions based on sophisticated analytical thought comparing and selecting complex alternatives. In-depth analysis with interpretative thinking will be required to define problems and develop innovative solutions.
Adopt and include the outcomes of extensive research in problem solving processes.
Seek out, build and maintain trusting relationships and partnerships with internal and external stakeholders in order to accomplish key business objectives, using influencing and negotiating skills to achieve outcomes.

All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

items.jobDescription:
hackajob is collaborating with Barclays to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

Join us as a " Group COO Chief of Staff Office - AVP” at Barclays. Operating as part of a global strategic team, you will collaborate with colleagues in London, New York, and other hubs to deliver work that influences the whole organization. The role requires sound understanding of banking, financial markets and the latest technology developments. This role provides the opportunity to make a strong immediate contribution for applicants who are proactive, tenacious and eager to learn about multiple products and clients.

To be a successful “Group COO Chief of Staff Office - AVP " role provides direct exposure to Group-wide leadership and works at the intersection of strategy, transformation, technology, and operational excellence. You will help shape critical materials for senior decision‑making, drive cross‑business initiatives, and generate insights that guide how the Group executes its priorities.

You may be assessed on the key critical skills relevant for success in role, candidate is expected to work in a high intensity, results orientated environment as part of the Group Chief Operating Officer Chief of Staff Office, a team that partners across the entire Barclays Group to support strategic priorities, enterprise initiatives, and business performance.

Basic/ Essential Qualifications

Consulting, strategy, CEO/COO/Group Office roles, or strategic roles in financial services.
Strong analytical and conceptual problem‑solving skills.
Experience of managing complex and big projects / programs / initiatives.
Strong understanding of financial services, technology, and emerging themes such as digital transformation and GenAI.
Ability to automate or run processes efficiently.
Advanced data, MI, PowerPoint and Excel skills.
Experience working with cross‑functional and global teams.
A proactive, collaborative, and commercially minded operator.
The candidate needs to driven, self-starter working on high stakes and time sensitive activities and hence is required to be meticulous and efficient.

Desirable Skillsets/ Good To Have

Experience in working with global offices and the ability to collaborate across multiple regions.
Hands-on experience working in cross-functional/multi geographical settings.
Understanding of Finance & Accounting concepts.
Prior experience of consulting or chief of staff.

This role will be based out of Mumbai.

Purpose of the role

To enable the success of senior executives by helping navigate complex challenges, make informed decisions and deliver against their strategic objectives.

Accountabilities

Strategic support to senior executives, such as the CEO or other top-level leaders including assistance in the development and execution of in year and multi-year business strategy, research, and analysis to support decision-making and act as delegate for the senior executive in specific situations.
Management of Colleague engagement planning in line with the strategic direction, managing communication channels and ensuring effective coordination across different departments and teams.
Management of key projects and strategic initiatives on behalf of senior executives, ensuring strategic projects are on track, monitoring of progress, and provision of regular updates to the executive team.
Improvement of operational efficiency, alongside functional partners, within the organisation including the identification of areas for improvement, streamlining of processes, and implementation of consistently excellent best practices to enhance productivity and effectiveness.
Development of performance reporting for key metrics that reinforce the strategic objectives of the division and support the external commitments made. Analyse and draw insight to drive performance. Involvement in aspects of financial analysis and budget management, including assistance in financial planning, forecasting, cost management and monitoring of financial performance against targets.
Support to senior leadership to ensure appropriate resourcing across the business/function including planning and forecasting, partnering with HR to drive talent development and succession planning including the identification of high-potential employees, provision of mentorship and guidance, and support to leadership development and diversity initiatives.
Support to the senior executive with risk and control oversight, escalation, crisis management and risk mitigation, support the development of contingency plans, coordination of responses to emergencies, and ensure business continuity.
Implementation of a robust governance framework which supports the board of directors, executive forums and governance committees through the preparation of board materials, organisation of meetings, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements.

Assistant Vice President Expectations

To advise and influence decision making, contribute to policy development and take responsibility for operational effectiveness. Collaborate closely with other functions/ business divisions.
Lead a team performing complex tasks, using well developed professional knowledge and skills to deliver on work that impacts the whole business function. Set objectives and coach employees in pursuit of those objectives, appraisal of performance relative to objectives and determination of reward outcomes
If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others.
OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes.
Consult on complex issues; providing advice to People Leaders to support the resolution of escalated issues.
Identify ways to mitigate risk and developing new policies/procedures in support of the control and governance agenda.
Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work done.
Perform work that is closely related to that of other areas, which requires understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function.
Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy.
Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively.
Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience.
Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes.

All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

About BiteSpeed

Hey there! We are a Sequoia-backed SaaS startup building an AI-native Marketing, Support & Sales suite for e-commerce brands.

We’re currently working with 3000+ e-commerce brands globally across 50+ countries and are fortunate to have raised $5.5M+ along the journey with marquee investors like Sequoia Capital India, Kunal Shah, Gaurav Munjal & more backing us.

Check out more about us here - https://www.bitespeed.co/ and do read through our 200+ odd 5-star reviews to get a sense of what our customers say about us here - https://apps.shopify.com/bitespeed-fb-messenger-chatbot (we openly brag about this 😉)

We’ve had some solid investors back us (making it easier for you to stalk us since you’d do this anyway):-

BiteSpeed Raises USD 3.5M Funding, Led by Peak XV’s Surge- https://www.businesswireindia.com/e-commerce-ai-startup-bitespeed-raises-usd-3-5m-funding-led-by-peak-xvs-surge-92455.html

E-commerce AI start-up BiteSpeed raises $3.5 million funding led by Peak XV’s Surge- https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/info-tech/e-commerce-ai-start-up-bitespeed-raises-35-million-funding-led-by-peak-xvs-surge/article68863058.ece

BiteSpeed Raises $1.9 Million Seed Funding From Sequoia India's Surge- https://www.entrepreneur.com/en-in/business-news/bitespeed-raises-19-million-seed-funding-from-sequoia/418414


About the role

This is a true generalist business role that involves working closely with our CEO on growth and revenue initiatives. It’s an intense, always on, high impact role. If you enjoy all things business & sales, juggling multiple initiatives and like rolling up your sleeves to get the job done this might be the role for you :).

The role spans across unlocking new 0-1 motions in international markets, starting and scaling new channels like partnerships and events, overseeing sales and account management strategy & revenue operations.

What you’ll do

Partner with sales teams on revenue maximisation initiatives ranging from pricing and packaging strategy to optimising demos and pitches for different segments of customers.

Setting up tooling and operational processes for sales monitoring and revenue planning.

Partner with CS teams on upselling and churn management strategy.

Own new 0-1 initiatives in international markets for expansion.

Own new channel initiatives like partnerships and event marketing.

Work closely with CEO on enterprise deals.

What makes you a good fit

2+ years of early stage startup experience on the business side.

Love all things business and sales and don’t shy away from getting your hands dirty to get the job done.

Comfortable with early stage startup imperfections and able to operate in disorder.

Extremely high agency and adaptability to make things happen.

Salary and Location

Location: Bangalore

Expected CTC: We pay top of market for the right folks and also offer generous equity (ESOP) to everyone in the team

Our Way Of Life - https://www.notion.so/bitespeed/Way-Of-Life-At-BiteSpeed-44d9b9614d9641419da910189b1e9f8e.

Our Purpose

At BiteSpeed, work is personal. You could blame this on us being existential, but most of us are spending the best years of our lives doing this and we want to be purposeful about the kind of workplace we’re trying to create.

Our purpose is about why we’re here and what we care about:-

Personal Transformation
Wealth Creation
Winning Together

Our Values

Our values are about how we do what we do. Values define the right thing to do. We hire, reward and sometimes have to let go based on our values.

We have 5 core values:-
Go Above And Beyond
Making Things Happen
Say It Like It Is
Progress Over Perfection
Don’t Take Yourself Seriously, Take Your Work Seriously

Perks & Benefits

Small things we’ve done to ensure we take care of our wellness, learning & keep things fun:-

Health Insurance - Health insurance cover and accident coverage for extra cushion and mental peace when rainy days hit us.

Quarterly Off-sites - Quarterly off-sites are a core part of the BiteSpeed culture. Our off-sites range from intense quarter planning sessions to crazy mafia nights and competitive cricket matches (with a lot of trash talking).

Cult Fitness Membership - All work and no play makes jack a dull boy. Cult Fit and Cult Play passes to make sure we hit the gym more often.

Personal Development - We sponsor courses, conference tickets, books on a case to case basis to ensure we’re constantly growing.

Salary In Advance - Trust first, by default. We pay out salaries in the first week of the month.

How to apply?

If this sounds interesting drop a personal note with why you'd want to work with us and what makes you a good fit on talent@bitespeed.co.

Know someone who might be a great fit? Refer them to us, if they end up joining we'll send you an Apple Airpods Pro as a gesture of thanks!

For any queries feel free to write to talent@bitespeed.co.

Welspun World is one of India's fastest growing global conglomerates with businesses in Home Textiles, Flooring Solutions, Advanced Textiles, DI Pipes, Pig Iron, TMT bars, Stainless Steel, Alloy, Line Pipes, Infrastructure & Warehousing.

At Welspun, we strongly believe in our purpose to delight customers through innovation and technology, achieve inclusive & sustainable growth to remain eminent in all our businesses. From Homes to Highways, Hi-tech to Heavy metals, We lead tomorrow together to create a smarter & more sustainable world.

Job Purpose/ Summary

We are looking for a Chief of Staff to work closely with the CEO, acting as a strategic partner in driving key business priorities, execution excellence, and organizational alignment.

This role is designed for someone who thrives in high-impact, high-visibility environments and can operate at the intersection of strategy, execution, and leadership.

Job Title

Chief of Staff

The Chief of Staff will act as a strategic partner to the CEO, driving business priorities, execution excellence, and organizational alignment. This role requires a high-caliber professional who can seamlessly operate across strategy, operations, and leadership, ensuring that key initiatives are executed with speed and precision.

Company Introduction

Varaha is a global, leading, climate tech venture focused on carbon removal solutions such as - regenerative agriculture, afforestation, biochar, and enhanced rock weathering - that support smallholder land stewards at scale. In just the third year of operation, we are removing and avoiding more than 1 million tons of CO2 and distributing the majority of the revenues to more than 100,000 smallholder land stewards in South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa. Backed by leading global investors, our founding and management team comes with a stellar track record of successfully scaling projects and products to millions of smallholder land stewards at leading Agriculture and tech businesses.

Job Title: Senior Strategy Associate/ Associate Manager

Location: Gurugram, India

Experience: 2+ years (Tier 1 College graduate)/Fresh MBA graduates

Industry: Management Consulting, Strategy, Venture Building, or Corporate Leadership

Function: Strategy and Program Management

About the Role : We are looking for a motivated and intellectually sharp professional to work with the Senior Managers in the COO’s Office in a cross-functional role. This position is suitable for candidates who want exposure to strategic initiatives and operational work while supporting teams that drive projects and improve processes.

The role involves contributing to key business initiatives across strategy, growth, operations, and transformation. Responsibilities include supporting strategic initiatives, assisting with operational excellence, performing data analysis and reporting, coordinating projects, maintaining documentation, and conducting basic financial analysis.

This opportunity is ideal for candidates with strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

Key Responsibilities

Support Strategic Initiatives: Support the team in executing strategic projects and operational transformation initiatives.
Assist with operational Excellence: Contribute to projects focused on improving efficiency and implementing best practices across our operations. This may include drafting SOPs, playbooks tailored to geography and project types.
Data Analysis & Reporting: Create and maintain reports to monitor operational KPIs (e.g., farmer count, cost per enrolment, retention, delivery risk). 
Project Coordination: Facilitate the day-to-day coordination of cross-functional projects, ensuring transparent communication between various internal and external teams
Documentation and System Support: Maintain organized documentation for all processes and systems, ensuring accuracy and version control. Help build and maintain internal tools and dashboards.
Financial Analysis: Support in project economics, helping create basic financial models, track project performance, and cost optimization. Assist in building simple tools and reports to compare projects on efficiency, impact, and risks.

Ideal Candidate Profile

Tier 1 institutes: 2+ years of work experience in a dynamic environment, such as a startup, consulting firm, or a fast-paced corporate role or fresh MBA graduates from premier universities
Strong analytical skills with a good understanding of Excel and a knack for organizing and interpreting data
Ability to manage project documentation, draft reports, and prepare briefs to support execution.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work effectively with different teams.
Detail-oriented and highly organized, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and prioritize effectively.
A proactive and curious mindset, eager to learn and take on new challenges.
Interest in climate action, sustainability, or social impact is a plus.

If you are interested in becoming a part of this venture then I would love to know your story, why you are interested and your skillset and experience. please contact at Career Page.

Please note this job description is not designed to contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Hiring, transferring and promotion practices are performed without regard to the above-listed items.

items.jobTitle: Chief of Staff to the CEO - Navi Limited
items.jobDescription:
About the Team
The CEO's Office works closely with the CEOs to manage and execute key strategic
objectives. The team collaborates with various departments across the organization to
drive the successful implementation of initiatives important to the leadership, ensuring
alignment with the company's long-term vision and goals.

About the Role
The Chief of Staff to the CEO (NL) is a very high exposure role at Navi. This role provides
the birds eye view of every part of the business. This role will work closely with the
founders and the executive leadership team, playing a pivotal role in driving the
company’s strategic initiatives and ensuring operational success. This role will oversee
high-impact initiatives, enhance operational efficiency, and support cross-functional
collaboration.

Key Responsibilities
● Manage the CEO's office and make him more effective and efficient.
● Strategic Initiatives:
○ Collaborate with the CEO to develop and implement the company’s
strategic initiatives.
○ Within strategic initiatives, own and execute some initiatives which may
not fit neatly into one of the functions
● Strategic Alignment: Ensure that the CEO's vision and strategic goals are
communicated and understood throughout the organization, aligning efforts
across departments.
● Operational Efficiency: Improve current processes and coordinate
organizational procedures for optimized efficiency and productivity
● Communication Bridge: Act as a liaison between the CEO and other executives,
employees, and stakeholders, facilitating clear communication and enhancing
collaboration.
● Project Management: Oversee key projects and initiatives, ensuring that they
stay on track and that resources are allocated effectively.
● Problem Solving: Provide data-driven insights and recommendations to
support decision-making.
● Flexibility and Adaptability: Quickly shift priorities as needed, helping the CEO
navigate a rapidly changing business environment.

Must Haves
● Entrepreneurial mind-set along with an ability to hustle and solve problems
on-ground
● Ability to think outside the box and be comfortable dealing with unstructured
problem statements.
● Business first thinking
● Highly analytical and data driven
● Strong interpersonal skills, with a high level of empathy
● Strong verbal and written communication
● Ability to work in a fast paced environment and be a self-starter.

About Navi
We are on a mission to make finance simple, accessible and affordable for a billion
Indians. Guided by a strong customer-first approach, we are building tech-first solutions
that work at scale. Our offerings include a range of financial products and services
across Loans, Insurance, Mutual Funds, Digital Gold and UPI.
Founded by Sachin Bansal & Ankit Agarwal in 2018, we are one of India’s
fastest-growing financial services organisations. But we’re just getting started!

Our Culture
At Navi, we’re a place where ambition meets opportunity and ideas turn into impact
quickly. We empower people with high ownership from the start, encouraging them to
solve meaningful problems and build with excellence. Teams here work in an environment that values speed, collaboration and craftsmanship, while celebrating, learning, growth and shared wins along the way. Whether you’re shaping the future of fintech products or driving innovation behind the scenes, life at Navi means being part of a high-energy, talent-driven workplace where your contributions truly make a difference.

We’re guided by our own operating system - The Navi OS - a set of principles that
shape how we work and win together. You can explore them at navi.com/our-values to see what drives us every day.
If this feels like you, Navi is the place to grow, thrive and make a real impact.

At PwC, your work makes a difference. Join us to shape the future through innovation, teamwork and deep expertise

Associate Director – Impact Assessment
Location
Delhi NCR (with travel as required)
Role Summary
We are seeking an experienced Associate Director to provide strategic and technical leadership for Impact Assessment work within the Economics and Public Policy team at PwC. The role involves leading complex economic, social, and programmatic impact engagements, shaping client mandates, and driving the growth of the Impact Assessment practice across public sector, multilateral, and private sector clients.
The Associate Director will be responsible for overall engagement leadership, client relationship management, technical oversight across projects, people leadership, and business development, working closely with Partners and Directors to scale the practice.
Key Responsibilities
1. Engagement Leadership & Technical Oversight
Provide overall leadership on large and complex impact assessment, impact evaluation, and social/economic valuation engagements.
Set technical direction across projects, ensuring appropriate evaluation design, analytical rigour, and methodological robustness.
Guide teams on advanced application of econometric, statistical, and evaluation approaches (e.g., quasiexperimental methods, contribution analysis, mixedmethods evaluations, SROI where relevant).
Ensure quality, consistency, and credibility of highstakes outputs including flagship reports, policy papers, and seniorlevel presentations.
2. Client Leadership & Strategic Advisory
Serve as trusted advisor to senior clients across government, PSUs, multilaterals, donors, and large corporates.
Lead strategic conversations on impact measurement, evidencebased policymaking, programme design, and value for money.
Translate technical impact findings into strategic insights and decisionoriented recommendations for senior policymakers and CXOs.
Build and manage longterm client relationships, including identifying followon opportunities.
3. Practice Development & Business Growth
Lead business development efforts for impact assessment and evaluation work, including shaping proposals, EOIs, technical approaches, and pricing strategies.
Support development and institutionalisation of new service offerings (e.g., impact evaluation frameworks, social value measurement, outcomebased financing support).
Contribute to thought leadership, market positioning, and visibility of PwC’s Impact Assessment capabilities.
Support Partners and Directors in defining the strategic direction and growth roadmap for the Impact Assessment practice.
4. People Leadership & Capability Building
Provide leadership and mentorship to Managers and broader teams, supporting capability development and progression.
Ensure strong quality assurance across teams through structured reviews, coaching, and feedback.
Build internal capability in impact assessment tools, evaluation standards, and analytical approaches, aligned with global best practices.
Foster a highperformance, inclusive, and collaborative team culture.
Required Qualifications & Experience
Education
Postgraduate degree in Economics, Public Policy, Development Studies, Statistics, Social Sciences, Public Health, or related fields.
Experience
12–15+ years of relevant professional experience in consulting, advisory, government, multilateral, or development sector organisations, with 2-4 experience in managerial roles.
Demonstrated experience leading largescale impact assessments, impact evaluations, or social/economic valuation assignments.
Proven track record of client leadership and business development in impact, policy, or economicsfocused consulting.
Prior experience in Big 4 firms, global consulting organisations, multilateral agencies, or reputed policy/impact advisory institutions is strongly preferred.
Technical Skills
Deep expertise in impact evaluation methodologies, research design, and applied data analysis.
Strong understanding of quantitative methods (econometrics, statistical and survey analysis), with the ability to guide teams on complex analytical work.
Ability to oversee and interpret analysis conducted using Stata, R, Python, SPSS, or Excel.
Strong understanding of public policy, social sector programmes, CSR/ESG impact measurement, and/or large public investments and infrastructure.
Core Consulting & Leadership Competencies
Strong strategic thinking and problemsolving capability.
Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to engage credibly with senior clients and stakeholders.
Proven ability to manage multiple engagements, senior stakeholders, and competing priorities.
Commercial acumen with a growthoriented mindset.
Leadership style that emphasises mentorship, collaboration, and quality.

Why PwC?
Engage in purpose-driven work that builds trust and drives change.
Access continuous learning opportunities paired with clear paths for career growth.
Enjoy a welcoming, inclusive culture and flexible work arrangements.
Sharpen your expertise and contribute towards impactful outcomes through meaningful projects.
Show more Show less

About the job

Operate at the center of execution for one of the fastest-scaling consumer app studios.
We are looking for a highly organized and proactive Executive Assistant to work closely with leadership at Triplespeed. In this role, you will act as a force multiplier—owning scheduling, coordination, communication, and key operational workflows that keep the company moving fast.

You will work directly with founders and senior leaders, helping manage priorities, streamline operations, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks.

If you are detail-oriented, highly reliable, and thrive in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment—this role is for you. 

About Triplespeed

Triplespeed is a fast-moving app studio that’s profitable, scaling multiple apps to >$10M/yr revenues, and growing fast.

We build and scale direct-to-consumer apps at speed, and operate with a lean, high-performance team. We are looking for someone who can help leadership stay focused, organized, and operating at peak efficiency.

Key Responsibilities 

Act as a strategic gatekeeper for the leadership team—prioritizing time, filtering opportunities, and ensuring focus on high-impact initiatives
Coordinate internal and external communications (email, Slack, follow-ups).
Prepare documents, reports, and summaries for meetings and decision-making.
Track tasks, action items, and ensure timely follow-ups across teams.
Assist in operational workflows including hiring coordination, vendor communication, and basic project management.
Handle confidential information with discretion and professionalism.
Organize travel, logistics, and day-to-day administrative tasks.
Support leadership in special projects and ad-hoc initiatives.
Identify inefficiencies and help improve internal processes.

Who Can Apply

Candidates with 1–5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant, Operations Associate, or similar role.
Strong organizational and time management skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Highly detail-oriented with strong follow-through.
Ability to multitask and prioritize in a fast-paced environment.
Comfortable working closely with senior leadership.
Based in Bangalore and available to work in-office.

Additional Candidate Preferences

Experience working in startups or high-growth environments.
Experience supporting founders or senior executives.
Strong problem-solving mindset and ability to work independently.
Familiarity with tools like Google Workspace, Notion, Slack, etc.
Ability to anticipate needs and act proactively.

Preferred Skills

Calendar & schedule management
Communication & coordination
Task tracking & follow-ups
Operations support
Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Calendar)
Notion / task management tools
Attention to detail
Problem-solving
Discretion & professionalism

What We Offer

Competitive Compensation: We offer a strong salary package benchmarked against top startups.
High Ownership: Work directly with founders and play a key role in company execution.
Fast-Paced Environment: Be part of a lean, high-performance team building and scaling products rapidly.
Career Growth: Opportunity to grow into Operations Manager / Chief of Staff roles over time.

Role: Chief of Staff - Program Operations
Join NIAT as Our Chief of Staff – Program Operations
Are you ready to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of tech education in India? At the NxtWave Institute of Advanced Technologies (NIAT), we're seeking a dynamic Chief of Staff to lead our Program Operations. This is your opportunity to be at the forefront of educational innovation, guiding the next generation of tech leaders. 
About NIAT
NIAT stands as NxtWave’s flagship four-year, on-campus program in Computer Science Education. Our curriculum is among India’s most advanced, meticulously aligned with industry demands. Headquartered in Hyderabad's vibrant tech landscape, our Hyderabad campus is surrounded by global giants like Google, Microsoft, Apple, Infosys, and TCS, offering our students unparalleled exposure to the world of technology. 
With our phenomenal success in Hyderabad’s first two cohorts, NIAT has proven to be the ultimate launchpad for future tech leaders. Now, we’re taking this revolution nationwide. This is your moment to step up, drive change, and be at the helm of an educational movement that’s shaping India’s tech landscape.
As we expand across the country, we need visionary leaders who can build, innovate, and elevate our impact to new heights. If you’re driven by a passion for student success and the power of cutting-edge education, this is your once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to be at the forefront of the next big wave in tech education.
Are you ready to lead this transformation? Join us and make history. 🚀
Why Join Us?
Innovate Education: Be part of a revolutionary institution that's redefining computer science education in India. 
Dynamic Environment: Work alongside CEO, top-tier management, mentors and industry professionals in a fast-paced, growth-oriented setting. 
Career Advancement: Seize opportunities for professional development and career growth within a pioneering educational ecosystem. 
Your Role
As the Chief of Staff – Program Operations, you will:
Strategize and Lead: Develop and implement strategies to enhance program operations, ensuring seamless collaboration across teams. 
Oversee Performance: Manage Profit & Loss (P&L) and lead cross-functional teams, aligning campus initiatives with our central program objectives. 
Drive Student Success: Collaborate with academic and student success teams to monitor and boost student progress and outcomes. 
Enhance Engagement: Continuously refine processes to elevate student engagement, improving pedagogy, content, and class delivery. 
Elevate Campus Experience: Lead initiatives to enrich the student experience, including campus branding and marketing efforts. 
Foster Industry Connections: Work closely with placement and training cells to establish industry partnerships, securing robust internship and placement opportunities. 
Ensure Operational Excellence: Oversee on-ground operations for assessments, events, and program-related activities to ensure the smooth execution of our curriculum. 
What We’re Looking For
Results-Driven Leader: You thrive in fast-paced environments and are committed to achieving excellence. 
Process Innovator: You balance long-term process improvements with short-term goal attainment. 
Student-Centric Mentor: You possess empathy towards students and are dedicated to fostering their success. 
Skilled Communicator: You have excellent communication and interpersonal skills, adept at engaging with multiple stakeholders. 
Qualified Professional: You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Strategy, or a related field. 
Strategic Thinker: Previous experience in strategy or business planning is a plus. 
Local Insight: Being a native of the assigned campus's geographic state is preferred, as we have campuses in Hyderabad, Pune, Kolhapur, Mangalore, Bangalore, Chennai, Vijayawada, Jaipur, and Delhi. 
Location & Work Details
Working Days: Monday to Saturday (6-day workweek) 
Work Timings: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM 
Compensation: Competitive CTC based on experience and qualifications 
Work Location: During training period(2-3 months) Hyderabad and post training on the assigned Campuses ( Hyderabad/ Chennai/ Bangalore / Pune / Delhi)
If you're passionate about mentoring and shaping student success, this is your opportunity to make an impact! Apply now and be a part of the NxtWave revolution!
Show more Show less

items.jobDescription:
Job Description: Portfolio Analyst 
Location: Hyderabad, India
Employment Type: Full-Time
Experience Level: 4-6 years

Position Overview
We are seeking a detail-oriented Portfolio Analyst who will play a critical part in driving strategic execution, OKR governance, executive reporting, and communications across the Infrastructure and Platforms organization. This role will be instrumental in ensuring day-to-day priorities are tracked, communicated and delivered with precision and clarity.
Key Responsibilities
Develop and maintain executive dashboards and reporting materials for Monthly Operating Reviews (MOR), Quarterly Business Reviews (QBR), and other leadership forums.
Craft and deliver strategic communications, including internal updates, leadership presentations, and stakeholder engagement materials.
Design and produce high-impact executive presentations, translating complex data into clear, compelling narratives.
Support OKR tracking and performance management across CTO service towers, ensuring alignment with strategic goals.
Provide program and project oversight by coordinating milestone tracking, risk/issue management across CTO Chapters.
Act as a trusted deputy to the Chief of Staff, flexing across planning, operations, and communications as needed.
Coordinate “CTO Wide” event planning such as Town Halls and Leadership offsite meetings.
Lead data collection and analysis across a variety of CTO work in partnership with IT Finance, HR, CTO Leadership, and Portfolio Leads.
Serve in a ‘utility role’, providing support as needed across the Office of the CTO.

Required Skills & Experience:
4-6 years of experience in project management, business operations, or executive communications within a technology or enterprise environment.
Proven ability to manage OKRs, performance metrics, and executive-level reporting.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with demonstrated experience in creating and delivering presentations to senior leadership.
Strong analytical and organizational skills; ability to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word); experience with Clarity, Apptio, or similar tools a plus.
High emotional intelligence and ability to influence without authority in a matrixed organization.

Nice to Have 
Experience with a Project Management or Chief of Staff role supporting senior leadership within a technology organization
Familiarity with Agile and Waterfall methodologies
Experience with financial planning, compliance tracking, or vendor coordination
What We Offer
Collaborative work environment with global teams.
Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits.
Continuous learning and growth opportunities in geospatial and risk analytics technologies.
Show more Show less

Job Description

Join our dynamic Business Management team where you will play a pivotal role in optimizing business performance. As a trusted advisor, you will help drive key initiatives and mitigate business risks. This is an exciting opportunity to work closely with Business/Group Heads and make a significant impact. Your expertise in project management, presentation development, and business analysis will be highly valued. Be part of a team that continually seeks to simplify, improve, and add value to existing business processes.As a Fraud Strategy Business Manager Senior Associate in Connected Commerce, you will be a key player in driving business performance and initiatives. You will act as a trusted advisor to Business/Group Heads, helping to identify, escalate, and mitigate business risks. Your role will involve organizing complex information strategically, managing projects, and delivering high-quality presentations and reports. Join us to make a meaningful impact and collaborate with global managers to enhance business processes.

Job Responsibilities

Provide high-level responsiveness to executive ad-hoc requests related to project management and business analysis.
Organize complex information strategically, demonstrating strong design acumen.
Create wireframes and mock-ups for management presentations and dashboards.
Communicate effectively with key business partners to drive next steps.
Project manage and deliver key work streams and tasks.
Identify key business risks and drive resolution of mitigating controls.
Support audit, regulatory, and compliance deliverables.
Coordinate team activities and prepare materials for town halls and strategy sessions.
Maintain internal resources and documentation on collaboration sites.
Participate in collaborative initiatives to simplify and improve business processes.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills

Experience in Business Management or Chief of Staff role.
Proven ability to deliver timely, high-quality presentations and reports.
Strong communication, organization, and project management skills.
Ability to articulate and demonstrate thoughtful rationale in design decisions.
Intermediate to advanced proficiency in MS Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint).
Self-motivated and able to work independently.
Excellent written and oral communication skills.
Strong time management and prioritization skills.

Same Posting Description for Internal and External Candidates

About Us

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

Our professionals in our Corporate Functions cover a diverse range of areas from finance and risk to human resources and marketing. Our corporate teams are an essential part of our company, ensuring that we’re setting our businesses, clients, customers and employees up for success. 
Show more Show less

Program Manager - Founder’s Office, Revspot - Immediate Joiners Only

This isn’t a coordination role.
This is the cockpit of a rocketship - and we need your hands on the throttle.

Revspot is building India’s first AI-native revenue engine - unifying intelligence, targeting, and activation for high-ticket B2C categories. We have grown 100× in 15 months, crossed $1M ARR, and are now scaling with speed and intent.

The Founder’s Office is where priorities are set, decisions are executed, and momentum is protected. This role sits at the center of execution - translating founder intent into outcomes across the company.

We are looking for a Program Manager – Founder’s Office who can operate as an extension of the Founder: structured, execution-focused, and deeply accountable.

What You Will Own

Founder Execution Support
Work directly with the Founder to convert priorities into action. Track, drive, and close high-impact initiatives across GTM, operations, and strategy.
Program & Execution Management
Own cross-functional programs end-to-end — define scope, align stakeholders, track progress, and ensure delivery without slippage.
Operating Rhythm
Help maintain a single execution cadence across teams. Follow up on commitments, surface risks early, and keep velocity high.
Business & Performance Visibility
Track key metrics across revenue, burn, hiring, and funnel performance. Ensure leadership has clear, timely visibility into what matters.
Investor & Strategic Support
Assist with fundraising materials, diligence coordination, and structured investor updates.
People & Process Enablement
Support hiring processes, onboarding programs, and internal operating systems that reinforce ownership and accountability.

Who This Role Is For

3–6 years of experience in startups, founder’s offices, consulting, strategy, or operations roles.
Strong executors who enjoy running complex programs, not just designing frameworks.
Comfortable operating in high-intensity, fast-changing environments.
Structured thinkers with strong follow-through and attention to detail.
People who ultimately want to grow into senior leadership or founder roles over time.
You value learning velocity and responsibility more than titles.

Why This Role

This role offers direct exposure to founder-level decision-making without the expectations or compensation structure of a Chief of Staff title.

You will:

Work on the most important initiatives in the company
Learn how companies are actually built and scaled
Develop judgment, execution muscle, and operating maturity

For the right person, this is a high-growth trajectory role, not a terminal position.

Compensation & Structure

Fixed Salary: ₹16–20 LPA
Variable Pay: ₹4–6 LPA, linked to clearly defined performance outcomes
ESOPs: Meaningful equity participation, structured for long-term alignment
Reporting To: Founder & CEO

Location & Work Style

Location: Bangalore (full-time, in-office)
Work Style: High ownership, high intensity, founder-speed environment
Show more Show less

Job Title: Strategic Executive Assistant & Project Management Officer (PMO) to CEO

Function: CEO Office

Experience: 8–12+ Years (EA / PMO / Strategy Execution)

Location: Matar Facility

Reporting To: Chief Executive Officer

Domain: BESS / Renewable Energy / Power / Infrastructure / Technology

Role Overview

The Strategic EA & Project Management to CEO will act as an extension of the CEO, supporting strategic planning, cross‑functional execution, and high‑impact projects, particularly in the BESS and renewable energy domain. This role goes beyond traditional EA responsibilities and requires strong business acumen, project governance expertise, and exceptional stakeholder management.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Support to CEO

Act as a trusted partner to the CEO in managing business priorities, strategic initiatives, and decision support.

Prepare board decks, strategic presentations, investor notes, and management reports.

Track and follow up on CEO‑level action items, ensuring closure across functions.

Conduct market, competitor, and sector-level research in BESS, energy storage, and renewable ecosystems.

Project & Program Management (CEO Office PMO)

Own And Manage CEO-sponsored Strategic Projects, Including

BESS expansion programs

New market entry initiatives

Strategic partnerships and JVs

Large EPC / infrastructure programs

Define project plans, milestones, success metrics, and governance frameworks.

Drive cross-functional alignment between Engineering, Projects, Finance, Legal, HR, and Commercial teams.

Identify risks, resolve bottlenecks, and escalate proactively to the CEO.

Stakeholder & Governance Management

Serve as the single point of coordination between the CEO and:

Board of Directors

Investors / external partners

CXOs and functional heads

Manage board meeting logistics, agenda planning, minutes, and follow-ups.

Ensure confidential handling of strategic and sensitive information at all times.

Business & Performance Analytics

Monitor organizational KPIs & OKRs, project dashboards, and business performance metrics.

Prepare weekly, monthly, and quarterly CEO review packs.

Support CEO in performance reviews and strategic assessments.

Executive & Administrative Excellence

Manage high-impact calendar planning, prioritization, and travel planning.

Filter information and ensure CEO engagement only on high-value, decision-centric matters.

Support crisis management and time-sensitive decision-making.

Professional Experience

Required Skills & Competencies

8–12+ years of experience as:

Strategic EA to CXO/CEO

PMO / Chief of Staff / Strategy Execution roles

Proven experience handling CEO office operations and cross-functional strategic initiatives.

Direct exposure to BESS, renewable energy, power, EPC, or infrastructure sectors (strongly preferred).

Core Skill Sets

Strong project management and execution discipline.

High business and financial acumen.

Excellent ability to understand technical & commercial discussions in BESS and energy storage.

Exceptional Communication, Presentation, And Documentation Skills.

Strong discretion, confidentiality, and judgment.

Personal Attributes

Strategic thinker with hands‑on execution mindset.

High ownership, accountability, and resilience.

Ability to work under ambiguity and pressure. 
Show more Show less

Job Title: Program Management Associate (PMA)
Location: NIAT Campuses (On-site)
Department: Academic Operations
Reporting To: Program Manager


Role Overview
The Program Management Associate (PMA) plays a pivotal role within NIAT’s academic operations ecosystem, functioning as a hybrid contributor across execution and program management support. This role is designed for individuals who can effectively manage day-to-day operations while demonstrating the ability to think strategically and contribute to continuous program improvement.
As a PMA, you will ensure seamless academic delivery, enhance student experience, and collaborate cross-functionally to drive operational excellence. This position serves as a strong foundation for career progression into Program Management and leadership roles within NIAT.


Key Responsibilities
1. Academic Operations & Execution
Develop and manage daily and weekly schedules for classes, labs, and assessments
Coordinate with academic teams to manage session availability on the Learning Management System (LMS)
Oversee content deployment across LMS environments (Beta, Gamma, Live), including updates and issue resolution
Track and maintain student data, including attendance, submissions, and engagement metrics
Execute communication campaigns using tools such as WebEngage for reminders, nudges, and announcements
Maintain structured trackers, dashboards, and reports using Google Sheets/Excel


2. Student Support & Campus Coordination
Act as the primary point of contact for student queries, ensuring timely and professional responses
Facilitate coordination between students, academic mentors, and internal support teams
Support execution of academic activities including assessments, workshops, placement drives, and campus events
Ensure smooth on-ground academic operations and student experience


3. Program Management & Strategic Support
Collaborate with Program Managers to support planning and execution of academic programs
Identify operational inefficiencies and recommend process improvements
Work cross-functionally with academic, product, content, and student success teams to resolve issues
Support branding initiatives and student engagement activities on campus
Coordinate with placement and training teams to streamline students’ transition into the workforce


Required Skills & Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in any discipline (Business, Commerce, Arts, Science, etc.)
Proficiency in Google Sheets/Excel (filters, basic formulas, data management)
Strong comfort with digital tools, dashboards, and LMS platforms
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
High attention to detail and strong organizational capabilities
Demonstrated ownership, accountability, and proactive execution mindset
Familiarity with AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT) for drafting communication and operational support is an added advantage


Key Competencies
Problem-solving and analytical thinking
Stakeholder management and collaboration
Time management and multitasking
Adaptability in a fast-paced environment


Career Growth & Opportunities
Structured progression path to Program Manager roles
Exposure to large-scale academic program operations
Opportunity to contribute to strategic initiatives and organizational growth
Long-term pathway toward leadership roles such as Chief of Staff – Program Operations


Why Join NIAT?
At NIAT, you will be part of a fast-growing academic ecosystem focused on innovation, student success, and operational excellence. This role provides a unique blend of execution and strategic exposure, enabling you to build a strong foundation in program management and leadership.
Show more Show less

items.jobTitle: Corporate Planning & Management - Vendor Management (TPRM) - Analyst - Bengaluru
items.jobDescription:
Divisional Overview

JOB DESCRIPTION 

The Corporate Planning & Management (CPM) division integrates financial planning, procurement, product management, and engineering teams to support strategic decisions across the firm.

CPM has 5 operating pillars.

Finance & Planning supports the execution of the firm’s strategic objectives through the management of the planning process, firmwide reporting and analytics and insights into the firm’s business plans and budgets. They develop consistent framework for revenue division projections creating transparency, accountability and efficiency around projections. This pillar also includes the CF&O, EO and Engineering divisional CFOs, who are strategic finance advisors helping the firm and the non-revenue divisions achieve commercial financial opportunities. 
Global Procurement Strategically enables the firm's engagement with third parties, ensuring robust commercial and compliance controls while driving sustainability. It optimizes value, fosters innovation, and mitigates risks across the end-to-end supply chain for services and non-compensation spend. Departments include Strategic Sourcing, Third Party Risk Management, Procure to Pay, Integrated Travel and Expense and Sustainable Operations. 
Product & Reporting Manages end-to-end product lifecycle and reporting capabilities for vendor-supplied and internally developed solutions. We empower CPM stakeholders with intuitive tools and actionable insights while ensuring solutions meet business needs and regulatory demands. 
The CPM Engineering team provides engineering solutions that enable the firm to manage third-party spend, data and automation, plan budgets, forecast financial scenarios, allocate expenses and support corporate decision making in-line with the firm’s strategic objectives.
CPM Management team includes senior leadership and teams that provide strategic oversight and operational support across the division. This pillar encompasses the Chief of Staff function and Communications and User Engagement team, which oversee change management and people strategies for CPM.

Role Overview

Professionals in CPM have an analytical mindset, exhibit intellectual curiosity, and are from diverse academic backgrounds. This role sits within Global Procurement.

The role requires collaboration with different functions across TPRM including, the firm on a regular basis, an ability to work independently, and frequent interaction with senior stakeholders. It also entails in-depth analysis and reporting for senior management, requiring diligence and a commercial mindset. The candidate is required to work closely with global counterparts and should have excellent verbal and written communication skills.

The Analyst role is based in Bangalore and will work across all TPRM capabilities, to ensure and support the delivery of TPRM’s run-the-bank book of work as well as TPRM’s transformation portfolio. Utilizing excellent program, design, and change management skills, along with a drive to become experts and lead our strategic transformation, TRPM aims to deliver various programs that meet divisional goals, adhere to global and regional regulations, and enhance the Firm’s TPRM risk landscape. TPRM Enablement team collaborates closely with senior leadership, functional owners, internal divisions, and Engineering to drive and achieve change, objectives, and key results.

The ideal analyst-level candidate for a Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM) program is a high-energy, multi-disciplinary professional who balances technical auditing skills with the operational discipline of a project manager and the proactive drive of a problem-solver. This individual acts as a "risk gatekeeper," a "process driver," and a "strategic communicator," ensuring that every vendor engagement is assessed thoroughly and efficiently, even when faced with ambiguity or resource constraints.

Job Responsibilities Will Include, But Are Not Limited To

Become a Subject Matter Expert of the TPRM Policies and Standards, including process governance and controls, risk management, vendor oversight requirements
Act as a key advisor to internal stakeholders and help address queries related to Policy and Standard requirements
Build strong business relationships with teammates and stakeholders firmwide to understand impact of the Program requirements based on the business and divisional initiatives
Partner with the team to support deliver on and further evolve the Quality Assurance Program
Participate and support timely responses to regulators, and 2nd Line reviews (e.g., Compliance, Internal Audit, Operational Risk) related to third party risk management
Identify and lead Program enhancements driven by evolving and changing regulatory guidance
Collaborate with team members, Product Enablement team, other stakeholders and external vendors to design, test and implement new solutions or enhancements to existing processes and systems
Be engaged and help drive key TPRM projects for continuous process improvement and bring innovation into the team
Lead initiatives / projects from inception to completion
Communicate progress and decision points for projects to relevant functions and maintain key documentation of approvals where required
Coordinate content for internal senior forums and business engagement meetings (e.g., Committees, Governance Groups, etc.)
Oversight of business-as-usual Program components (i.e, Program Modifications, Policy Exclusion Categories, Non Standard Payments)

Qualifications

Bachelor’s in risk management, or Finance; 2-4 years in audit or TPRM. 
Vigilant, inquisitive, and detail-oriented with a focus on "trust but verify." Experience in front to back process mapping and future state process re-designing for large scale process transformation projects
Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Project & Visio or other business process management software
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills both written and oral; confident in interaction with all levels of employees and navigating organizations
Ability to rapidly assimilate complex concepts and form practical solutions.
Ability to break down complex processes into tasks and challenge status-quo
Be highly collaborative, team orientated and a strong consensus builder 
Self-motivated and well organized. Proven track record: ability to handle multiple tasks, prioritize accordingly and make decisions as required
Strong background in product management and strategy and good numerical analytical ability
Technical and operational problem-solving skills
Proactive, enthusiastic and team-oriented approach

About Goldman Sachs

At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.

We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.

We're committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html 
Show more Show less
