Executive Assistant to Founder & Managing Director
Premium Finlease Pvt. Ltd. | Hisar, Haryana | Full-time, On-site

About the role
We're looking for an Executive Assistant who can be the right hand to the Founder & MD overseeing two companies, including an RBI-registered NBFC with multiple branches. This is not a calendar-only role. You'll move between classic EA work (inbox, travel, scheduling), light HR/ops coordination, project follow-throughs across both businesses, and finance/NBFC compliance support. If you like wearing multiple hats, working closely with a decision-maker, and seeing your work directly impact the business — read on.

What you'll own
- Calendar, inbox, travel, and meeting prep for the MD — keeping the day tight and the priorities clear
- Acting as the first filter for incoming requests, vendors, and internal teams
- Driving follow-ups across departments and branches so decisions don't fall through the cracks
- Coordinating with HR on hiring, onboarding, KPI tracking, and employee communication
- Supporting NBFC compliance workflows — document tracking, audit prep, regulatory filings coordination (you won't own compliance, but you'll keep it moving)
- Preparing briefing notes, MIS summaries, and decks for board, investor, and partner meetings
- Managing confidential information with absolute discretion
- Running point on special projects the MD assigns — from system rollouts to vendor negotiations

What we're looking for
- 4–7 years as an EA, Chief of Staff (junior), or in a similar role supporting a founder, CXO, or business owner
- Comfort with multi-entity environments — bonus if you've worked with an NBFC, bank, or financial services firm
- Advanced PowerPoint skills — can build investor-grade decks from scratch, not just plug content into templates
- Strong Excel — comfortable with pivots, lookups, conditional formatting, dashboards, and basic financial modelling
- Hands-on with Claude — uses it daily for drafting, summarising long documents, research, decks, and speeding up routine work; knows how to write good prompts and get useful output
- Sharp written and spoken English and Hindi
- High emotional intelligence — knows when to push, when to wait, and how to read the room
- Treats deadlines like commitments, not suggestions
- Discreet, organised, and unfazed by shifting priorities

Nice to have
- Exposure to HR systems, KPI dashboards, or compliance/audit processes
- Working knowledge of basic finance (P&L, MIS reading)
- Experience handling external stakeholders — auditors, lawyers, bankers, regulators
- Familiarity with Claude Projects, Claude in Excel, or other Claude-powered workflows

What you'll get
- Direct access to the MD and decision-making at the top
- Exposure to two businesses, multiple verticals, and the inner workings of an NBFC
- A role that grows into Chief of Staff for the right person
- Compensation aligned with experience and capability

Show more Show less

About Us

This form is always open and we respond to everyone within 72 hours.

Team: Less than 5 folks
Office: Bangalore; few part-time remote roles

Open Roles

Passively looking for our first growth hire
Not looking but always open to work with entrepreneurial engineers/designers
Not looking for roles like PM, Consultant, Chief of Staff, etc
Very rarely looking for generalist interns

How We Work

Less meetings, more work: We do just one scheduled meeting/week. Every day, everyone drops an async standup update on Slack. Whoever misses the update has to send a video version in commentary format to entertain others.
Broad roles: We like to keep roles broad by default. This means an engineer could be writing product docs, doing demo calls, etc. A UX designer could be creating logos or videos. We look for at least one spike and leave the rest fuzzy.
Work trials: We do a paid 1 week work trial before hiring so that both of us can experience what it's like working together before committing. Some people in less demanding full-time roles have continued to work with us for longer periods or multiple short stints because they enjoy it.

How We Hire

Fill the form. It's intentionally a little long because the founder personally reads and responds to everyone within 72 hours irrespective of the decision.
If we like you, we might ask some follow-up questions and quickly get on a call.
That's it! If the call goes well, we roll out the work-trial offer.

Role Summary

This role leads IT-wide planning, governance, and communications to ensure the CIO’s priorities translate into measurable business outcomes.

We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Gurugram for our hybrid working model.

Key Responsibilities

Lead IT Strategic Planning & Governance
Drives the definition of IT OKRs, quarterly business reviews, and multi-year strategic planning cadences to align priorities and track value delivery
Drive Executive Decision Enablement
Build insights, narratives, and recommendations that inform CIO/leadership decisions, including board materials and enterprise updates
Orchestrate Cross-Functional Initiatives
Coordinate strategic programs that span pillars, ensuring ownership clarity, risk management, and measurable outcomes
Evolve IT Operating Model
Deploy “run IT like a product” standards across agile delivery, resource planning, intake, and change management
Champion Operational Excellence

Qualifications

8–10 years in technology strategy, PMO leadership, or chief-of-staff–style roles, ideally within a CIO’s office
Experience partnering with senior executives and driving cross-functional impact
Strong strategic thinking, communication, and organizational leadership skills
Familiarity with product-centric delivery, agile, and enterprise planning tooling

About MongoDB

MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.

Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.

To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world!

MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.

MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer.

Role Overview 
You will be the execution backbone of MyOperator’s Founder’s Office. Your job is to take ambiguous, high-priority ideas from the CRO and COO, convert them into structured plans with clear owners and timelines, and drive them to completion across sales, operations, and product teams — without requiring constant follow-ups.

On any given day, you might be diagnosing a conversion drop-off in the lead-to-revenue funnel, building a multi-step n8n workflow that connects CRM events to Google Chat alerts, deploying an AI tool to auto-generate weekly pipeline reports, or restructuring a Zoho CRM module to fix data hygiene issues. You will own the systems that run the business — CRM, automations, dashboards, integrations — and use AI tooling to eliminate repetitive manual work.
This is not a traditional project management role. You will work across every team, report directly to founders, and be expected to diagnose and fix problems — not escalate them. High visibility, high autonomy, high accountability.

About MyOperator
MyOperator is a Business AI Operator platform that allows businesses, teams, and AI Agents to work in tandem for customer operations, i.e., handle Sales, Support, Escalation, Feedback, and Refund processes. With over 12,000+ businesses using our platform, we are the largest in the space.MyOperator is built for people who want to work on ambitious problems at a meaningful scale. We value ownership, speed, critical thinking, and a bias for building things that create real customer and business outcomes. This is a high-expectation, high-learning environment where people are trusted to think independently, challenge ideas openly, move with urgency, and keep raising the bar as we build for long-term impact.

Key Responsibility Areas
Founder Execution Engine — Receive ambiguous directives from CRO/COO, break them into task lists with owners, timelines, and success criteria. Track completion across teams (sales, ops, product) and close loops without needing follow-up from founders.
Revenue Operations Monitoring — Monitor the lead-to-revenue funnel end-to-end: track lead sources, conversion rates at each stage (MQL → SQL → Demo → Close), identify drop-off points, and recommend specific process or workflow changes to reduce time-to-close.
CRM Ownership & Data Hygiene — Own Zoho CRM configuration and adoption. Set up and maintain pipeline stages, custom fields, and reporting views. Identify data gaps, flag adoption issues across sales teams, and drive improvements so the CRM is the single source of truth for all deal activity.
Automation & Integration — Build multi-step automation workflows using n8n (or equivalent). Connect CRM events to email notifications, follow-up sequences, reporting triggers, and cross-tool data syncs. Read API documentation to troubleshoot and extend integrations between tools.
AI Enablement — Identify repetitive tasks across sales and ops (report generation, data compilation, meeting summaries) and deploy AI tools (GPT-based, Claude, or custom scripts) to automate them. Measure time saved and output quality.
Real-Time Dashboards — Build and maintain dashboards (Zoho Analytics, Google Sheets, or equivalent) that give founders live visibility into: deal velocity, funnel conversion rates, team activity, and execution status of founder-initiated projects.
Cross-Team Problem Solving — Step into broken processes across sales, support, and ops teams. Diagnose the root cause, implement a fix, and document the solution — without escalating back to founders.

Requirements — Must Have
2–4 years of experience in business operations, revenue operations, or a founder’s office / Chief of Staff role at a B2B SaaS or tech company.
Hands-on CRM experience with Zoho CRM (preferred), Salesforce, or HubSpot — including pipeline setup, custom field configuration, report building, and data hygiene enforcement.
Built and deployed multi-step automation workflows using n8n, Zapier, or Make — not just single-action triggers. Can demonstrate at least 2 workflows with conditional logic and cross-tool data passing.
Can read API documentation, understand request/response structures (JSON, REST), and independently troubleshoot integration issues between SaaS tools without requiring engineering support.
Advanced Excel / Google Sheets proficiency — pivot tables, VLOOKUP/INDEX-MATCH, array formulas, and building operational trackers that teams actually use daily.
Has built at least one dashboard or tracker that was actively used by leadership for decision-making (will be asked for a live walkthrough or screenshots during the interview).

Requirements — Good to Have
Direct reporting experience to founders, CXOs, or C-suite executives in a prior role.
Hands-on use of AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or custom GPT workflows) for actual work output — not just experimentation. Can show specific examples of AI-augmented deliverables.
Working understanding of B2B SaaS funnel metrics — lead generation, MQL/SQL definitions, attribution models, and how they connect to revenue.
Basic design sense for landing pages, internal wikis, or user journey maps (using Notion, Figma, or similar).
Prior experience at an early-stage or high-growth company (Series A–C, sub-500 employees) where scope was earned through initiative, not assigned through hierarchy.

This Profile Is Not For
People who need fully defined, fixed daily responsibilities and are uncomfortable when priorities shift based on founder directives.
People who escalate problems to managers instead of diagnosing root causes and implementing fixes themselves.
People uncomfortable with APIs, unfamiliar SaaS tools, or learning new technical systems quickly and independently.
Show more Show less

items.jobDescription:
About Astellas

Astellas is a global life sciences company committed to turning innovative science into VALUE for patients. We provide transformative therapies in disease areas that include oncology, ophthalmology, urology, immunology and women's health. Through our research and development programs, we are pioneering new healthcare solutions for diseases with high unmet medical need. Learn more at Astellas.com.

Are you driven to make a real difference in the lives of patients?

We're seeking passionate individuals who thrive in dynamic environments, embrace new ideas, and aren't afraid to take intelligent risks. People who act with unwavering integrity and are deeply committed to making a tangible impact.

Purpose And Scope

Works with Transformation Managing Principals and Principals on one or more strategic initiatives within a CxO Function. Lead significant workstreams of a large strategic initiative or a small strategic initiative. Supports the Transformation Office leadership team in the development and building of critical transformation capabilities for the successful execution of Astellas’ transformation agenda. Coaches more junior members of the team.

Responsibilities And Accountabilities

Proven experience in developing and executing communication strategies, preferably in a global or multi-location context. Strong understanding of internal and external communication channels, including social media and digital platforms. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messages for diverse audiences. Strong project management skills and the ability to work collaboratively across teams and locations. Proficiency in SharePoint and other digital communication tools.

General: Working with Transformation Managing Principals and Principals, supports in the identification and scoping of transformation opportunities that contribute to the delivery of our CSP goals Works with Transformation Managing Principals and Principals on one or more strategic initiatives within a CxO Function (or enterprise-wide as needed)

Lead significant workstreams of a large strategic initiative or small strategic initiatives, manage all resources involved (people and non-payroll budget) Supports in the identification and building of coalition of key stakeholders and sponsors to help deliver such strategic initiatives Takes and active role in the coordination with other transformation initiatives to ensure alignment and in the delivery to initiatives targets, flagging deviations prior to them occurring where needed Supports the Transformation Office leadership team in the development and building of one or more transformation capabilities Supports the Transformation Office leadership team in the development and execution of the Transformation Office strategy Coach other junior team members globally as required Work in accordance with Transformation Office standards Contribution to achieve CSP Performance Goals by working on one or more strategic initiatives within a CxO Function that delivers part of the annual operational cost saving or revenue increase committed to deliver our CSP goals.

Role Specific

Lead the Engagement strategy across our three GCC locations: India, Poland, and Mexico. This role will focus on enhancing internal and external stakeholder engagement, maintaining our GCC SharePoint site, and developing a cohesive brand and communication strategy that resonates across diverse markets.

Key Stakeholders

Internal: CxO and Chief of Staff, CxO sponsors, CF Division Heads, TO Head, Teams within strategic initiatives. External: Supporting consultants as applicable

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Change Management, Public Relations, Marketing, or a related field. Extensive experience in transformation execution with a proven track record of leading global transformational change initiatives within complex organisations. Ideally a background in pharmaceutical industry.

Preferred Qualifications

A master's degree is preferred.

8+ years of transformation execution experience with a proven track record of delivering transformational change initiatives within complex organizations.

Good sense of both the strategic context for Astellas as well as the operational challenges of executing strategy well Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams or small initiatives simultaneously and deliver results within specified timelines and budgets.

Strong change management skills, consistent past delivery of varied types of projects in relevant areas of the business Ability to recognize complex situations and use multiple formal and informal methods to find solutions, as well as the networking and influencing skills to drive change in senior and mid-level leaders

Ability to present complex issues to top management and contribute to discussions in order to drive decision making Proven experience in driving change and managing resistance within an organization.

Ability to navigate ambiguity and adapt to a rapidly changing business environment.

Preferred

Knowledge of pharmaceutical industry is highly desirable.

Knowledge of emerging technologies and digital transformation trends is highly desirable.

Working Environment

This position is based in India and will require on-site work each week.

(OR) At Astellas we recognize the importance of work/life balance, and we are proud to offer a hybrid working solution allowing time to connect with colleagues at the office with the flexibility to also work from home. We believe this will optimize the most productive work environment for all employees to succeed and deliver. Hybrid work from certain locations may be permitted in accordance with Astellas’ Responsible Flexibility Guidelines.

What awaits you at Astellas?

Global collaboration: Become part of a connected global business of like-minded life science leaders, all dedicated to improving patients' lives worldwide.
Real-world patient impact: Contribute to transformative therapies that reach patients around the world, knowing your work makes a difference every day.
Relentless Innovation: Join a company at the forefront of scientific breakthroughs, where you'll have the opportunity to shape the future of healthcare.
A Culture of Growth: Chart your own course within a supportive environment that values your contributions, champions your development, and empowers you to pursue your passions.

Our Organizational Values and Behaviors

Values : Innovation, Integrity and Impact sit at the heart of what we do.

Behaviors : We come together as ‘One Astellas’, working with courage

Category

Astellas is committed to equality of opportunity in all aspects of employment.

EOE including Disability/Protected Veterans

We’re not just looking for an assistant—we’re looking for a sharp thinker and smart operator who understands business, stays updated with the world, communicates with clarity, and is comfortable navigating technology.

This role sits at the center of the Founder’s Office, working closely on priorities, decisions, and day-to-day execution. If you are someone who asks the right questions, understands context quickly, and uses tech to get things done faster and smarter, this role is for you.

What You’ll Do

About The Access Group
The Access Group is one of the largest UK-headquartered business management software providers. It provides solutions that empower more than 128,000 small and mid-sized organisations in commercial and non-profit sectors across Europe, USA, and APAC, giving every employee the freedom to do more of what’s important. Its innovative cloud solutions and integrated AI software experience across multiple Access products transform how business technology is used. Access employs over 7,000 people, continuously driving product innovation and customer service excellence.

About STAAH
STAAH works with more than 20,000+ hotel properties and accommodation providers across 90 countries through its operations in New Zealand, Australia, India, Malaysia, Indonesia, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam, UK and Europe. STAAH has built a strong reputation for enabling accommodation providers to connect to over 500 online travel agents (OTAs) and technology partners.

PRODUCT MARKETING LEAD
The Product Marketing Lead will be responsible for the messaging and positioning of the STAAH and SU Product portfolio, eventually leading to better engagement and growth in revenue.

Job Title: Lead - Product Marketing and Growth

Location: India | Department: Marketing

Job Description
Key requirements: The requirement is for a Product Marketing Specialist – STAAH and Su.

Customer segmentation and messaging: Gather market intelligence and refine product messaging in collaboration with product experts, partner services and business development teams.
Product Benefits & Features: Articulate technical features and product capabilities into concise and simple language for communicating it to the hoteliers and PMS partners. Focus on sharpening product messaging, after understanding the effective way of using a certain feature and listing the benefits for the business and users.

Product marketing assets: Build and update marketing assets for each product line, gauge what’s working and what’s not working, and what’s being used by the sales team. Responsible for building product videos, infographics, product brief documents, brochures, white papers, eBooks, etc. along with the Creative team.

Product demand generation: Collaborate with demand gen teams to help them build the right content and messaging for competitive positioning of our on-going campaigns and Ads (Google, Facebook, LinkedIn). Developing content for emailers, landing pages, and social media banners.
Engagement and Dissemination of Content: Responsible for featuring key product attributes and highlights on the website, Newsletters, Blog, Social channels and PR.

Competitive intelligence: Develop in-depth competitive intelligence through SEM Rush and other available tools, ensuring that it can be easily consumed by sales and partner teams, with the primary aim to improve the conversion rate. 

Product launches/new releases: Work closely with cross-functional teams to assist with new product releases/ launches, ensuring that GTM strategy is in place. Also, the stakeholders and channels are well versed with new messaging and positioning. 

Product Development & Roadmap: Collaborate with the product and tech teams to stay in sync with Product roadmap, enhancements and possibly build a calendar of product updates. Accordingly, plan and schedule campaigns across channels.

Professional experience requirements:
Bachelor’s Degree or ideally an MBA with at least 5 years of professional experience
At least two years of experience in B2B Marketing
Experience in market/competitive intelligence/market research will be an advantage
Strong communication - writing skills and presentation skills
Understanding of Travel & Hospitality space will be an added advantage.
Technical, Problem-solving attitude to learn and grow with experience.

Role summary

We’re hiring a Brand Manager to manage and own the end-to-end relationship with premium global consumer electronics brands we represent in India. This is an end-to-end commercial role — you will lead strategy, forecasting, Go to market in Audio Category, SOP’s, ecommerce, retail activations, marketing, launches and post-sales coordination. You will act as the primary SPOC (Single point of contact) and domain bridge between brand HQ, Country Management team and our India teams.

What you will own (Responsibilities)

Strategy, Planning & Enablement
Own the annual brand P&L, GTM roadmap, seasonal plans, content, updates, reviews, brand liaising and pricing strategy in consultation with the Promoters.
Define target segments, shopper journeys and premium positioning for India.
Act as enabler for brand take-off and SPOC for strategic brand coordination.

Commercial, Channel & Stakeholder Management
Manage distributor relationships, B2B partners, and large retail/omnichannel accounts with the Sales team.
Drive distribution expansion across modern retail, specialty stores, and online retailers; onboard and manage key accounts.
Own trade promotions, credit note processes, incentives, and reconciliations in partnership with Finance.
Coordinate cross-functional teams including Sales, E-commerce, Marketing, L&D, Finance, Supply Chain, Operations, and Service, acting as SPOC across internal stakeholders.
Lead collaboration with external agencies (creative, media, PR, merchandising, influencers) and oversee vendor performance as SPOC for external partners.

Mystery Shopping
Plan and execute mystery shopping exercises across retail touchpoints in collaborations with Marketing and Training team.
Execute customer experience, staff interaction, and ensure compliance in conformation with brand guidelines.
Identify gaps in service delivery and retail execution with an aim towards suggesting corrective action.
Share insights with relevant teams to drive improvements and maintain premium brand standards.

Ecommerce & Performance Marketing
Collaborate with the E-com team to own marketplace strategy (assortment, A+ content, DSP/ads, deal participation) as SPOC for brand e-commerce initiatives.

Product Launches & Merchandising
Lead product launch planning and execution (timelines, inventory, retailer readiness, collateral, PR, launch events) with cross-functional support.
Implement in-store merchandising standards, visual merchandising, and retail staff training via the sales/merchandising team & audits.
Own & maintain demo inventory for the various purposes.
Act as SPOC for product launch coordination with HQ and partners

Operations & After-sales
Conduct random customer calls for problem identification and suggest suitable measures for correction.
Manage RMA coordination with service.
Maintenance of tech specs and the relevant statutory and regulatory paperwork.

Reporting & Analytics
Deliver weekly/monthly revenue forecasts, SKU-level sell-through, inventory days, channel margins and campaign ROI with the help o f BI.
Prepare board-ready presentations for HQ and internal leadership as SPOC for brand reporting and escalations.

Success metrics (KPIs)
YoY revenue growth for brand(s).
Improvement in gross & channel margins.
Sell-through rate and inventory turns.
Marketing ROI (ROAS) and campaign conversion lifts.
On-time product launches & channel readiness.
Reduction in after-sales escalations; improved CSAT.
Mystery shopping scores and retail compliance metrics.

Required experience & skills
08 – 10+ years in brand management / commercial roles within consumer electronics, premium lifestyle, sports tech, or FMCG/retail.
Strong commercial skills: forecasting, pricing, P&L ownership, advanced Excel, Power Point & Canva.
Hands-on in product launches, trade promotions and retail activations — with experience delegating and coordinating across teams.
Analytical mindset — comfortable using sales analytics, GA, and CRM basics.
Willingness to travel domestically for Recces, rollouts, and events.

Reporting & location
Reports to: Head of Business / Director (with dotted line to head of marketing).
Location: New Delhi (office-first) with frequent domestic travel.
Individual contributor; Cross-functional role — manages agencies and coordinates countrywide Sales/E-com teams.

What we offer
High-impact ownership of premium global brands in India.
Autonomy to shape strategy with strong cross-functional support.
Fast-paced environment with scope to build processes and grow the team.
Competitive salary + performance incentives + travel allowance.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Gurugram, Haryana, India; Mumbai, Maharashtra, India; Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
4 years of experience in marketing working across one or more marketing fields (i.e., growth, product marketing, brand marketing, social).
Experience managing cross-functional or cross-team projects.

Preferred qualifications:

Experience working in one or more of the following fields: growth, product, brand, social, or partner marketing or research.
Experience with marketing and sales operations, financial analytics, or other related field.
Experience in consulting and working with behavioural marketing data.
Experience working on projects with external stakeholders.

About The Job

Whether you're on a consumer product (like Gmail, Search, Maps, Chrome, Android) or a business product (Google Ads, AdSense, Google Marketing Platform, Analytics), you take part in a complete marketing experience as you lead every facet of the product's journey. From determining positioning, naming, competitive analysis, feature prioritization and external communications, you help shape the product and help it grow a consumer base. This means you work with a cross-functional team across Sales, corporate communications, legal, webmasters, product development, engineering and more. In this role, you'll be involved with product marketing strategy from beginning to end.

As a Product Marketing Manager for Marketing Special Projects, Partnerships, and Analytics, you will be the strategic glue for our marketing organization. You will be responsible for institutionalizing a data-driven culture, leading high-priority "special projects" that require agile cross-functional navigation, and managing key strategic partnerships. You will work at the intersection of business strategy and execution, defining the analytical frameworks that inform our decision-making, while ensuring our partnerships are not just operational, but transformative. You will collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Sales teams to align marketing activities with overall business objectives, utilizing advanced measurement techniques to prove impact and optimize Return on Investment (ROI).

Responsibilities

Lead and own high-priority, cross-functional initiatives using a problem solving mindset. Develop project plans, define success metrics, and drive execution with agility to bring order to complex, ambiguous challenges.
Define and implement robust measurement frameworks (Key Performance Indicator (KPI) selection, incrementality, and full-funnel attribution) to evaluate the success of our marketing investments.
Develop and manage strategic co-marketing partnerships. Craft and execute joint go-to-market strategies that leverage partner ecosystems to hypercharge reach and engagement.
Manage the end-to-end partnership lifecycle, from campaign execution and to performance reporting.
Act as the primary liaison within marketing and internal stakeholders. Share actionable, incisive findings to generate buy-in and guide product/brand development that best serves the unique nuances of the Indian market.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

items.jobTitle: Hyderabad/Bangalore - Market Research – Brand Research & Insights - Assistant Manager
items.jobDescription:
Summary

Position Summary 

Assistant Manager: Market Research – Brand Research & Insights

Looking for candidates who can support strategic marketers with advanced analytics and data-driven insights, with experience in interpreting and validating research data, especially from surveys. This role will be central to designing, implementing, analyzing, and validating research surveys that inform marketing and brand strategy. The ideal candidate will be highly skilled in survey methodologies, experienced with data validation and statistical testing, and passionate about distilling meaningful, actionable findings from diverse data sources.

Key skills and professional experience:

Overall, 7+ years’ experience with at least 5+ years of relevant data science, statistical analysis, or marketing analytics experience, ideally with exposure to U.S./Global marketers or professional services.
Proven expertise in survey design (e.g., questionnaire development, sampling strategy, logic flows) and hands-on experience with survey platforms such as Qualtrics, Decipher, or similar.
Advanced skills in statistical analysis of survey data, including detailed cross-tabs, custom data cuts, weighting, and conducting/explaining statistical significance tests (t-tests, chi-square, etc.).
Hands-on experience with standard survey statistical packages such as SPSS and SAS, and familiar with qualitative primary research, AU innovation
Working knowledge of data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) and ability to translate research results into compelling charts, dashboards, and visual stories.
Familiarity with secondary research databases (EIU, EMIS, Gartner, IDC) is advantageous.
Experience with Generative AI tools and techniques (e.g., large language models, prompt engineering, or AI-driven analytics) is a strong plus.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to synthesize large datasets, spot trends, and draw logical inferences.
Strong project management skills—ability to scope, plan, execute, and deliver analytics projects on time.
Collaborative and team-oriented, with a proven ability to manage multiple activities and competing demands simultaneously.
Strong communication skills, able to translate technical findings into actionable business insights, and present them to non-technical stakeholders.
Attention to detail and quality, with a track record of ensuring data integrity and actionable recommendations.

Work you’ll do

As a Market Research Specialist, you will drive primary research initiatives that support brand and marketing objectives. Your expertise will shape strategic decision-making through rigorous survey design, thoughtful analysis, and clear communication of findings.

Key job responsibilities include:

Survey Research and Analytics

Design, program, and field end-to-end surveys for brand tracking, customer/market insights, and campaign strategy.
Conduct detailed data cuts and analyze survey datasets to uncover patterns, trends, and key drivers.
Perform statistical significance testing to validate research insights; clearly communicate implications and confidence levels to marketing and business leaders.
Ensure data quality through meticulous cleaning and validation; manage sampling, quotas, and response tracking.
Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business objectives into effective survey instruments and research plans.

Data Interpretation and Visualization

Synthesize and interpret quantitative and qualitative data, generating actionable insights for strategy and reporting.
Produce visual outputs—dashboards, presentations, infographics—to distill complex analyses into easily understood narratives.
Stay current with innovations in survey research (AU innovation, tools, methodologies) to enhance research outcomes.

Collaboration and Project Management

Serve as the subject matter specialist for survey analytics within the team, supporting knowledge sharing and capability building.
Liaise with marketers, researchers, and leadership to align survey projects with strategic priorities.
Manage multiple projects end-to-end; scope, plan, and deliver research requests efficiently.

Day-to-day responsibilities:

Collaborating with teams leads to managing workflow, ensuring adequate resources, and supporting work requests.
Contribute to knowledge sharing and continuous improvement of analytics capabilities within the team.

The team

The Brand Research & Insights (BR&I) team is part of the broader Brand Marketing & Communications team. The USI BR&I team supports strategic marketers in developing and implementing effective marketing strategies and plans across industries, regions, and functions. The team provides a wide range of services, which help the Business Chief Marketing Officers, Strategic Marketers, and Partners, Principals, and Directors (PPDs) expand client relationships and build Deloitte’s presence and reputation in the market. It develops marketing and research-based insights through competitive intelligence reports, executive profiles, persona-based research, event analysis, research material, and marketing brochures to equip leadership teams to drive strategic initiatives and win business for the firm.

Qualifications

Required: Master’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.

Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Mathematics/ B. Tech

Location: Hyderabad, Bangalore

Shift Timings: 2pm to 11pm

How you’ll grow

At Deloitte, we’ve invested a great deal to create a rich environment in which our professionals can grow. We want all our people to develop in their own way, playing to their own strengths as they hone their leadership skills. And, as a part of our efforts, we provide our professionals with a variety of learning and networking opportunities—including exposure to leaders, sponsors, coaches, and challenging assignments—to help accelerate their careers along the way. No two people learn in exactly the same way. So, we provide a range of resources, including live classrooms, team-based learning, and eLearning. DU: The Leadership Center in India, our state-of-the-art, world-class learning Center in the Hyderabad offices, is an extension of the Deloitte University (DU) in Westlake, Texas, and represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to our people’s growth and development. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India

Benefits

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Deloitte’s culture

Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy,

centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture that is inclusive, invites authenticity, leverages our diversity, and where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives.

Learn more about Life at Deloitte.

Corporate citizenship

Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people, and our communities. We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities. Learn more about Deloitte’s impact on the world.

#BM&C_G&P

Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Recruiting tips

From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Gurugram, Haryana, India; Mumbai, Maharashtra, India; Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
4 years of experience in marketing working across one or more marketing fields (i.e., growth, product marketing, brand marketing, social).
Experience managing cross-functional or cross-team projects.

Preferred qualifications:

Experience working in one or more of the following fields: growth, product, brand, social, or partner marketing or research.
Experience with marketing and sales operations, financial analytics, or other related field.
Experience in consulting and working with behavioural marketing data.
Experience working on projects with external stakeholders.

About The Job

Whether you're on a consumer product (like Gmail, Search, Maps, Chrome, Android) or a business product (Google Ads, AdSense, Google Marketing Platform, Analytics), you take part in a complete marketing experience as you lead every facet of the product's journey. From determining positioning, naming, competitive analysis, feature prioritization and external communications, you help shape the product and help it grow a consumer base. This means you work with a cross-functional team across Sales, corporate communications, legal, webmasters, product development, engineering and more. In this role, you'll be involved with product marketing strategy from beginning to end.

As a Product Marketing Manager for Marketing Special Projects, Partnerships, and Analytics, you will be the strategic glue for our marketing organization. You will be responsible for institutionalizing a data-driven culture, leading high-priority "special projects" that require agile cross-functional navigation, and managing key strategic partnerships. You will work at the intersection of business strategy and execution, defining the analytical frameworks that inform our decision-making, while ensuring our partnerships are not just operational, but transformative. You will collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Sales teams to align marketing activities with overall business objectives, utilizing advanced measurement techniques to prove impact and optimize Return on Investment (ROI).

Responsibilities

Lead and own high-priority, cross-functional initiatives using a problem solving mindset. Develop project plans, define success metrics, and drive execution with agility to bring order to complex, ambiguous challenges.
Define and implement robust measurement frameworks (Key Performance Indicator (KPI) selection, incrementality, and full-funnel attribution) to evaluate the success of our marketing investments.
Develop and manage strategic co-marketing partnerships. Craft and execute joint go-to-market strategies that leverage partner ecosystems to hypercharge reach and engagement.
Manage the end-to-end partnership lifecycle, from campaign execution and to performance reporting.
Act as the primary liaison within marketing and internal stakeholders. Share actionable, incisive findings to generate buy-in and guide product/brand development that best serves the unique nuances of the Indian market.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Company Overview

Reliance Retail is India's largest and fastest-growing retailer, known for its diverse omni-channel presence through integrated store concepts and digital commerce platforms. Since its inception in 2006, the company has committed to revolutionizing Indian retail, offering superior quality products and unmatched shopping experiences. With a loyal customer base of over 193 million and a vast network of more than 15,000 stores, Reliance Retail continues to enhance customer value and strengthen its market presence.

Reliance Retail is seeking a mid-level professional for the position of DM Fashion. This full-time role is based in Balasore, Bhadrak, Bhubaneswar, Koraput, and Jeypore. The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of digital marketing strategies and be capable of driving our fashion segment through innovative marketing techniques. Join our team to leverage your skills in a collaborative environment, aiming to create impactful strategies that resonate with our customers.

This Regional Marketing Role will be accountable for executing a comprehensive marketing strategy aligned with global business objectives. This individual will oversee regional marketing teams within a sales region, enabling the organization to gather insights into regional market dynamics, activate campaigns, enable the sales teams, and nurture key customer relationships. They will work in close collaboration with global CDG marketing and regional sales leadership to drive growth and achieve business goals. They will guide the regional marketing organization toward success by coaching and developing regional marketing talent.

How You Will Make An Impact

Oversees the marketing for India & neighbouring markets in collaboration in sales.
Lead the execution of the Global CDG Marketing Strategy within the selling region. 
Uses acquired expertise to analyze and solve problems to achieve business objectives & growth.
Enable sales to activate Go to Market strategies.
Manage, develop, and mentor the regional marketing team.
Consolidate and report on Market Dynamics.
Execute regional marketing tactics that support the global CDG marketing plan.
Maintain alignment with product marketing & BMC teams to ensure consistent branding and messaging for regional programs.
Collaborate with regional sales leaders to ensure that local strategies harmonize with global CDG marketing plans.
Translate the Go-to-Market plan (GTM) and goals into practical quarterly targets, initiatives, and metrics for each region and business unit.
Report on GTM plan performance to Regional Marketing Leadership.
Collaborate with Regional Sales leaders to execute regional marketing programs and campaigns to support sales objectives.
Analyze the sales cycle, channel mix, and customer segmentation to optimize the selling process across different customer segments and personas.
Ensure successful lead/opportunity capture, nurture, and delivery to the sales teams.
Implement targeted campaigns tailored to specific in-region needs not covered by the global plan ensuring consistent branding and messaging, with the global team.
Set KPIs in alignment with the GTM plan.
Travel with marketing team in the region, to provide coaching and feedback on performance.
Complete performance reviews and other performance management responsibilities for the team.
Develop and maintain expertise on market analysis including VoC and VoS.
Synthesize, consolidate, and report on local market dynamics to Marketing and Sales leadership within the selling region.
Assist team to manage KOLs and other customer relationships as necessary.

What You Bring

Deep knowledge of local markets in the selling region.
Skilled in performance management.
A knowledge of project management best practices.
Proficiency in driving revenue through highly effective marketing and measuring results.
Skilled in building positive relationships with customers, partners, and internal teams, across multiple departments and functions.
Customer centric, creative, and visionary thinker.
Proficient problem solver and data driven decision maker.
Can work independently and is accountable to team performance.
Minimum: Bachelor's degree in life sciences, clinical sciences, or marketing. Preferred: MBA with a marketing focus.
10-12 years of proven work experience with a mix of marketing, strategic, technical, and business background.
A minimum of 2 years marketing experience including upstream and downstream activities
Experience working in a field marketing team is an advantage.
Sales Experience is an advantage.

Who We Are: For 70 years, Bio-Rad has focused on advancing the discovery process and transforming the fields of science and healthcare. As one of the top five life science companies, we are a global leader in developing, manufacturing, and marketing a broad range of high-quality research and clinical diagnostic products. We help people everywhere live longer, healthier lives. Recently voted a Best Place to Work, Bio-Rad offers a unique employee experience with collaborative teams that span the globe. Here, you are supported by leadership to build your career and are empowered to drive change that makes an impact you can see.

Benefits: Bio-Rad's biggest asset is its people, and the reason why our Total Rewards deliver programs that provide value, quality, and inclusivity while satisfying the diverse needs of our evolving workforce. Our robust offerings serve to enrich the overall health, wealth, and wellbeing of our employees through the various stages of an employee’s work and life cycle. We’re proud to offer a variety of options, including competitive insurance plans for you and your immediate family, Annual Health checkup , Marriage Leave, Paternity Leave ,Employee Assistance Programme , extensive learning and development opportunities, and more.

EEO Statement: Bio-Rad is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer, and we welcome candidates of all backgrounds. Veterans, people with disabilities, and people of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, and orientations are encouraged to apply.

Agency Non-Solicitation: Bio-Rad does not accept agency resumes, unless the agency has been authorized by a Bio-Rad Recruiting Representative. Please do not submit resumes unless authorized to do so. Bio-Rad will not pay for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Role: Product Marketing Manager

Location: Mumbai

WHAT YOU’LL DO

We’re MiQ, a global programmatic media partner for marketers and agencies. Our people are at the heart of everything we do, so you will be too. No matter the role or the location, we’re all united in the vision to lead the programmatic industry and make it better.

As a Product Marketing Manager in our India Commercial department, you’ll have the chance to: 

Delivery 
Identify valuable target audiences and refine target personas by product as needed
Scripting and periodically refreshing the MiQ sales narrative; determining product positioning and identify key market differentiators
Build compelling sales collateral and act as the main stakeholder for all sales materials delivered to the sales team
Accomplishing tasks on time and to a high quality, managing different deadlines and priorities
Project Management 
Facilitate and improve internal product communication process and ensure alignment across sales, product and marketing departments
Own the go-to-market product release calendar and ensure deliverables are executed in line with these timelines
Staying focused and contributing to projects, organizing stakeholders and multiple milestones
Internal Collaboration 
Strategize and partner with the marketing team to execute external marketing activations and campaigns including advertising, blog posts, social media, events/webinars, email campaigns, etc.
Collaborate with the global product marketing teams localize global product releases for India and manage delivery timelines
Working with internal stakeholders, building relationships and working across differences to find solutions
Communication 
Deliver expert training sessions, demos and webinars for both internal and external audiences 
Shape thought leadership linked to both MiQ product differentiators and industry developments
Crisp and clear communication to external partners/clients or internal stakeholders; flexing communication style to persuade others and influence positive outcomes 
Innovation 
Find new creative ways of storytelling and differentiating MiQ from competitors in the market
Develop a process for reporting on competitive industry intelligence
Acting with curiosity. Finds new and better ways of working, then applies or implements solutions to unarticulated needs, new or existing market requirements. Open to receiving and trying new ideas, processes or ways of working.
Who are your stakeholders? 

Your key stakeholders will be on the product and commercial teams. You will lead the product commercialization charge across our suite of programmatic solutions including (but not limited to): Advanced TV, Total Video and MiQ Sigma.

What You’ll Bring

Project Management

Highly organized and can demonstrate ability to plan and execute complex projects with multiple objectives and deliverables.
Proactive in following and improving team processes with attention to detail
Able to plan ahead and predict blockers in order to prevent them.

Analyzing

Ability to correctly interpret qualitative and quantitative data and extract key relevant themes.
Aptitude for building a deep understanding and empathy for internal and external audiences.
Proactively seeks to understand and is comfortable with the highly technical detail of MiQ’s products.

Product Storytelling:

Customer-centric, and leads with strategic segmentation 
Creates compelling product storytelling, positioning and messaging based on a clear understanding of MiQ’s market, products and clients needs.
Commercially minded, with an ability to build attractive product packaging models.

Collaboration:

Builds strong, positive stakeholder relationships to influence people and reach objectives.
Understands cultural, team, and individual differences in verbal and non-verbal communication and adapts approach to stakeholders accordingly.
Proactively open and transparent on work priorities and progress with all stakeholders.
Approaches change and the need for agility with a positive attitude.

Communication:

Can speak clearly to a given audience, demonstrating empathy with what’s important to them and achieving the objectives of the presentation.
Expert at making complex product information easy to understand.
Flawless command of the English language; written communication is always compelling, mistake free and in MiQ’s Tone of Voice.
Creates a range of suitable content outputs that showcase a detail-orientated approach, an eye for design, and are always within MiQ’s brand guidelines.

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

5+ years of experience in the programmatic/digital advertising space
You have an understanding of agency and marketer needs
You have experience in product marketing, especially within adtech
You have an intimate understanding of the sales process for a managed service media business, including selling to both media agencies and media teams at brands directly
Excellent persuasive communicator in both written and verbal forms, adept at crafting compelling arguments
Creative thinker who can relate industry challenges to MiQ solutions and explain complex problems in simplified (e.g. visual) forms
Proven ability to develop powerful external facing collateral 
Detail oriented, organized and willing to get hands-on with tasks when necessary to hit deadlines and solve problems
Knowledge of Hubspot a plus
A can-do attitude to provide energy, drive, and enthusiasm
A dream to grow your career as the company scales

We’ve highlighted some key skills, experience and requirements for this role. But please don’t worry if you don’t meet every single one. Our talent team strives to find the best people. They might see something in your background that’s a fit for this role, or another opportunity at MiQ.

If you have a passion for the role, please still apply.

What impact will you create? 

You will provide an important bridge between the global product marketing team, the Indian marketing team, and the product team, representing the marketing department in key cross-departmental meetings.

In this role, you will be reporting to the Product director to:

Define go-to-market product strategies for our full suite of products
Develop product narratives and positioning statements
Design product-specific collateral geared towards key audience segments (media agencies and brands). 

What’s in it for you?

At MIQ, our ambition has been to produce the best programmatic media results, underpinned by the sharpest data insights and delivered by the smartest people. And, since 2010, that’s exactly what we have been doing.

Our Centre of Excellence in Bengaluru is where the good stuff happens, where our data experts use all of their know-how to turn your business challenges into business-changing solutions.

Values 

Our values are so much more than statements. They unite MiQers in every corner of the world. They shape the way we work and the decisions we make. And they inspire us to stay true to ourselves and to aim for better. Our values are there to be embraced by everyone, so that we naturally live and breathe them. Just like inclusivity, our values flow through everything we do - no matter how big or small.

We do what we love - Passion
We figure it out - Determination
We anticipate the unexpected - Agility
We always unite - Unite
We dare to be unconventional - Courage

Benefits

Every region and office have specific perks and benefits, but every person joining MiQ can expect:

A hybrid work environment 
New hire orientation with job specific onboarding and training
Internal and global mobility opportunities
Competitive healthcare benefits
Bonus and performance incentives
Generous annual PTO paid parental leave, with two additional paid days to acknowledge holidays, cultural events, or inclusion initiatives.
Employee resource groups designed to connect people across all MiQ regions, drive action, and support our communities.

Apply today! 

Equal Opportunity Employer

Attesa Coffee is a start-up specialty green coffee sourcing and distribution company headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands. We work directly with producers at the origin to bring our customers (specialty coffee roasters) high-quality coffees safely, on time, and consistently with the use of efficient digital frameworks. We believe in traceability through the chain and are highly committed to transparency towards all our stakeholders.

We are looking for a Creative Marketing Lead to join our team. The member will work towards increasing the reach and impact of Attesa Coffee’s profile and visibility through the delivery of high value marketing, communication content and PR. The candidate will work with key stakeholders and will receive project support and on-going performance feedback from relevant team members.

Tasks

Assist in planning, writing, and managing weekly / monthly e-Newsletter and other specialty coffee related content (for e.g. email marketing, presentation material)

Design and create high-quality visual and written content using Canva and other graphic design applications for social media, newsletters, blogs, and the company website.

Assist with updating and maintaining Attesa Coffee’s social media presence, including scheduling Instagram and Facebook updates.

Assist with updating Attesa Coffee’s website when needed.

Assist in analysis of Attesa Coffee’s entry into new product and geographical markets via research and analysis.


Requirements

Degree in Business / Communications or Marketing related field.

1-2 yrs of experience with Market research, and website management

Working experience of 1-2 years with Canva and other graphic designing Apps for designing and content creation

Experience with Newsletters and Blogs.

Portfolio on Canva, Social Media Posts & Reels.

Proficient with Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)

Ability to work independently and as a member alongside other teams.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to communicate within the team.


Nice to have


Knowledge / interest in specialty coffee

Big ideas and persistent curiosity


Benefits

Work in an entrepreneurial and ownership driven environment.

Opportunity to grow within a young and dynamic international team.

Core knowledge and workings of the physical / digital B2B distribution industry.

Real life understanding of business development and organizational management

About Born West
Born West is a California-based creative technology agency building digital products, mobile and web apps, AI-powered tools, and platform systems for clients including L'Oréal, Oracle NetSuite, Superfeet and Pathstone. Our India subsidiary, Born West India, supports our global delivery work with a fully remote team of ~25 engineers, designers, and specialists across India.
‍
We are hiring our first dedicated People Operations Coordinator to own the ground-level coordination that keeps our India team running smoothly — hardware, onboarding, payroll coordination, compliance administration, and event logistics.
‍
Role Summary
This is a coordination and execution role, not a decision-making or strategic one. You will be the operational spine of Born West India — the person who makes sure new hires have their machines, payroll data gets to the CA on time, statutory compliance is maintained, and India-based events run without friction.
‍
You will report to the Chief of Staff in the US, work closely with the Country Directors in India on day-to-day matters, and serve as the primary point of contact for the India team on people and operations questions.
‍
You are not expected to set policy, make hiring or comp decisions, resolve interpersonal disputes, or represent the company in technical or client contexts. Policy, strategy, and final approvals flow from the founders and Chief of Staff. You execute.
‍
‍
ResponsibilitiesIT Asset and Hardware Operations
Procure hardware (MacBooks, iPhones, iPads, peripherals) for new hires, replacements, and special requests, based on approvals from Country Directors or Founders.
Coordinate shipping of hardware to employees' home addresses across India safely and on time.
Handle hardware issues end-to-end: arrange transport of faulty machines to Apple service centers, track repair status, and ship repaired machines back to employees.
Arrange temporary replacement machines where possible to minimize downtime.
Maintain accurate asset records in Zoho Creator IT Asset Tracker: assignments, serial numbers, warranty status, location, and condition.
Manage asset return and recovery during employee exits.
Onboarding and Offboarding
Execute new hire onboarding: collect employment documents, coordinate with the CA for payroll setup, issue equipment, provision Zoho People and other system accounts, and run orientation on policies.
Get offer letters, NDAs, and IP assignment agreements (drafted by leadership) executed and filed correctly.
Maintain employee records in Zoho People with full accuracy.
Coordinate exit formalities: final settlement preparation with the CA, hardware recovery, access revocation, issuance of experience letters and relieving documents (drafted by leadership).
Payroll and Statutory Compliance Coordination
Collect and validate monthly payroll inputs (attendance, leaves, expense claims, reimbursements) and submit to the CA on schedule.
Serve as the primary liaison between employees and the CA for payroll queries.
Track leave balances, compensatory offs, and holiday schedules in Zoho People per the Delhi Shops and Establishments Act policy (effective April 1, 2026).
Support PF, ESI, Professional Tax, and TDS filings executed by the CA: provide data, verify outputs, flag discrepancies.
Maintain a compliance calendar and ensure statutory deadlines are tracked and met.
POSH Act Compliance
Coordinate the operations of the Internal Committee (IC) constituted under the POSH Act, 2013: schedule meetings, maintain records and minutes, track complaint timelines, and prepare the annual report for filing with the District Officer.
Coordinate POSH awareness training for all employees and onboard new hires to the policy.
Liaise with the external POSH consultant (IC external member) on cases and training rollouts.
Note: This role does not chair the IC and does not investigate complaints. The Presiding Officer and IC members own that work.
Employee Support and Grievance Routing
Serve as an alternate intake channel for employee concerns that cannot be raised with their manager (manager conflicts, cross-team issues, compensation questions, general discomfort).
Log concerns confidentially and route to the Chief of Staff for handling. Do not attempt resolution independently.
Default grievance path remains: employee to manager. This role is an alternate channel, not a replacement.
POSH complaints go directly to the IC, never through this role informally.
Event and Travel Logistics
Plan and execute Born West India's annual in-person events: venue sourcing, hotel negotiation, conference room and A/V setup, meals, team travel and stays for out-of-city attendees.
Be on-ground at events for execution support.
Coordinate any future local co-working meetups as the program develops.
Arrange travel and accommodation for employees traveling for work on an ad-hoc basis.
Operational Admin and Finance Support
Raise POs and coordinate hardware and vendor payments with the Country Directors.
Manage virtual office correspondence, courier receipts, and any physical mail routing.
Maintain vendor relationships (hotels, travel agents, shipping providers, IT repair partners).
Track India-entity operational expenses and reconcile with monthly invoices to the US parent entity.
Reporting and Coordination
Weekly sync with the Chief of Staff: status updates, blockers, upcoming milestones, observations on team operations.
Escalate showstoppers same-day or next-business-day to the Chief of Staff.
Coordinate daily with Country Directors on matters affecting delivery teams.
Share observations on team engagement and concerns with the Chief of Staff as part of the weekly sync. This is informal and directional, not formal sentiment analysis.
‍
‍
What This Role Is Not
To set expectations clearly:
You are not an HR Business Partner or HR Generalist. You do not own policy, comp decisions, or grievance resolution.
You are not a decision-maker on hiring, terminations, hardware budgets, or vendor selection. You execute against decisions made by leadership.
You are not a client-facing or technical representative of the company at events or conferences.
You are not the IC Presiding Officer. You support IC operations.
You are not responsible for the emotional or personal wellbeing of employees. You route concerns to the right place.
‍
Required Qualifications
3–5 years of experience in HR Operations, People Ops, or Admin Ops roles, ideally at a startup or small-to-mid-sized company (20–200 employees).
Hands-on experience with Zoho People, Zoho Payroll, or comparable HRMS (BambooHR, Keka, GreytHR, etc.).
Working knowledge of Indian employment and statutory landscape: PF, ESI, PT, TDS, Shops and Establishments Act, POSH Act, gratuity basics.
Strong written and verbal English. You will be writing to founders and the Chief of Staff regularly.
Delhi NCR residence, with ability to travel within NCR when required and to other Indian cities 2–4 times per year for events.
Comfort with remote, async work. You will not have an office to go to or colleagues sitting next to you.
Execution discipline. Follow-up, documentation, and closing loops are the job. We need someone who finishes things without being reminded.
Discretion. You will handle confidential employee information, payroll data, and sensitive matters.
Bachelor's degree in HR, Business Administration, or a related field. MBA/PGDM in HR is a plus, not required.
Nice to Have
Experience supporting a US-based parent company or working across US-India time zones.
Experience with Apple device lifecycle management (MDM, repair coordination, procurement).
Event coordination experience (corporate offsites, team events, conferences).
Familiarity with Mercury, Wise, or other fintech payment tools.

Show more Show less

Role Overview
We are looking for a Product Marketing Manager to support the execution of key brand initiatives. The role involves managing day-to-day campaign execution, coordinating with internal stakeholders and agencies, and ensuring seamless delivery of brand and product communication across channels.

Key Responsibilities
Support end-to-end execution of brand campaigns across ATL/BTL, digital and partner platforms
Coordinate with creative, media, and production agencies to deliver campaign assets
Ensure consistency in brand messaging and adherence to brand guidelines
Work with product and business teams to support marketing for product launches and initiatives
Support execution of co-marketing initiatives with ecosystem partners
Manage timelines, approvals, and campaign workflows across stakeholders
Track campaign deliverables and support reporting of key marketing initiatives

What We’re Looking For
4–5 years of experience in marketing
Strong campaign execution and project management skills
Experience working with agencies and cross-functional teams
Ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously
Strong communication and organizational skills

Job Title: Group - Brand Manager/ Assistant Manager 
Location: Mumbai
Salary Range: TBD
Experience: Between 4 to 7 years (slightly flexible on this)

About you
We are looking for someone with a real eye for detail, a passion for exceptional food, for building brands and with loads of creativity. As is true for any brand role, a business acumen will be necessary along with range of experience across B2B and/or B2C marketing. As an in-house marketing professional, impeccable English skills are required. Strong knowledge of Digital Marketing and work experience with a creative agency and/or a food brand will be valued.
Key qualities
Self starter 
Impeccable project management 
Team player
Problem solver
Creative
Dedicated
Your Key Responsibilities:
Work with your team to set the Marketing Calendar and create plans. 
Plan, create and execute weekly/daily campaigns and promotions to drive sale
Manage the Marketing Budget to ensure we drive profitable growth
Oversee and deliver on all digital marketing content and assets. Manage social media accounts and create ways to generate higher website traffic, stronger brand awareness, and newer opportunities (LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram etc.)
Engage and Collaborate with Chefs, Influencers and Brands in innovative and exciting ways
Drive brand awareness and consideration through PR while working closely with a PR agency
Report on key brand metrics linked to brand objectives on a monthly basis
Design, conduct, and analyse consumer research and brand health tracking and help measure campaign performance to ensure high level of effectiveness
Study and analyse current trends in market and implement strategies to drive brand sales growth. Brainstorm new growth strategies

About Prasuma – building India’s favourite Food Brand!
Prasuma is committed to creating sensational food using the freshest & finest ingredients, cutting edge food technology and raising the quality of food available to consumers here in India. As the pioneer of the Fresh & Chilled Deli Meats segment in the region, Prasuma has earned a reputation over 35 years of product and service excellence among top modern retail outlets, restaurants and chefs nationwide. Prasuma also brought to Indian consumers their favourite Momo brand and then has been the first to launch Baos in India.
Show more Show less

About Media.net: 
Media.net is a leading global advertising technology company that helps power the open web by developing innovative products for web and app publishers as well as advertisers. Media.net has one of the most comprehensive portfolios of advertising technology in the industry across search, native, display, mobile, video, and more.
It provides end-to-end solutions for its large, diversified client base worldwide, and its platform and products are licensed by some of the largest publishers, ad networks, and other ad tech companies globally.

About the Role:
We are looking for analytical, creative minds that can conceptualize and execute on the synergistic union of technology, user experience, and monetization. These range from fledgling ideas that need nurturing to become functional and scalable, to multi-billion dollar brands looking to grow their user base, enhance user retention, or optimize their monetization.
Be it solving mundane problems with a utility focused approach or creating products that engage and excite a range of audiences, the role requires agile individuals who can develop and execute impactful GTM strategies.
The role requires you to collaborate with product, engineering, media, analytics, sales, account management, and compliance/legal to develop experiences and collaterals that engage partners and drive adoption.
You will build competition and market intelligence through research to make decisions on branding, positioning, prioritization of features/integrations and ensure successful execution around the growth and monetization of the products/projects assigned. You will work with internal stakeholders at all levels to support their products/offerings or take on projects geared towards achieving a partner’s specific goals. It would be crucial for you to track product KPIs and take data-driven decisions for product enhancements that impact business.

What You Will Do: 
Go-to-market strategy
Target market and competitive research
Product positioning & feature conceptualization
Managing product launches
Content & messaging strategy
User segmentation & identifying targeted marketing strategies
Experimentation 
Defining KPIs and goals
Measuring progress & assessing the impact of products
Monitor key customer engagement & satisfaction indicators
Product Adoption & Growth 
Onboarding customers
User engagement strategy execution
Iterative testing and product improvisation
Consumer journey optimization
Re-engagement strategies
Identify and apply cross-pollination opportunities
Team Performance Management 
Take ownership of teams to drive required efforts
Monitor performance and plan goal setting for the future
Define processes roles to be followed for task execution
Mentor existing reportees & train new joinees
Monetization 
Drive monetization strategy balanced by a customer centric approach
Identify new revenue sources and optimize existing ones
Maximize user LTV using a combination of best working and new market practices
Analyze ROI from products and manage changes required for maximizing it

What We Look for in a Candidate: 
You have 2 + years of relevant experience in marketing/product/online customer success in a technology company or startup
You have the ability to collaborate and build relationships and influence decisions at multiple levels of an organization
You thrive in a robust environment and align available resources to a common goal
You have a passion for technology, and a keen eye to identify opportunity in an ever changing landscape
You have strong analytical skills geared towards iterative solution of user problems based with data
You are open to travel to our global offices and industry shows to connect with peers, vendors and partners
You are proactive in your approach to problem solving and navigating ambiguity and complexity
You have a customer- first approach with an innate curiosity to understand improvise on user needs
You think quick on your feet and can derive actionable insights based on information available
You are an expert communicator who can lead and manage relationships across organizational boundaries and vendor relationships

Develop and implement comprehensive marketing strategies to promote forging products and services for exotic and thermal sector.

Collaborate with the sales team to create and execute marketing campaigns that drive sales and customer engagement.
Create compelling marketing materials, including brochures, presentations, and case studies.
Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers, industry partners, and stakeholders.
Monitor and analyze marketing performance metrics to measure the effectiveness of strategies.
Stay up-to-date with industry developments and competitor activities to ensure a competitive edge.

Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

Role Overview
We are looking for a dynamic and result-oriented professional to drive brand building and digital growth initiatives. This role will own end-to-end brand strategy, social media presence, and performance marketing (Google & Meta Ads) to enhance visibility, engagement, and business outcomes.

Key Responsibilities
1. Brand Strategy & Positioning
Develop and execute brand strategy aligned with business goals
Ensure consistent brand communication across all digital channels
Plan and execute brand campaigns, launches, and activations
Monitor competitor activity and market trends to refine positioning

2. Social Media & Content
Manage and grow presence across Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, YouTube, etc.
Create and execute content calendars (posts, reels, campaigns)
Drive engagement, reach, and follower growth
Collaborate with design and content teams for creatives
Handle community management (comments, DMs, queries)

3. Performance Marketing (Paid Campaigns)
Plan, execute, and optimize Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube)
Manage Meta Ads (Facebook & Instagram) campaigns
Monitor key metrics like CAC, ROI, CTR, conversion rates
Conduct A/B testing, audience targeting, and campaign optimization
Manage budgets and ensure efficient spend allocation

4. Analytics & Growth
Track and analyze performance using tools like Google Analytics, Ads Manager
Generate insights to improve campaign performance and user acquisition
Drive lead generation, conversions, and revenue growth
Prepare and present performance reports and dashboards

5. Collaboration & Execution
Work closely with product, sales, and design teams
Coordinate with agencies, vendors, and external partners
Ensure timely execution of campaigns and initiatives

Qualifications & Skills
Must Have
3–6 years of experience in digital marketing / brand management
Hands-on experience with Google Ads and Meta Ads
Strong understanding of social media platforms and digital campaigns
Experience in campaign planning and execution
Analytical mindset with ability to interpret data and optimize performance
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

Good to Have
Experience in B2C / consumer internet / startup environment
Exposure to SEO, influencer marketing, or marketplaces
Basic understanding of design tools (Canva, Figma, etc.)
